whatIs.title: What is the Time Converter?
whatIs.description: Convert time zones in real-time across major cities worldwide, and convert between Unix timestamps and dates. Supports 12+ major time zones including KST, UTC, EST/PST, CET, and JST.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Select source and target time zones, then enter date and time to convert.
In the Unix Timestamp tab, enter a number to convert to KST/UTC/ISO 8601 format.
The Relative Time tab shows how long ago or until a given time.
Click copy to save converted times to clipboard.

tips.title: Tips
items: New York (EST) is 14 hours behind Korea (13 during DST).
Unix timestamps count seconds since Jan 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC.
DST regions change UTC offsets seasonally - verify when converting US/EU times.
Use this to convert server log UTC timestamps to local time.

items.q: What's the time difference between Korea and the US?
items.a: Eastern US (New York) is 14 hours behind Korea, Western US (LA) is 17 hours behind. During DST (Mar-Nov), it's 13 and 16 hours respectively.

items.q: What is a Unix timestamp?
items.a: A Unix timestamp (Epoch time) is the number of seconds elapsed since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC. It's timezone-independent.

items.q: What is DST?
items.a: Daylight Saving Time advances clocks by 1 hour in summer. The US observes it from March to November. Korea, Japan, and China do not use DST.

whatIs.title: What is the Salary Take-Home Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Salary Take-Home Calculator shows you exactly how much you'll actually receive each month after deducting the four major insurances (National Pension, Health Insurance, Employment Insurance, Long-term Care Insurance), income tax, and local income tax from your gross annual salary. It's especially useful for job seekers, professionals negotiating a job offer, or anyone comparing compensation levels. The calculator uses the latest 2025 rates for accurate results.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your gross annual salary (total yearly pay before deductions).
2. Enter the number of dependents (including yourself) and the number of children under 20.
3. If you receive non-taxable allowances (meal allowance, car maintenance, etc.), enter the monthly amount.
4. Click Calculate to see your monthly take-home pay with a full breakdown of each deduction.

tips.title: Tips
items: Meal allowance (up to ₩200,000/month) and car maintenance allowance (up to ₩200,000/month) are non-taxable — be sure to enter these if they apply. They can increase your take-home pay by tens of thousands of won.
When negotiating a salary, compare take-home amounts rather than gross figures to get a true picture of your compensation.
Income tax deductions vary by number of dependents, so recalculate whenever your family situation changes.

items.q: What rates does the calculator use?
items.a: It applies the 2025 rates: National Pension 4.5%, Health Insurance 3.545%, Long-term Care Insurance 12.95% of health insurance premium, and Employment Insurance 0.9%.

items.q: What are common non-taxable allowances?
items.a: The most common non-taxable items are the meal allowance (up to ₩200,000/month), self-driving subsidy (up to ₩200,000/month), and childbirth/childcare allowance (up to ₩200,000/month).

items.q: Why might the result differ from my actual paycheck?
items.a: Each company handles fringe benefits and additional deductions differently, which can cause minor discrepancies. For the exact figure, refer to your pay stub.

points.0: Work income deduction: Progressive deduction applied according to total salary
points.1: 4 major insurances: Health insurance 3.545%, National pension 4.5% (cap 80.52 million KRW), Employment insurance 0.9%
points.2: Long-term care insurance: 12.27% of health insurance premium
points.4: Work income tax credit and child tax credit reflected
points.5: Refund possible with additional deductions during year-end tax settlement

loan.title: Loan Calculator
loan.description: Calculate monthly payments for various loan types including equal installment, equal principal, and bullet payment loans.

whatIs.title: What is the Loan Calculator?
whatIs.description: A precision loan calculator that uses the same formulas as Korean banks to compute monthly payments, total interest, and full repayment schedules for four repayment methods: equal principal-and-interest, equal principal, lump-sum at maturity, and deferred repayment.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the loan principal, annual interest rate, and loan term.
2. Choose a repayment method: equal principal-and-interest, equal principal, lump-sum, or deferred.
3. Click 'Calculate' to see the monthly payment, total repayment, and total interest.
4. Check the monthly repayment schedule and save multiple scenarios to compare.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Even a 0.1% rate difference adds up to roughly ₩3.88M extra interest on a ₩300M 30-year loan.
Equal principal repayment minimizes total interest but starts with higher monthly payments.
Maintaining a good credit score lowers your spread rate and improves loan terms.
In falling rate environments, choose variable rates; in rising ones, lock in a fixed rate.
If making early repayments, do so early in the loan term when interest savings are greatest.

items.q: Which is better: equal installment or equal principal?
items.a: Equal principal results in lower total interest but higher initial payments. Choose it if you have sufficient upfront cash. For predictable monthly expenses, equal installment is more convenient.

items.q: What is a deferred (grace period) loan?
items.a: During the grace period you pay only interest; principal repayment begins afterward. It reduces early pressure but results in higher total interest.

items.q: How do LTV and DTI affect a loan?
items.a: LTV caps the loan relative to collateral value, DTI limits annual payments as a percentage of income. Lower ratios increase borrowing limits and help secure better rates.

items.q: When is the early repayment fee waived?
items.a: Most banks waive early repayment fees after 3 years from origination. Government-backed loans may differ.

savings.title: Savings Calculator
savings.description: Calculate interest and maturity amount for various savings products.

whatIs.title: What is the Savings Calculator?
whatIs.description: A savings calculator that accurately handles four types of Korean savings accounts: regular, flexible, goal-based, and compound interest. Using the same calculation method as actual bank products, you can instantly see the maturity amount, monthly interest, and after-tax payout.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select a savings type: Regular, Flexible, Goal-based, or Compound.
2. Enter the monthly deposit (or target amount), term, and annual interest rate.
3. Click Calculate to see the maturity amount, total deposits, total interest, and after-tax payout.
4. Check the monthly schedule tab to view how your balance grows each month.

tips.title: Savings Tips
items: Compare products based on after-tax returns (15.4% interest income tax).
Internet banks often offer base rates 0.5–1%p higher than traditional banks.
Check preferential rate conditions carefully (payroll deposit, card usage, auto-transfer).
Set monthly deposits within 30% of your income to reduce early termination risk.
For long-term goals (5+ years), compound savings offer better returns.
Splitting deposits into short-, mid-, and long-term accounts maintains liquidity while pursuing higher returns.

items.q: Which is better: regular or flexible savings?
items.a: Salaried workers benefit from regular savings with higher rates. Freelancers with irregular income are better suited for flexible savings.

items.q: How much interest for early withdrawal?
items.a: Early termination applies a much lower rate (typically 30–50% of the base rate). Try to keep accounts until maturity.

items.q: Compound vs simple interest savings?
items.a: Simple interest applies only to principal. Compound interest adds interest to principal each period. The longer the term, the greater the compound advantage.

items.q: How to combine housing subscription with regular savings?
items.a: Open a housing subscription account first for lottery eligibility, then use remaining capacity for high-rate regular savings.

retirement.title: Retirement Pay Calculator
retirement.description: Calculate retirement pay and retirement income tax based on Korean labor law.

whatIs.title: What is the Severance Pay Calculator?
whatIs.description: A calculator that accurately computes severance pay, retirement income tax, and net take-home amount per Korean Labor Standards Act. It uses the 3-month average wage and years of service to compute statutory severance, then applies the retirement income deduction and annual-division method.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your employment start date and retirement date (or years of service).
2. Enter your average monthly wage for the 3 months preceding retirement.
3. Click 'Calculate' — severance pay, tax, and net amount are computed automatically.
4. Review the step-by-step deduction breakdown on the results screen.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Severance = daily average wage × 30 × years of service. Under 1 year is prorated monthly.
Receiving severance as an annuity grants an additional 30% deduction on retirement income tax.
Employees with 10+ years can use mid-career settlement to reduce the overall tax burden.
Transferring to an IRP keeps investment earnings tax-free with greater tax benefits after age 55.
With DC-type pensions, 1/12 of annual salary is deposited yearly — benefits grow with pay raises.

items.q: How long must I work to get severance?
items.a: Employers must pay severance to employees who have worked continuously for at least 1 year averaging 15+ hours per week.

items.q: What is the annual-division method?
items.a: The taxable base is divided by 12, tax rate applied, then multiplied by 12. This prevents disproportionately high tax on lump-sum payments.

items.q: Severance pay vs DB/DC pensions?
items.a: Traditional severance is funded by the employer directly. DB pensions are managed by financial institutions. DC pensions deposit 1/12 of annual salary into an employee-controlled account.

items.q: How much retirement income tax should I expect?
items.a: Tax is calculated by deducting the retirement income deduction then applying the annual-division method. Longer tenure means higher deductions and lower effective rates.

tax.title: Tax Calculator
tax.description: Calculate income tax, VAT, and capital gains tax based on Korean tax law.

whatIs.title: What is the Tax Calculator?
whatIs.description: An all-in-one Korean tax calculator for income tax, VAT, and capital gains tax. It applies the latest NTS rates and deductions so you can accurately estimate your tax liability and identify tax-saving opportunities.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the type of tax: income tax, VAT, or capital gains tax.
2. Enter your income amount and applicable deductions.
3. Click 'Calculate' to see the tax amount, local income tax, and effective tax rate.
4. Review deduction breakdowns and tax-saving tips to plan your strategy.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Income tax uses progressive rates (6%–45%), so maximize deductions like personal exemptions and pension contributions.
Contributing ₩6M to pension savings and ₩3M to IRP yields up to ₩1.65M in tax credits annually.
Business owners should manage tax invoices carefully to utilize input VAT credits.
A single-household homeowner holding a property for 2+ years may be exempt from capital gains tax.
For complex tax situations, consult a certified tax accountant for legal optimization.

items.q: How are income tax rates determined?
items.a: Korea uses progressive rates from 6% to 45%: under ₩14M at 6%, ₩14M–50M at 15%, ₩50M–88M at 24%, above ₩88M from 35% to 45%.

items.q: General vs simplified VAT taxpayers?
items.a: Businesses over ₩48M annual revenue are general taxpayers (10% VAT). Below are simplified taxpayers (1–4% by industry) with limited input VAT credit.

items.q: Capital gains tax exemption conditions?
items.a: A single-household owner holding (and in designated areas, residing in) a home for 2+ years qualifies for exemption. Luxury homes (over ₩1.2B) are taxed on the excess.

items.q: Do I need to file local income tax separately?
items.a: No. Local income tax (10% of income tax) is automatically calculated and collected with your income tax return.

exchange.title: Exchange Rate Calculator
exchange.description: Convert currencies with real-time exchange rates.

realEstate.title: Real Estate Calculator
realEstate.description: Calculate real estate investment returns and acquisition tax.

whatIs.title: What is the Real Estate Calculator?
whatIs.description: A comprehensive real estate calculator handling jeonse loans, mortgage loans, and acquisition tax in one tool. Based on 2025 tax laws with LTV, DTI, and DSR regulations reflected for realistic financing plans.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the calculation type: Jeonse Loan, Mortgage Loan, or Acquisition Tax.
2. Jeonse Loan: Enter the deposit amount, loan amount, interest rate, and term.
3. Mortgage Loan: Enter property price, down payment, interest rate, and repayment method.
4. Acquisition Tax: Select purchase price, property type, and special conditions for automatic tax calculation.
5. Results are saved in the URL for sharing or future reference.

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips
items: First-time home buyers can receive a 50% acquisition tax reduction (up to ₩300M).
Newlyweds (within 7 years of marriage) get 50% tax reduction on up to ₩100M in acquisition tax.
LTV limits vary by region: 70% in regulated areas, 80% in non-regulated areas.
Under DSR 40% rule, total annual loan payments cannot exceed 40% of annual income.
Calculate total costs including interest, tax, and agent fees before committing.

items.q: Jeonse loan vs mortgage?
items.a: Jeonse loan finances the key money deposit (2-year term). Mortgage is for purchasing a home (up to 30–40 years).

items.q: When must acquisition tax be paid?
items.a: Within 60 days of the acquisition date. Late payment incurs penalties.

items.q: Heavy tax rules for multi-homeowners?
items.a: Those with 2+ homes in regulated areas purchasing additional property may face 8–12% heavy taxation.

items.q: Equal installment vs equal principal?
items.a: Equal installment offers predictable payments. Equal principal has higher initial payments but lower total interest.

whatIs.title: What is JSON Formatter Pro?
whatIs.description: JSON Formatter Pro is a professional developer tool for validating, formatting, minifying, and analyzing JSON data. Built on the CodeMirror 6 editor, it offers syntax highlighting and error line indicators, along with JSONPath queries, JSON5/JSONC parsing, and automatic repair of broken JSON via jsonrepair. Use it for analyzing API responses, editing config files, validating data structures, and more.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Paste your JSON data into the editor or drag and drop a file.
2. Choose a mode from the top tabs: Format (pretty-print), Minify (compact), Tree (hierarchy view), or Stats.
3. Use the JSONPath query field to extract specific data — for example, `$.store.book[*].title`.
4. For broken JSON, click the Auto-fix button and jsonrepair will attempt to restore it.

items.q: Can it parse JSON5 and JSONC?
items.a: Yes. It supports JSON5 (comments, trailing commas, single quotes, etc.) and JSONC (JSON with comments).

items.q: How do I use JSONPath queries?
items.a: It supports the JSONPath standard: `$` for root, `.key` for child access, `[*]` for all array elements, and filter expressions like `[?(@.age>18)]`.

items.q: Can I load JSON directly from a URL?
items.a: Yes — use the URL import feature to fetch JSON from an external API endpoint. If CORS restrictions apply, the request is routed through a CORS proxy.

whatIs.title: What is the JSON ↔ CSV Converter?
whatIs.description: A two-way converter for JSON and CSV formats aimed at developers. Use it to turn a REST API's JSON response into a spreadsheet-ready CSV, or convert CSV data exported from Excel into a JSON array. It handles nested JSON objects, custom delimiters, header inclusion, and other options you need in real-world workflows.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the conversion direction: JSON → CSV or CSV → JSON.
2. Paste your data or upload a file.
3. Configure options such as delimiter (comma, tab, semicolon) and whether to include a header row.
4. Copy the result or download it as a file.

items.q: How are nested JSON objects handled?
items.a: Nested objects can be flattened using dot notation (e.g., `address.city`) or serialized as a JSON string — your choice.

items.q: Does it handle non-Latin characters correctly?
items.a: UTF-8 encoding is used by default, so Korean and other Unicode characters are handled correctly. Enable the BOM option if you plan to open the CSV in Excel.

items.q: Can it handle large files?
items.a: Files of several megabytes to tens of megabytes can be processed within browser memory limits. For very large files, chunked processing is recommended.

sqlFormatter.title: SQL Formatter
sqlFormatter.description: Format and beautify SQL queries with syntax highlighting.

whatIs.title: What is the SQL Formatter?
whatIs.description: A tool that instantly transforms messy SQL queries into clean, structured code with automatic indentation, keyword normalization, and sub-query structuring. Supports MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, and Oracle.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Paste the SQL query into the input panel.
2. Click 'Format' to apply automatic indentation and keyword alignment.
3. Use 'Minify' to produce a single-line optimized SQL string.
4. Copy the result or save to history for future reference.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Format SQL before submitting PRs — reviewers understand logic much faster.
Paste ORM-generated queries to quickly spot performance bottlenecks.
Large INSERT statements become easy to validate once VALUES rows are aligned.
Consistent keyword casing helps the team maintain SQL coding standards.
CTE and recursive queries are indented level by level, making debugging far easier.

items.q: Which database dialects are supported?
items.a: MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server (T-SQL), Oracle (PL/SQL), and ANSI SQL standard. DB-specific syntax is recognized correctly.

items.q: Can it format nested subqueries?
items.a: Yes. IN, EXISTS subqueries and nested queries are indented by level. CTE and recursive queries are also structured.

items.q: What does minify do?
items.a: Removes unnecessary whitespace, line breaks, and comments to produce a compact single-line SQL string.

items.q: Window functions supported?
items.a: Yes. ROW_NUMBER(), RANK(), and GROUP BY ROLLUP, CUBE, GROUPING SETS are all formatted correctly.

Adjust the quality slider and select output format.
Click resize, preview the result, and download.

tips.title: Tips
items: Enable aspect ratio lock to auto-calculate dimensions from one value.
WebP format is 25-35% smaller than JPEG at the same quality.
Use social media presets to prevent images from being cropped on platforms.

items.q: Does resizing reduce image quality?
items.a: Enlarging beyond original size will reduce quality. When shrinking, keep quality at 80%+ for minimal visible degradation.

items.q: JPEG, PNG, or WebP - which is best?
items.a: JPEG for photos (small size), PNG for transparency needs, WebP for web optimization.

items.q: What's the maximum upload size?
items.a: Processing is done in-browser with no server limits, but images over 50MB may take longer to process.

imageEditor.title: Image Editor
imageEditor.description: Edit images online with filters, cropping, and effects.

whatIs.title: What is this tool?
whatIs.description: The Image Editor lets you crop, rotate, add text, and apply filters (brightness, contrast, saturation, blur, sepia) to photos directly in your browser. Includes undo/redo for safe editing.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Drag and drop or select an image to upload.
Choose crop, text, filter, or rotate from the tool tabs.
For crop, drag to select an area. For text, type and position on the image.
Download the edited result when finished.

tips.title: Tips
items: Adjusting brightness and contrast together effectively corrects backlit or dark photos.
When adding text, set font size, color, and style before positioning on the image.
Use the Undo button freely to experiment without worry.

items.q: Is original image quality preserved?
items.a: Yes, processing uses Canvas API at original resolution. JPEG compression may cause slight quality differences on save.

items.q: Can I add non-English text?
items.a: Yes, all Unicode text including Korean, Japanese, Chinese, and emoji is supported.

items.q: What if I close the browser during editing?
items.a: Edits are not saved. Always download your result before closing.

whatIs.title: What is the Character Counter?
whatIs.description: The Character Counter analyzes text in real time, showing character count, word count, sentence count, line count, and whitespace count. It's particularly useful when writing content with length limits — blog posts, social media captions, cover letters, academic abstracts, and more. You can toggle between counting with or without spaces to match whatever standard applies.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Paste or type your text into the input area.
2. Character count (with/without spaces), word count, sentence count, and line count are displayed in real time.
3. Set a maximum character target to track your progress visually.
4. Use the Clear button to reset and start analyzing a new piece of text.

tips.title: Tips
items: Naver Blog typically counts characters including spaces, while academic papers often use character counts excluding spaces — check the submission guidelines of wherever you're submitting.
Platform character limits: X (Twitter) 280, Instagram caption 2,200, KakaoStory 600.
For cover letters, paste each section separately to manage the character count for each part individually.

items.q: How are emojis counted?
items.a: Emojis can count as 1 or 2 characters depending on their Unicode code point. Some platforms count emojis as 2 characters, so leave some buffer when writing near the limit.

items.q: How is the word count calculated?
items.a: Words are delimited by spaces or punctuation. For Korean, spacing units are used; for English, spaces are used as separators.

items.q: Can I load text from a file?
items.a: The current version supports paste-in input. You can open your file, select all with Ctrl+A, and paste it in.

uuidGenerator.title: UUID Generator
uuidGenerator.description: Professional UUID generator for developers. Supports v1, v4, v7, and Nil UUIDs with bulk generation and multiple output formats.

v1.name: Timestamp-based
v1.description: UUID generated based on timestamp and MAC address.
v1.useCase: Database records where time ordering is important

v4.name: Random-based
v4.description: Most common UUID generated with completely random values.

v7.name: Sortable
v7.description: Sortable UUID combining timestamp and random values.
v7.useCase: Modern applications where database performance matters

nil.name: Nil UUID
nil.description: Special UUID with all bits set to zero.

whatIs.title: What is the UUID Generator?
whatIs.description: A UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) is a 128-bit identifier guaranteed to be globally unique, widely used as database primary keys, API request tracking IDs, filenames, and more. This tool generates v1 (time-based), v4 (random), v7 (sortable time-based), and Nil UUIDs securely in the browser, with support for bulk generation and various output formats (with/without hyphens, uppercase, etc.).

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the UUID version: v1, v4, v7, or Nil.
2. Set how many UUIDs to generate (1 to 1,000).
3. Choose the output format (with hyphens, uppercase, with braces, etc.).
4. Click Generate, then copy the results or download as a file.

tips.title: Tips
items: For modern systems, prefer v7 over v4. Since v7 embeds a timestamp, it sorts chronologically and performs much better as a database index.
Storing UUIDs as 32-character strings without hyphens saves storage space when using them as database primary keys.
Use the bulk generation feature to produce hundreds of UUIDs at once for test data in SQL INSERT statements.

items.q: What is the difference between v4 and v7?
items.a: v4 is fully random with an extremely low collision probability. v7 embeds a millisecond-precision timestamp at the front, enabling chronological sorting and better database index efficiency.

items.q: Are generated UUIDs stored on a server?
items.a: No. All UUIDs are generated entirely in your browser and never sent to any server. The process is completely client-side.

items.q: Is there any risk of UUID collisions?
items.a: For v4 UUIDs, the probability of a collision is approximately 5.3×10⁻³⁶ — effectively zero for all practical purposes.

uuidGenerator.placeholder: Select a UUID version and click generate

cronTester.title: Cron Expression Tester
cronTester.descriptionText: Test and analyze Cron expressions in real-time. Predict next execution times, get Korean descriptions, and use common patterns for easy schedule management.

validation.invalidFormat: Cron expression must have 5 fields (minute hour day month weekday)

validation.monthEndWarning: Month-end dates (29-31) may not exist in all months

result.noExecutions: Enter a valid Cron expression to see execution schedule

whatIs.title: What is the Cron Expression Tester?
whatIs.description: The Cron Expression Tester lets you validate and interpret cron schedule expressions used in Linux/Unix crontabs, AWS EventBridge, GitHub Actions, and similar systems — all in real time. It explains what a given expression means in plain language and shows a list of upcoming execution times, helping you catch scheduling mistakes before they happen.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter a cron expression in the input field (e.g., `0 9 * * 1-5`).
2. The tool immediately explains the schedule in plain English.
3. Review the list of upcoming execution times to verify the schedule is correct.
4. Select from preset buttons for common patterns.

tips.title: Tips
items: When using day-of-week ranges like `0 9 * * 1-5`, confirm whether your system uses 0–6 (Sun–Sat) or 1–7 (Mon–Sun) notation.
AWS CloudWatch Events / EventBridge supports an optional sixth field for the year.
Step intervals using `/` (e.g., `*/5 * * * *` for every 5 minutes) have varying support across systems — always test before deploying.

items.q: What is the difference between standard cron and Quartz cron?
items.a: Standard cron uses 5 fields (minute hour day month weekday). Quartz cron uses 6–7 fields (second minute hour day month weekday [year]). This tool is based on the standard 5-field format.

items.q: Are special characters like `L` and `W` supported?
items.a: L (last) and W (weekday) are Quartz-specific characters not supported by standard crontab. Check your runtime environment before using them.

items.q: The expression is correct but the times look off — what's wrong?
items.a: Cron runs on the server's system time. Make sure your server's timezone is set to the one you expect (e.g., Asia/Seoul for KST).

cronTester.placeholder: Enter a Cron expression to see execution schedule and description

jwtDecoder.description: Safely decode and analyze JWT tokens. Display Header, Payload, and Signature separately and validate token integrity.

security.title: 🔒 Security Notice
security.description: This tool runs locally in your browser and tokens are not sent to any server. However, please handle production tokens with care.

about.description: Understand the structure and features of JSON Web Tokens

whatIs.title: What is the JWT Decoder?
whatIs.description: The JWT Decoder is a developer tool that Base64-decodes the three parts of a JWT (JSON Web Token) — Header, Payload, and Signature — and displays their contents for analysis. It's useful for debugging API authentication issues, checking token expiration, and inspecting claims. All processing happens in the browser, so you can safely analyze tokens without them ever leaving your device.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Paste your JWT token (without the Bearer prefix) into the input field.
2. The token is split into Header (algorithm, token type), Payload (claims data), and Signature.
3. Time-related claims like exp (expiration) and iat (issued at) are displayed as human-readable dates.
4. The tool automatically checks whether the token is still valid based on the current time.

tips.title: Tips
items: Production JWT tokens may contain personal or sensitive data. Never paste them into an untrusted online decoder. This tool processes everything locally in your browser with no server transmission.
JWT Payloads are only signed, not encrypted. Avoid storing sensitive information in the Payload.
Monitor token expiration (exp) regularly so you can design appropriate refresh cycles.

items.q: Can you verify the Signature?
items.a: Signature verification requires the server's secret key. Since this is a client-side tool, it doesn't accept secret keys for security reasons — it only provides structural analysis of the Header and Payload.

items.q: Can asymmetric algorithms like RS256 be decoded?
items.a: Yes — the Base64 decoding approach is the same regardless of algorithm. Header and Payload contents are accessible for any JWT algorithm.

items.q: Why does the tool say the token is invalid?
items.a: If the exp (expiration) timestamp is earlier than the current time, the token is shown as expired. If the token has a formatting error, a parsing failure message is displayed.

A 20-30% down payment improves approval rates and reduces interest burden.

items.q: What's the typical car loan interest rate?
items.a: As of 2025, finance companies charge 5-9% annually, while bank auto loans range from 3.5-6%.

items.q: Loan vs lease - which is better?
items.a: Loans give you ownership after payoff, better for long-term keeping. Leases have lower upfront costs, ideal for 3-5 year vehicle cycling.

items.q: Can I get a car loan with no down payment?
items.a: Some finance companies offer 100% financing, but rates are higher and approval criteria stricter.

whatIs.title: What is the Car Tax Calculator?
whatIs.description: This tool accurately calculates all taxes incurred when purchasing a vehicle, including acquisition tax, registration tax, bond purchase, and stamp duty by vehicle type, purpose, and region. It automatically applies eco-vehicle discounts and multi-child family benefits.

howToUse.title: How to Use

usageTips.title: Tips
items: Compact cars (1,000cc or less) qualify for acquisition tax discounts.
EVs get up to 1.4M KRW acquisition tax reduction as of 2025.
Multi-child families (3+ children under 18) may qualify for tax exemption.
Used car taxes may be based on standard assessed value rather than actual price.

items.q: How much is car registration tax?
items.a: For passenger cars, acquisition tax is 7% (personal use) or 4% (commercial). Compact cars get a reduced rate of 4% with additional discounts.

items.q: What are EV tax benefits?

items.q: What is bond purchase?
items.a: A mandatory bond purchase when registering a vehicle. The rate varies by vehicle price, displacement, and region.

whatIs.title: What is Bogeumjari Loan?
whatIs.description: Bogeumjari Loan is a government-backed fixed-rate mortgage provided by Korea Housing Finance Corporation (HF). It offers more stable rates than variable-rate bank loans, with preferential terms for first-time buyers, newlyweds, and multi-child families.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Select loan type (general/first-time/newlywed/multi-child).
Enter property price and combined household annual income.
Choose loan term (10-50 years).
View eligibility, estimated loan limit, and monthly payment.

tips.title: Tips
items: First-time buyers get LTV up to 80% vs 70% for general type.
Newlywed type offers 0.3%p rate discount. Check if you married within 7 years.
Online-only (Akkim-e) version offers 0.1-0.2%p additional rate discount.

items.q: What are the eligibility requirements?
items.a: Combined household income under 70M KRW, property under 600M KRW, and homeownership restrictions apply.

items.q: What's the interest rate?
items.a: As of 2026, Akkim-e Bogeumjari rates are approximately 4.05-4.35% annually. Type-specific discounts apply additionally.

items.q: How does it differ from Didimddol Loan?
items.a: Didimddol has stricter income requirements but lower rates (2-3%). Bogeumjari has broader eligibility and higher loan limits. Compare both for your situation.

medianIncome.title: Median Income Reference
medianIncome.description: Check standard median income by household size and government welfare program eligibility thresholds

whatIs.title: What is Median Income?
whatIs.description: The standard median income is the midpoint of national household income announced annually by the Ministry of Health and Welfare. It serves as the qualification threshold for various government welfare programs including basic livelihood benefits and housing support.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Select the year (2023-2026) to view median income data.
Review median income by household size (1-6 persons).
Check amounts at various percentage thresholds (32%-100%).
See which government welfare programs each threshold qualifies for.

tips.title: Tips
items: Livelihood benefits: 32%, medical: 40%, housing: 48%, education: 50% of median income.
Median income uses pre-tax income. The 'recognized income' calculation differs from simple salary.
Median income increases yearly - check again even if you didn't qualify last year.

items.q: What is the 2026 median income?
items.a: For 1-person households approximately 2.56M KRW/month, 4-person households approximately 6.49M KRW/month. About 6.4% increase from the previous year.

items.q: How is recognized income calculated?
items.a: Recognized income = Income assessment + Asset-to-income conversion. Use the Bokjiro (bokjiro.go.kr) simulation tool for precise calculation.

items.q: What benefits are available at 100% median income?
items.a: National Employment Support System, youth rent subsidy, and child tax credit may apply. Each program has additional requirements beyond income.

bmi.description: Calculate your Body Mass Index (BMI) to start healthy weight management

meaning.1: Used as a reference indicator for overall health status
meaning.2: Has limitations as it doesn't reflect muscle mass, bone density, etc.
meaning.3: Observe weight changes through regular measurements

management.0: Balanced diet and regular exercise are important

management.2: Consult with professionals considering individual differences

whatIs.title: What is the BMI Calculator?
whatIs.description: The BMI (Body Mass Index) Calculator uses your height and weight to express your degree of body fat as a number. It classifies your result as underweight, normal, overweight, or obese (Stages 1–3) according to WHO and Korean Society for the Study of Obesity (KSSO) guidelines, and also shows your healthy weight range. Use it for regular weight management and health monitoring.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your height (cm) and weight (kg).
2. Your BMI score and weight classification (underweight / normal / overweight / obese) are displayed.
3. See the healthy weight range and how far you are from your goal weight.
4. Recommended BMI ranges by age and gender are also provided for reference.

tips.title: Tips
items: BMI doesn't account for muscle mass, so athletes and muscular individuals may be classified as overweight. Use it alongside the Body Fat Calculator for a fuller picture.
Asian populations face higher metabolic risk at the same BMI as Westerners. The KSSO defines 23–24.9 as overweight and 25+ as obese for Koreans.
Measuring waist circumference alongside BMI gives a more accurate health risk assessment. Abdominal obesity is defined as waist > 85 cm for men and > 80 cm for women.

items.q: What is the normal BMI range?
items.a: WHO defines normal as 18.5–24.9. The KSSO defines normal for Koreans as 18.5–22.9. The KSSO standard is more commonly used in Korea.

items.q: Does it apply to children and teenagers?
items.a: The adult BMI chart applies to those 18 and older. For children and adolescents, weight status should be assessed using growth chart percentiles — consult a pediatrician.

items.q: Can a low BMI still indicate health problems?
items.a: Yes. A BMI below 18.5 (underweight) can be associated with malnutrition, osteoporosis, and weakened immunity. If you've experienced rapid weight loss, consult a doctor.

whatIs.title: What is the Calorie Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Calorie Calculator takes your gender, age, height, weight, and activity level to calculate your Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR) and Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE). Using the Harris-Benedict or Mifflin-St Jeor formula, it recommends a daily calorie intake based on your goal — weight loss, maintenance, or gain.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your gender, age, height (cm), and weight (kg).
2. Select your activity level (sedentary / light / moderate / active / very active).
3. Choose your goal: lose weight, maintain, or gain weight.
4. Your BMR, TDEE, and recommended daily calories for your goal are displayed.

tips.title: Tips
items: Eating 500 kcal below your TDEE typically results in about 0.5 kg of weight loss per week.
Eating far below your BMR can cause muscle loss and rebound weight gain (yo-yo effect).
When choosing your activity level, consider all daily movement — your job, commute, and lifestyle — not just exercise.

items.q: What is the difference between BMR and TDEE?
items.a: BMR (Basal Metabolic Rate) is the minimum energy your body needs at complete rest. TDEE (Total Daily Energy Expenditure) is your actual daily calorie burn — BMR multiplied by an activity factor.

items.q: Which formula is more accurate?
items.a: The Mifflin-St Jeor formula is considered more accurate for modern populations. The Harris-Benedict formula is older (developed in 1919). This calculator defaults to Mifflin-St Jeor.

items.q: Do I need to follow the calculated calories exactly?
items.a: The result is an estimate. Individual metabolism, hormones, and health conditions all vary, so use it as a reference and adjust based on actual weight changes.

exerciseCalories.title: Exercise Calorie Burn (30 minutes)
exerciseCalories.note: * Based on 70kg adult male, individual differences may apply.

measurement.1: Place the tape measure lightly against the skin

management.0: Balanced diet and regular exercise are fundamental

management.2: Aim for gradual changes rather than rapid weight loss
management.3: Adequate sleep and stress management are also important

whatIs.title: What is the Body Fat Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Body Fat Calculator estimates your body fat percentage from body measurements (waist, neck, hip circumference, etc.) using the Navy and YMCA formulas. It helps you understand your body composition — the ratio of fat to muscle — beyond what a scale can tell you, enabling more specific health and fitness goals. No special equipment needed.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your gender (measurement sites differ for men and women).
2. Enter height, weight, neck circumference, and waist circumference (plus hip circumference for women).
3. Body fat percentage and classification are shown using both the Navy and YMCA formulas.
4. Lean mass (muscle, bone, etc.) and fat mass are displayed in kilograms.

tips.title: Tips
items: For consistent tracking, measure in the morning on an empty stomach under the same conditions each time.
Measure waist circumference at navel height while exhaling, using a flexible tape measure.
Healthy body fat targets: men 10–20%, women 18–28%. Athletes often maintain lower percentages.

items.q: Which formula is more accurate — Navy or YMCA?
items.a: The Navy formula uses more measurements and is generally considered more accurate. The YMCA formula only uses waist circumference, making it simpler but somewhat less precise.

items.q: Why might the result differ from an InBody scan?
items.a: InBody uses bioelectrical impedance for a more direct measurement. This tool provides an estimate based on body circumference — the margin of error is typically 2–4%.

items.q: Can body fat be too low?
items.a: Yes. Body fat is essential for hormone production and organ protection. Health problems can arise when body fat drops below about 3–5% for men or 10–13% for women.

measurementGuide.title: Accurate Circumference Measurement Methods

waistTips.3: Place lightly against skin, don't squeeze

neckTips.0: Measure at the narrowest part of the neck
neckTips.3: Don't extend or retract the neck

hipTips.0: Measure at the widest part of the hips

measurementGuide.measurementTipText: Morning measurements after using the bathroom and before eating provide the most accurate results.

whatIs.title: What is the Work Hours Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Work Hours Calculator helps part-time workers, hourly employees, and contract workers estimate their wages. Based on the 2026 minimum wage, it automatically calculates base pay, night shift premium (10 PM – 6 AM), overtime pay, and weekly holiday pay. You can see a complete breakdown of deductions and your final take-home pay in one view.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your hourly wage (or auto-fill the minimum wage).
2. Set your shift start/end times and work days.
3. Check the box if your shift includes night hours.
4. Click Calculate to see base pay, night premium, weekly holiday pay, estimated taxes, and take-home pay.

tips.title: Tips
items: Working 15 or more hours per week qualifies you for weekly holiday pay (주휴수당). It is calculated as: (scheduled weekly hours ÷ 40) × 8 × hourly wage.
Night shift premium applies to hours worked between 10 PM and 6 AM the following day — an additional 50% of ordinary wages.
If you receive a monthly salary, divide it by 209 hours to calculate your effective hourly rate.

items.q: What is the 2026 minimum wage?
items.a: The 2026 minimum wage is ₩10,030 per hour. Paying below this amount is a violation of the Minimum Wage Act.

items.q: Do premium allowances apply equally at workplaces with fewer than 5 employees?
items.a: Workplaces with fewer than 5 employees may be exempt from overtime, night, and holiday premiums (50% add-ons). However, the minimum wage and weekly holiday pay still apply.

items.q: Can daily workers claim weekly holiday pay?
items.a: Even daily workers can claim weekly holiday pay if they worked 15 or more hours per week at the same workplace for 4 consecutive weeks.

whatIs.title: What is the Lotto Number Generator?
whatIs.description: A tool that automatically generates Lotto 6/45 numbers and analyzes historical winning statistics. It offers multiple generation modes: fully random, frequency-based (using commonly drawn numbers), and number exclusion. Note that lotto is a pure game of chance — every number combination has an equal probability of winning (approximately 1 in 8.14 million).

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select a number generation mode: fully random, frequency-based, or with exclusions.
2. Set how many sets to generate (1–5).
3. Click Generate to get 6 numbers from 1 to 45.
4. Check the historical statistics section for number frequency and numbers that haven't appeared recently.

items.q: Does picking frequently drawn numbers improve my odds?
items.a: No. Each lotto draw is an independent event, so past frequency has no effect on future probabilities. The frequency-based mode is provided as a fun option, not a strategy.

items.q: Does buying the same numbers every week eventually guarantee a win?
items.a: The jackpot probability is the same every week regardless of whether you repeat numbers or change them (approximately 1 in 8,145,060). There is no mathematical difference.

items.q: Are the generated numbers truly random?
items.a: Yes — the browser's crypto.getRandomValues() API is used to generate cryptographically secure random numbers.

stats.title: Winning Probability Information
stats.disclaimer: * All number combinations have equal winning probability, and past statistics do not guarantee future results.

whatIs.title: What is the Ladder Game?
whatIs.description: The ladder game (사다리타기) is a traditional Korean random-draw method in which participants descend a ladder of vertical and horizontal lines to land on a result. This online tool lets you enter participant names and outcomes, then generates a fair ladder with a seeded algorithm. It supports direct position selection, blind mode, and reveal-one-by-one mode. Perfect for deciding order, assigning duties, drawing lots, or choosing prizes — and results can be shared via URL.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter participant names (separated by commas or line breaks).
2. Enter the result items (prizes, penalties, etc.).
3. Choose a game mode: position selection, blind, or reveal one by one.
4. Generate the ladder and select a starting column to see the result.

items.q: Are the results fair?
items.a: A seeded random algorithm generates the ladder. The same seed always produces the same result, enabling URL sharing — and no one can predict the result in advance.

items.q: How many participants can join?
items.a: Up to 10 participants. The ladder width adjusts automatically to fit the number of participants.

items.q: Can results be saved or shared?
items.a: Yes — participant names, results, and the seed are encoded into the URL, so sharing the link reproduces the same ladder. You can also save the result screen as an image.

whatIs.title: What is the Regex Extractor?
whatIs.description: The Regex Extractor is a powerful developer tool for finding, extracting, and replacing patterns in text using regular expressions. Perform tasks like log file analysis, email/phone number extraction, code refactoring, and data cleaning in an intuitive GUI. Preset patterns for common use cases — email, phone number, URL, IP address — are also included.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter a regex pattern or pick a preset (email, phone number, URL, etc.).
2. Set the flags: g (global), i (case-insensitive), m (multiline).
3. Paste the text you want to analyze into the input area.
4. Matches are highlighted; in extraction mode you can copy the list of matches.

items.q: Is there a Replace function?
items.a: Yes — you can switch between extraction mode and replace mode. Capture groups in the replacement pattern are referenced as $1, $2, etc.

items.q: Does it work with Korean text?
items.a: JavaScript regex supports Unicode, so it works fine with Korean text. The Korean character range is expressed as [가-힣].

items.q: What do I do when I get a regex error?
items.a: An error message is displayed for invalid patterns. Check for mismatched parentheses and properly escaped special characters (e.g., `\.`).

whatIs.title: What is this tool?
whatIs.description: The QR Code Generator converts text, URLs, email, phone numbers, SMS, WiFi credentials, and contact cards (vCard) into QR codes. Customize colors, error correction levels, and embed logos for branded QR codes.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Select the QR code type (text, URL, email, phone, SMS, WiFi, vCard).
Enter the information for your selected type.
Adjust size, colors, and error correction level. Upload a logo if needed.
Download the QR code as PNG or copy to clipboard.

items.q: What is error correction level?
items.a: It's the QR code's ability to remain readable even when partially damaged or covered by a logo. Choose from L(7%), M(15%), Q(25%), H(30%). H is recommended when embedding logos.

items.q: Do QR codes expire?
items.a: QR codes themselves don't expire. If it contains a URL, it works as long as the web address is valid.

items.q: Will colored QR codes still scan?
items.a: Yes, as long as the foreground is sufficiently darker than the background. Light foreground on dark background may not scan properly.

whatIs.title: What is the Fuel Cost Calculator?
whatIs.description: An expense calculation tool for accurately computing fuel costs and depreciation for business vehicles. Enter the vehicle type (sedan / SUV / truck), fuel type (gasoline / diesel / LPG / electric), fuel efficiency, and mileage, and it calculates the deductible business expense based on your business-use ratio. Useful for accurately claiming vehicle-related expenses on personal income tax returns and corporate tax filings.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the vehicle type and fuel type.
2. Enter fuel efficiency (km/L) and monthly mileage (km).
3. Enter the fuel price (₩/L) and the business-use percentage (%).
4. Monthly and annual fuel costs, along with the deductible business amount, are calculated automatically.

items.q: What vehicle expenses can be claimed as business expenses?
items.a: Self-employed individuals can claim fuel costs, insurance premiums, depreciation, and repair costs in proportion to their business-use ratio. Keeping a mileage log makes it easier to substantiate the deduction.

items.q: Does it support electric vehicles?
items.a: Yes. For electric vehicles, the calculation is based on energy consumption (kWh/100km) and electricity rates (₩/kWh), equivalent to fuel costs.

items.q: How is depreciation calculated?
items.a: Annual depreciation is calculated by dividing the vehicle's acquisition cost by its useful life (e.g., 5 years for passenger cars, 4 years for trucks), then applying the business-use ratio.

base64Converter.title: Base64 Encoder/Decoder
base64Converter.description: Encode text and files to Base64 or decode Base64 back to original.

errors.encodeError: Error encoding. Please check your input.
errors.decodeError: Error decoding. Please verify the Base64 format.

whatIs.title: What is Base64?
whatIs.description: Base64 is an encoding scheme that converts binary data to ASCII strings. Widely used in email attachments, web image data URIs, and API communication.

useCases.title: Use Cases
items: Embed images directly in HTML/CSS (Data URI)
Encode API authentication tokens
Email attachment transmission
Text transmission of binary data

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. On the Encode tab, enter text or a file to convert it to a Base64 string.
2. On the Decode tab, enter a Base64 string to restore the original text or file.
3. Enable the URL-safe option if you need URL-safe Base64.
4. When encoding files, you can also output in data URI format (data:image/png;base64,...).

tips.title: Tips
items: Base64-encoding an image lets you embed it directly in HTML (``) or CSS (`background-image: url(data:...)`).
JWT tokens have Base64URL-encoded Headers and Payloads — use this tool to decode them easily.
Base64 increases data size by about 33%, so consider alternatives when file size matters.

items.q: What is the difference between Base64 and Base64URL?
items.a: Base64URL replaces `+` and `/` (which have special meaning in URLs) with `-` and `_`, and omits the `=` padding. It's used in JWTs, cookies, and URL parameters.

items.q: Is Base64 a form of encryption?
items.a: No. Base64 is an encoding scheme, not encryption. Anyone can decode it, so it must not be used for security purposes.

items.q: Can it encode Korean text?
items.a: Yes — any UTF-8 encoded text, including Korean and all Unicode characters, can be converted to Base64.

urlEncoder.title: URL Encoder/Decoder
urlEncoder.description: Safely encode URLs or decode encoded URLs back to original. Includes URL parameter analysis.

input.textPlaceholder: Enter text or URL to encode...

errors.decodeError: Error decoding. Please verify the URL encoding format.

whatIs.title: What is URL Encoding?
whatIs.description: URL encoding (percent-encoding) converts characters that cannot be used in URLs to %XX format. Allows safe inclusion of special characters, spaces, and non-ASCII characters in URLs.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. On the Encode tab, enter a URL or query parameter to convert it to percent-encoding (%XX).
2. On the Decode tab, enter a percent-encoded URL to restore the original string.
3. Use the URL parameter parser to break a query string into key-value pairs.
4. Choose between encodeURIComponent and encodeURI depending on what you're encoding.

tips.title: Tips
items: Use encodeURIComponent when encoding individual query parameter values (not the full URL). encodeURI is intended for the full URL.
Korean characters in URLs can be handled differently by different browsers, so always transmit them in percent-encoded form for safety.
When debugging complex API requests, use the URL parameter parser to break the query string apart and quickly identify issues.

items.q: What is the difference between encodeURI and encodeURIComponent?
items.a: encodeURI preserves URL structural characters (`:`, `/`, `?`, `#`, etc.) and encodes the rest. encodeURIComponent also encodes those structural characters, making it suitable for individual parameter values.

items.q: Should spaces be encoded as `+` or `%20`?
items.a: In URL paths, `%20` is the standard. In HTML form data (application/x-www-form-urlencoded), spaces are encoded as `+`. Check the server specification when making API requests.

items.q: How do I put a password with special characters in a URL?
items.a: Values containing special characters like passwords must be encoded with encodeURIComponent. Characters like `@`, `:`, and `/` will cause URL parsing errors if included unencoded.

hashGenerator.title: Hash Generator
hashGenerator.description: Generate hash values for text or files. Supports MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512 and more.

whatIs.title: What is a Hash?
whatIs.description: A hash function converts arbitrary data into a fixed-length value. Same input always produces the same hash. Used for file integrity verification, password storage, and more.

algorithms.title: Algorithm Descriptions
items: MD5: 128-bit, fast but not recommended for security
SHA-1: 160-bit, for legacy compatibility
SHA-256: 256-bit, current standard recommendation
SHA-512: 512-bit, highest security level

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the string you want to hash in the Text tab.
2. Select a file in the File tab to generate a hash for integrity verification.
3. Choose an algorithm: MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, or SHA-512.
4. Copy the generated hash or compare it against a reference hash for verification.

tips.title: Tips
items: After downloading a file, compare its hash against the official hash to verify it hasn't been tampered with. Especially useful for verifying software distribution packages.
MD5 and SHA-1 are cryptographically weak for password storage. Use purpose-built algorithms like bcrypt or Argon2 for hashing passwords.
SHA-256 is the most widely used secure hash algorithm today, found in Git commit IDs, SSL certificates, and more.

items.q: Is MD5 still acceptable to use?
items.a: MD5 has known collision vulnerabilities and is not suitable for cryptographic security purposes. It is still used for non-security tasks like file integrity checksums.

items.q: Does the same input always produce the same hash?
items.a: Yes — hash functions are deterministic: the same input always produces the same output. Even a single character change results in a completely different hash.

items.q: Can the original text be recovered from a hash?
items.a: Generally, no. Hash functions are one-way by design — mathematically irreversible. Add a salt to passwords to protect against rainbow table attacks.

diffViewer.title: Diff Viewer
diffViewer.description: Compare two texts or code and visually identify the differences.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Enter original text on the left
Enter modified text on the right
Click the Compare button
Green means added, red means removed

useCases.title: Use Cases
items: Review code changes
Compare document versions
Track config file changes
Compare before/after translations

whatIs.title: What is the Diff Viewer?
whatIs.description: A tool that compares two pieces of text or code side by side and color-codes additions, deletions, and changes. It's useful whenever you need to quickly spot differences between two texts — code reviews, configuration file changes, document version comparisons, and more. Like git diff, it supports line-by-line and word-by-word comparison.

tips.title: Tips
items: During code reviews, paste the before and after code to quickly gauge the scope of changes.
When comparing config files (JSON, YAML, ENV), use the ignore-whitespace option to focus on actual content changes rather than formatting differences.
Paste translation files (ko.json, en.json) side by side to spot any missing keys.

items.q: What is the difference between unified view and split view?
items.a: Unified view shows changed lines sequentially. Split view places the original on the left and the modified version on the right simultaneously — split view is generally better for understanding context.

items.q: Can it handle large amounts of text?
items.a: It can compare thousands of lines within browser memory limits. For very large files, it's recommended to compare only the relevant sections.

items.q: Can differences in whitespace or line breaks be ignored?
items.a: Enabling the ignore-whitespace option filters out indentation and trailing space differences so you can focus on meaningful content changes.

colorConverter.title: Color Converter
colorConverter.description: Convert between HEX, RGB, HSL, HSV, CMYK and other color formats.

useCases.title: Use Cases
items: Web design color code conversion
Share colors between design tools
Check CMYK values for printing
Create color palettes

whatIs.title: What is the Color Converter?
whatIs.description: A designer and developer tool that converts between color formats — HEX, RGB, HSL, HSV, CMYK, and more — in real time. Use it whenever you need to translate between color systems for web CSS development, graphic design, or print production. Includes a color palette preview and one-click clipboard copy.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Pick a color using the color picker or enter a HEX/RGB/HSL value directly.
2. The entered value is instantly converted to all other formats.
3. Use the copy button next to any format to copy the value to your clipboard.
4. Preview the actual color in the color swatch.

tips.title: Tips
items: `hsl()` is intuitive in CSS: H (hue 0–360), S (saturation 0–100%), L (lightness 0–100%) make it easy to adjust colors.
Screen RGB colors don't print exactly as-is. Check the CMYK values and share them with your printer for accurate print colors.
For transparency (alpha channel), use #RRGGBBAA in HEX or rgba(r,g,b,a) in CSS.

items.q: What is the difference between 3-digit and 6-digit HEX codes?
items.a: #RGB is shorthand for #RRGGBB. For example, #F0A equals #FF00AA. The shorthand is only valid when each channel is a repeated pair of identical digits.

items.q: Does CMYK guarantee accurate print colors?
items.a: CMYK values from RGB conversion are approximate due to the difference between color spaces. For exact print colors, use a standard color matching system such as Pantone.

items.q: Why does the same HEX code look different across browsers?
items.a: Differences in monitor color profiles (sRGB, Display P3, etc.) and calibration can cause the same value to appear differently. Use a color management system (CMS) for design work that requires consistency.

loremIpsum.title: Lorem Ipsum Generator
loremIpsum.description: Generate dummy text for web design and development. Supports Latin and Korean.

whatIs.title: What is Lorem Ipsum?
whatIs.description: Lorem Ipsum is dummy text used in the publishing and graphic design industries. Used to preview layout and design before actual content is ready.

useCases.title: Use Cases
items: Website layout design
Print mockup creation
App UI prototypes
Presentation templates

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the language: Latin or Korean.
2. Set the generation unit (words / sentences / paragraphs) and quantity.
3. Click Generate to create placeholder text.
4. Copy the generated text or save it as a file.

tips.title: Tips
items: Using Lorem Ipsum in UI mockups lets you focus on the layout without getting distracted by actual content.
Korean placeholder text provides the most realistic approximation of Korean typography for checking fonts, line spacing, and text sizes.
Adjust the paragraph count to produce the right volume for your context — long body copy, short descriptions, card content, and so on.

items.q: Where does Lorem Ipsum come from?
items.a: It is excerpted from Cicero's 'de Finibus Bonorum et Malorum' (On the Ends of Good and Evil). It has been used as placeholder text in the printing industry since the 1500s.

items.q: Is it okay to ship Lorem Ipsum to production?
items.a: No. Lorem Ipsum is a placeholder used only during development and design. It must be replaced with real content before going live.

items.q: Can it generate text wrapped in HTML tags?
items.a:
Enable the HTML format option to wrap paragraphs in

tags — convenient for publishing and front-end development work.

unitConverter.title: Unit Converter
unitConverter.description: Convert length, weight, temperature, area, volume, data, and CSS units.

supported.title: Supported Units
items: Length: mm, cm, m, km, inch, ft, yard, mile
Weight: mg, g, kg, ton, oz, lb
Temperature: Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin
Area: mm², cm², m², km², pyeong, acre, ft²
Volume: mL, L, m³, gallon, fl oz
Data: bit, B, KB, MB, GB, TB
CSS: px, rem, em, pt, vw, vh

tips.title: Useful Tips
items: Real estate: 1 pyeong ≈ 3.3m²
CSS: 1rem = 16px (default)
Data: 1KB = 1024B

whatIs.title: What is the Unit Converter?
whatIs.description: A tool for converting between a wide variety of measurement units — length, weight, temperature, area, volume, data size, and CSS units. It covers everyday use cases like cooking and travel as well as developer-specific conversions such as CSS px, rem, em, and vw. Conversion formulas are shown alongside results to help with learning.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select a category: length, weight, temperature, area, volume, data, or CSS.
2. Enter the source unit and value.
3. Select the target unit to see the converted value immediately.
4. For CSS unit conversion, set the base font size (reference value for rem).

items.q: What is the formula for Celsius–Fahrenheit conversion?
items.a: °F to °C: (°F − 32) × 5/9. °C to °F: (°C × 9/5) + 32. Body temperature 37°C = 98.6°F; water boiling point 100°C = 212°F.

items.q: What is the base value for CSS rem to px conversion?
items.a: The browser's default font size is 16px, so 1rem = 16px by default. If you've changed the font-size on the HTML element, update the base value accordingly.

items.q: Is 1 GB equal to 1000 MB or 1024 MB?
items.a: Under the SI standard, 1 GB = 1000 MB (gigabyte). In binary notation, 1 GiB = 1024 MiB (gibibyte). Hard drives typically use SI units; operating systems typically display binary units.

textConverter.title: Text Converter
textConverter.description: Convert text case, coding style, and formatting in various ways.

useCases.title: Use Cases
items: Standardize variable names
Convert CSS class names
Convert API field names
Data cleanup and formatting

whatIs.title: What is the Text Converter?
whatIs.description: A developer tool offering a variety of text format transformations: case conversion (upper/lower), naming-convention styles (camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, kebab-case, etc.), whitespace cleanup, and line break handling. Use it for standardizing variable naming conventions, processing CSV data, or cleaning up document text.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Paste the text you want to transform into the input area.
2. Select the desired conversion: uppercase, lowercase, camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, etc.
3. The converted result is displayed immediately.
4. Use the copy button to save the result to your clipboard.

tips.title: Tips
items: Useful for batch-converting API response field names from snake_case to JavaScript's camelCase.
Easily convert SQL column names (UPPER_CASE) to code variable names, or vice versa.
Try the join-lines feature to merge multiple lines into one, or replace line breaks with a custom delimiter.

items.q: What is the difference between camelCase and PascalCase?
items.a: camelCase starts with a lowercase letter and capitalizes subsequent words (e.g., myVariable). PascalCase capitalizes every word (e.g., MyClass). PascalCase is typically used for class names; camelCase for variables and functions.

items.q: Does it work with Korean text?
items.a: Case conversion only applies to Latin characters. In mixed text, only the English parts are converted while Korean characters remain unchanged.

items.q: What rules does Title Case follow?
items.a: Title Case capitalizes the first letter of each word. Handling of articles (a, an, the) and conjunctions (and, or) may vary depending on settings.

whatIs.title: What is the Barcode Generator?
whatIs.description: A free online tool for generating various barcode formats — CODE128, EAN-13, EAN-8, UPC-A, QR code, and more. Create barcodes instantly for product management, inventory, event tickets, library books, and other applications, then download them in PNG or SVG format.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the barcode format (CODE128, EAN-13, EAN-8, UPC-A, etc.).
2. Enter the data to encode in the barcode.
3. Configure options such as width, height, color, and whether to display the human-readable text.
4. Preview the barcode and download it as a PNG or SVG file.

items.q: What data do I need to create an EAN-13 barcode?
items.a: EAN-13 consists of 13 digits. The first 3 are the country code (Korea: 880), the next 4–6 are the company code, the rest are the product code, and the final digit is a check digit. Official GS1 code registration is required.

items.q: Can the generated barcode be read by a scanner?
items.a: Yes — the generated barcodes can be read by standard barcode scanners and smartphone camera apps. We recommend doing a scan test after printing.

items.q: Which format should I download — SVG or PNG?
items.a: Use SVG for print materials where scalability matters; use PNG for web or general documents. Label printer software typically has better PNG support.

jsonXmlConverter.description: Convert JSON and XML data quickly and accurately. Professional conversion options and large file processing support for developers.

whatIs.title: What is the JSON ↔ XML Converter?
whatIs.description: A developer tool for fast and accurate bidirectional conversion between JSON and XML data serialization formats. Essential for integrating REST APIs with SOAP/XML-based legacy systems, converting configuration file formats, and data migration tasks. Offers advanced conversion options including root element naming and attribute handling.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the conversion direction: JSON → XML or XML → JSON.
2. Paste the source data or upload a file.
3. For JSON → XML conversion, set the root element name.
4. Copy the result or download it as a file.

tips.title: Tips
items: JSON arrays have no direct XML equivalent. When converting, array items are generated as repeated elements with the same tag name.
Configure how XML attributes (@attribute) and text nodes (#text) are handled to produce the JSON structure you want.
When integrating SOAP APIs, converting the XML response to JSON makes it much easier to work with in JavaScript.

items.q: How are XML namespaces (xmlns) handled?
items.a: xmlns attributes are treated as regular attributes during JSON conversion. Complex SOAP XML with namespaces may require manual cleanup.

items.q: How is JSON null represented in XML?
items.a: JSON null can be represented as an empty element (`<key/>`) or with the attribute xsi:nil="true". Choose your preferred handling in the conversion options.

items.q: Can large XML files be converted?
items.a: Files of several megabytes can be processed, but very large files may slow down due to browser memory limits. Files over 10 MB are best handled server-side.

whatIs.title: What is this tool?

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Drag and drop or select a GLB, GLTF, OBJ, or STL file.
Drag to rotate the model and scroll to zoom in/out.
Use tabs to switch between Viewer, Convert, Optimize, and Print analysis.
Take screenshots or export to different formats.

tips.title: Tips
items: Enable auto-rotate to view the model from all angles.
Wireframe mode reveals mesh structure and polygon density.
The Print tab estimates volume, print time, and material weight before actual printing.

items.q: Which 3D file formats are supported?
items.a: GLB, GLTF, OBJ, and STL. GLB is most convenient as it includes textures in a single file.

items.q: Are files uploaded to a server?
items.a: No. All processing happens in your browser. Files are never sent to external servers.

items.q: How accurate is the 3D printing analysis?
items.a: Volume and bounding box calculations are precise, but print time and material estimates are based on default FDM/SLA settings.

tips.title: Secure Password Tips
items: Use passwords with at least 12 characters
Combine uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters
Don't include personal information (birthday, phone number)
Use different passwords for each site
Use a password manager

passphrase.title: Benefits of Passphrases
items: Easy to remember with high security
Random word combinations provide sufficient entropy
Recommended to use 4 or more words
Adding separators further improves security

whatIs.title: What is the Password Generator?
whatIs.description: A security tool that generates strong, secure passwords and easy-to-remember passphrases. It provides random passwords combining upper/lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters, as well as EFF wordlist-based passphrases. A password strength analyzer shows the security level of the generated password. All processing happens entirely in the browser — nothing is sent to a server.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the mode: random characters or passphrase.
2. Set the length and character types to include (uppercase, lowercase, numbers, special characters).
3. Click Generate to create a cryptographically secure password.
4. Copy it to your clipboard and save it in a password manager.

items.q: Are generated passwords stored on a server?
items.a: No. Passwords are generated entirely in the browser using the Web Crypto API (crypto.getRandomValues) and are never transmitted anywhere.

items.q: Aren't passphrases weaker than random character passwords?
items.a: A passphrase of 4–5 or more words has sufficient entropy. A passphrase like 'correct-horse-battery-staple' can actually be stronger than a random character string while being much easier to remember.

items.q: How should I manage my passwords?
items.a: Use a password manager such as Bitwarden, 1Password, or KeePass to safely store unique passwords for each site. Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) on all important accounts.

businessDays.description: Business days exclude weekends (Sat, Sun) and Korean public holidays, including lunar calendar holidays like Seollal and Chuseok.

whatIs.title: What is the D-Day Calculator?
whatIs.description: A single tool that covers D-Day countdown to a specific date, calculating the difference between two dates, and adding or subtracting days from a date. Handles everything date-related — exam prep, travel planning, event coordination, contract expiry tracking, and more. Includes Korean public holiday data and supports business day calculations.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. In the D-Day tab, select a target date to see the number of days remaining from today.
2. In the Date Difference tab, select two dates to see the total days, weeks, months, and years between them.
3. In the Date Calculation tab, add or subtract days from a reference date to find a resulting date.
4. Enable the Exclude Holidays option for business day calculations to exclude Korean public holidays.

tips.title: Tips
items: Use business day mode for calculating contract review periods or legal filing deadlines.
Register important dates — probation end dates, visa expiry, insurance renewal — as D-Days and bookmark them for easy reference.
Share a project deadline countdown with your team using the URL sharing feature.

items.q: What are the standards for D-1 and D-0?
items.a: This calculator treats the target date itself as D-0 and the day before as D-1. D-Day conventions can vary by context or organization, so verify important dates directly.

items.q: How far ahead does the Korean holiday data go?
items.a: Korean public holiday data for 2024–2030 is built in, including substitute holidays and lunar-calendar holidays (Lunar New Year, Chuseok).

items.q: Does it handle February 29 in leap years?
items.a: Yes — all date calculations, including leap year dates, are handled accurately.

whatIs.title: What is the Percent Calculator?
whatIs.description: A one-stop tool for the percentage calculations you encounter every day. It supports four calculation types: applying a discount rate, finding what percentage one value is of another, calculating the percentage change between two values, and adding or subtracting a percentage. Useful for shopping discounts, grade calculations, inflation analysis, and more.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the calculation type: basic percentage / ratio / percentage change / add-subtract percentage.
2. Enter the numbers required for that calculation.
3. The result is displayed instantly.
4. Multiple calculation types can be viewed simultaneously for easy comparison.

tips.title: Tips
items: To add tax: pre-tax price × 1.1 = price including 10% VAT. To reverse it: tax-inclusive price ÷ 1.1 = pre-tax price.
Reverse discount calculation: 'If 30% off equals ₩70,000, what was the original price?' → ₩70,000 ÷ (1 − 0.3) = ₩100,000.
Investment return rate = (current price − purchase price) / purchase price × 100. Use the percentage change tab.

items.q: Is a 20% discount followed by a 10% discount the same as 30% off?
items.a: No. 20% off then 10% off equals 0.8 × 0.9 = 0.72, which is a 28% discount. Sequential percentage discounts do not simply add up.

items.q: What is the difference between percentage points (%p) and percent (%)?
items.a: Going from 3% to 5% is a '2 percentage point increase' but also roughly a '67% increase.' %p expresses the absolute difference; % expresses the relative change.

items.q: How are decimal places handled?
items.a: Results are displayed to two decimal places by default. You can change the decimal precision setting if you need more precise results.

international.description: The international standard where age increases on your birthday. This became the unified legal age in Korea since June 2023.

korean.description: Traditional Korean age where you start at 1 year old at birth and gain a year every January 1st.

year.title: Year Age
year.description: Simply current year minus birth year. Everyone born in the same year has the same age regardless of birthday.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Enter your birthdate to see results in real-time

View international age, Korean age, and year age all at once

Next birthday D-Day with upcoming age
School grade info (entry year, current grade, same-grade peers)
Life timeline (major milestones by year)

whatIs.title: What is the Age Calculator?
whatIs.description: Enter your date of birth to instantly see your international age (만 나이), Korean counting age (세는 나이), calendar-year age (연 나이), Chinese zodiac sign (12 animals), and star sign, all at once. Following the 2023 Civil Act amendment, the international age standard is now official in Korean public and legal contexts, making accurate age calculation essential for employment, insurance, medical care, and more.

tips.title: Tips
items: Since June 2023, the international age (만 나이) is the official standard for legal and public matters in Korea. Be careful not to confuse it with the traditional Korean counting age.
Use this calculator to accurately verify insurance product age criteria, mandatory retirement age (age 60), and National Pension eligibility ages (63–65).
Helpful for checking age-based policy eligibility — such as minor status (under 19) or 'youth' criteria (19–34) as defined by various government programs.

items.q: What is the difference between international age and Korean counting age?
items.a: International age (만 나이) increments by 1 after each birthday — the global standard. Korean counting age (세는 나이) starts at 1 at birth and adds 1 on every January 1st.

items.q: What is calendar-year age (연 나이)?
items.a: It is the current year minus the birth year (e.g., 2025 − 2000 = 25). Used for school enrollment age criteria and military service age calculations.

items.q: What is the international age if my birthday hasn't passed yet this year?
items.a: If your birthday hasn't occurred yet this year, your international age is (current year − birth year − 1). It increases by 1 on your birthday.

gpaCalculator.title: GPA Calculator
gpaCalculator.description: Enter courses with credits and grades to calculate your GPA

whatIs.title: What is the GPA Calculator?
whatIs.description: An academic management tool that calculates your grade point average (GPA) from the credit hours and grades (letter grade or score) for each course. Supports 4.5, 4.3, and 4.0 grading scales. Use it to check whether you've met scholarship GPA requirements, graduation requirements, or graduate school application criteria.

items.q: Which scale should I use — 4.5 or 4.0?
items.a: It depends on your school. Most Korean universities use the 4.5 scale, but some use 4.3 or 4.0. Check your institution's academic regulations.

items.q: Are P/F (Pass/Fail) courses included in the GPA?
items.a: Generally, P/F courses are not included in GPA calculations. Exclude them when entering your courses in this calculator.

items.q: What are the standard grade-to-GPA conversions?
items.a: On a 4.5 scale, the typical mapping is: A+ = 4.5, A0 = 4.0, B+ = 3.5, B0 = 3.0, C+ = 2.5, C0 = 2.0, D+ = 1.5, D0 = 1.0, F = 0. This may vary by institution.

reverse.description: Calculates the required GPA for remaining credits to reach your target
reverse.impossible: Not achievable — exceeds maximum scale of {max}.
reverse.alreadyAchieved: You've already reached your target GPA!
