whatIs.title: What is the Random Picker?
whatIs.description: A drawing tool with three functions: random number generation, random item selection from a list, and random team assignment. Use it whenever you need a fair, unbiased random selection — deciding order, assigning duties, choosing a presenter, dividing into teams, or running a giveaway. Cryptographically secure random numbers ensure unbiased results.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select a mode from the tabs: Numbers, List, or Team Split.
2. Number mode: set the range (min–max) and quantity to generate.
3. List mode: enter items separated by line breaks or commas, and set how many to pick.
4. Team mode: enter a participant list and the number of teams to randomly assign everyone.

tips.title: Tips
items: When determining order without repeats, enable the 'no duplicates' option in List mode.
For team assignments, use the auto-balance option to distribute participants as evenly as possible.
Share the result via URL to revisit it later or use it as documentation.

items.q: Is it truly fair random?
items.a: Yes — it uses crypto.getRandomValues() from the Web Crypto API, generating cryptographically secure random numbers with no bias or manipulation.

items.q: Can the same number appear more than once?
items.a: Duplicates are allowed by default. Enable the 'no duplicates' option to ensure only unique numbers or items are selected.

items.q: Can I save the pick result?
items.a: You can copy the result to your clipboard, or share it as a link using the URL sharing feature.

whatIs.title: What is the Timer / Stopwatch?
whatIs.description: Three tools in one: a stopwatch, a countdown timer, and a Pomodoro timer. Use it for timing workouts, cooking, presentation practice, or focused work sessions (Pomodoro technique). It continues running in background tabs and plays an alert sound when the timer ends.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. In the Stopwatch tab, start and stop with the button, and use Lap to record split times.
2. In the Timer tab, enter hours/minutes/seconds and start the countdown — an alert plays when it ends.
3. In the Pomodoro tab, set your focus time (default 25 min) and break time (5 min) and start a cycle.
4. The timer keeps running even if you switch to another tab.

tips.title: Tips
items: Pomodoro technique: 25 minutes of focus → 5-minute break → after 4 cycles, a 15–30 minute long break. A proven time management method for improving concentration and productivity.
Use the stopwatch lap feature during interval training to record each set's time.
Set the timer during presentation rehearsals to practice fitting within your allotted time.

items.q: Does the timer reset if I close the browser?
items.a: Yes — closing the browser resets the timer. Keep the tab open for important timers, or use a dedicated app with notification support.

items.q: Why isn't the alert sound playing?
items.a: Check whether the browser tab is muted and whether your system volume is on. Some browsers require a user interaction before they can play audio.

items.q: Can I set a timer longer than one hour?
items.a: Yes — use the hours input to set timers of several hours. Great for cooking, fermentation, or sleep timers.

tips.title: Notes
items: Binary: The 0 and 1 number system computers actually process
Octal: Used for Unix file permissions (chmod 755)
Hex: Used for color codes (#FF0000), memory addresses, etc.

whatIs.title: What is the Number Base Converter?
whatIs.description: A developer tool that converts in real time between binary (Base 2), octal (Base 8), decimal (Base 10), and hexadecimal (Base 16). Essential for computer science study, network subnet calculations, memory address analysis, color code (HEX) processing, and bitwise operations.

items.q: Why is hexadecimal so commonly used in computing?
items.a: Hexadecimal can represent 4 binary bits as a single digit, making it concise for memory addresses, color codes, hash values, and more. It uses digits 0–9 and letters A–F (representing 10–15).

items.q: Are negative numbers and decimals supported?
items.a: The current version supports positive integer conversion. Negative numbers require two's complement, and decimal fractions require a separate conversion approach.

items.q: Can I convert an IP address to binary?
items.a: Yes — enter each octet (0–255) individually into the decimal field to convert it to binary. For example: 192 → 11000000, 168 → 10101000.

whatIs.title: What is the Notepad?
whatIs.description: An online notepad that manages multiple notes in tabs with auto-save. Notes are stored securely in your browser's localStorage without any login, and work offline without an internet connection. Use it for quick text editing, checklists, code snippets, work notes, and more.

items.q: Where are notes saved?
items.a: Notes are saved in your browser's localStorage. They are never sent to a server and your privacy is protected. However, clearing your browser cache may also delete your notes.

items.q: Can I view my notes on another device?
items.a: localStorage is tied to the specific browser, so notes are not accessible on other devices. To share with another device, copy the content and send it via email or a messaging app.

items.q: Is there a limit on the number of notes?
items.a: You can save notes up to the localStorage capacity (typically 5–10 MB). Ordinary text notes won't come close to filling that limit even with hundreds of entries.

compoundCalculator.title: Compound Interest Calculator
compoundCalculator.description: Calculate compound interest and investment returns with principal, rate, and period

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Enter principal, annual rate, and investment period
Select compound frequency (annual/semi-annual/quarterly/monthly/daily)
Add monthly deposits to calculate investment returns with regular contributions

tips.title: The Magic of Compound Interest
items: Compound interest earns interest on interest, benefiting long-term investments
Shorter compounding periods (monthly > annual) generate more interest
Rule of 72: 72 ÷ rate = years to double your money

whatIs.title: What is the Compound Interest Calculator?
whatIs.description: A financial tool that calculates compound interest and final returns from your principal, annual interest rate, and investment period. It also supports monthly additional contributions, letting you simulate the long-term growth of dollar-cost averaging investments. A chart visually shows how your assets grow exponentially over time thanks to the power of compounding.

items.q: What is the Rule of 72?
items.a: Divide 72 by the annual return rate to estimate how many years it takes for your investment to double. For example, at a 6% annual rate: 72 ÷ 6 = 12 years to double.

items.q: How much does the compounding frequency (monthly vs. annual) affect returns?
items.a: Monthly compounding reinvests interest more frequently than annual compounding, resulting in slightly higher returns. At 12% per year: annual compounding yields 12%, while monthly compounding yields an effective rate of about 12.68%.

items.q: How do I calculate real returns adjusted for inflation?
items.a: Fisher equation: real return ≈ nominal return − inflation rate. For example, 8% nominal return with 3% inflation equals about 5% real return. Subtract the inflation rate from this calculator's output to assess the real value.

Switching to LED lighting can reduce lighting costs by 80%

rateInfo.title: Rate Structure
items: Korean residential electricity uses progressive pricing tiers
Summer (Jul-Aug) and winter (Dec-Feb) have adjusted tier ranges
Bill consists of base fee + usage fee + climate fee + fuel adjustment
VAT (10%) and Electric Industry Fund (3.7%) are added

whatIs.title: What is the Electricity Bill Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that calculates monthly electricity costs based on Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO)'s progressive residential tariff system. It reflects all items appearing on an actual bill: basic charge, energy charge, climate environment charge, fuel cost adjustment, VAT, and the electricity industry promotion levy. Use it to estimate your bill before it arrives, or to simulate the cost of additional appliances like air conditioners or electric heaters.

tips.title: Tips to Reduce Your Electricity Bill
items: Know your progressive tiers (Tier 1: ≤200 kWh, Tier 2: ≤400 kWh). If you're near a tier boundary, cutting usage slightly can save a disproportionately large amount.
Summer (Jul–Aug) and winter (Dec–Feb) seasons have adjusted tier limits. Track your usage separately during these months to avoid unexpected jumps.
Combine air conditioning with a fan or circulator, adjust your refrigerator temperature, and eliminate standby power to save 10–30 kWh per month.
Installing a small rooftop solar panel reduces your net usage, potentially dropping you into a lower progressive tier for significant savings.

items.q: What is a progressive tariff?
items.a: A system where the unit price increases as usage increases. Korea applies a 3-tier system; the Tier 3 rate is roughly 3 times the Tier 1 rate, so heavy users pay steeply more.

items.q: How much more will an air conditioner cost per month?
items.a: Running a 1.5 kW AC for 8 hours/day over 30 days adds about 360 kWh. Depending on your existing usage, this can push you into Tier 2 or 3 and add tens of thousands of won. Try entering the combined usage in the calculator to compare.

items.q: Why might the result differ slightly from my actual bill?
items.a: Items such as the fuel cost adjustment, TV license fee, and welfare discounts vary by individual circumstances. This calculator uses standard rates for a typical household, so small differences from your actual bill are normal.

simulator.title: Appliance Simulator
simulator.description: Enter daily usage hours for your appliances to estimate monthly electricity consumption.
simulator.note: * Actual usage may vary by product efficiency and environment.

items: Useful for checking responsive design in web development
Share device info for technical support requests
Compare and verify display specifications

whatIs.title: What is the Screen & Device Info Tool?
whatIs.description: A tool that instantly displays detailed information about the device you are currently using: screen resolution, browser viewport dimensions, device pixel ratio (DPR), operating system, browser version, and network connection type. Web developers and designers can use it to debug responsive layouts, verify media query breakpoints, and understand the user's rendering environment without writing any code.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Open the page — all device and browser information is detected automatically. No input required.
2. Compare the physical screen resolution (px) with the browser viewport size to understand how your device scales content.
3. Resize the browser window to see viewport values update in real time, making it easy to identify exact responsive breakpoints.
4. Click the copy button to copy all device info to your clipboard and paste it into bug reports or support tickets.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Use alongside Chrome DevTools' device simulation to quickly validate actual viewport values at specific resolutions.
On Retina displays (DPR ≥ 2), CSS pixels differ from physical pixels. Always check DPR when debugging blurry image issues.
The network connection type (4G/Wi-Fi/offline) is shown, helping you verify real connectivity conditions during performance testing on slower networks.

items.q: Why are the screen resolution and viewport size different?
items.a: Physical screen resolution is the actual pixel count of the display. The viewport is the area the browser uses to render web content. A device with DPR 2 and a 1920×1080 display may show a 960×540 viewport for CSS purposes.

items.q: Is my device information sent to any server?
items.a: No. All information is read locally by JavaScript in your browser and is never transmitted or stored on any server.

items.q: Will I see different items on mobile vs. desktop?
items.a: Yes. Mobile-specific items such as touch screen support, accelerometer, and orientation sensor are only shown on mobile devices. Desktop shows mouse/hover capability instead.

whatIs.title: What is the Emoji Picker?
whatIs.description: A tool to quickly find any of thousands of Unicode emojis by browsing categories or searching by keyword, then copy them to your clipboard with a single click. Perfect for crafting social media posts, messages, or documents without having to open your keyboard's emoji panel.

items.q: Can I search in both Korean and English?
items.a: Yes. Keywords like '웃음', '하트' in Korean and 'smile', 'heart' in English both work. Matches are made against emoji names and tags.

items.q: Why do some copied emojis look different or broken in other apps?
items.a: While emojis follow the Unicode standard, each platform (Apple, Google, Samsung, etc.) renders them differently. Older devices may not support newer emoji codepoints and display an empty box (□) instead.

items.q: Are the latest emojis (e.g., Emoji 15.0) included?
items.a: We include emoji data based on the latest Unicode standard available. However, whether an emoji displays correctly depends on your browser and OS support. Chrome on Windows 11 / macOS Ventura or later offers the best coverage.

Convertible range: 1900-2100
The same lunar date may correspond to different solar dates depending on leap month

whatIs.title: What is the Lunar-Solar Date Converter?
whatIs.description: A tool that converts between the Korean lunar calendar (lunisolar calendar) and the Gregorian solar calendar in both directions. Useful for finding the solar date of traditionally observed events like birthdays, memorial days, and Korean holidays (Seollal, Chuseok) that are tracked by the lunar calendar.

items.q: Why does the same lunar date fall on a different solar date each year?
items.a: The lunar calendar is based on the moon's cycle (~29.5 days), giving it about 354 days per year — 11 days shorter than the solar year. A leap month (윤달) is added every few years to realign them, which is why the solar equivalent of a lunar date shifts annually.

items.q: How are dates in a leap month (윤달) handled?
items.a: A checkbox is provided to indicate whether a date falls in a leap month. For example, '윤4월 15일' and '4월 15일' are different days. If your birthdate or anniversary falls in a leap month, be sure to select that option.

items.q: What year range does the converter support?
items.a: Conversion data covers 1900 to 2100, which is more than sufficient for practical everyday use.

ipChecker.title: IP Address Checker
ipChecker.description: Check your public IP address, location, ISP, and network information at a glance.

whatIsIp.title: What is an IP Address?
items: An IP (Internet Protocol) address is a unique identifier for devices connected to the internet
Public IP is the address visible externally, while private IP is the internal network address
There are two formats: IPv4 (e.g., 192.168.0.1) and IPv6 (e.g., 2001:db8::1)

whyCheck.title: When to Check Your IP
items: When setting up remote access or server configuration
Verifying VPN connection status
Checking ISP information
Setting up IP-based access controls

whatIs.title: What is the IP Address Checker?
whatIs.description: A tool that instantly displays your public IP address along with geolocation data (country, city), ISP, ASN, and timezone — all in one place. Commonly used to verify VPN connectivity, check which region a streaming or gaming service sees you as, and quickly retrieve your IP for firewall whitelist configurations.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Open the page and your current public IP address appears automatically — no input needed.
2. Click the copy icon next to your IP to save it to your clipboard, or view your approximate location on the map.
3. Enable a VPN and refresh the page to instantly see the updated IP and location.
4. To look up a specific IP address, enter it in the input field and press the lookup button.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: When using a VPN or proxy, this tool lets you instantly verify whether your IP has changed and whether you are connected from the intended country.
Useful for confirming that region-locked content (streaming, gaming) is accessible from the correct geographic location.
Quickly copy your public IP for server configs, firewall rules, or remote access setups that require a fixed IP whitelist.

items.q: Does the IP location reveal my home address?
items.a: No. IP-based geolocation shows the region where your ISP has assigned that IP block, not your physical address. Accuracy is typically at the city level.

items.q: Will I see an IPv4 or IPv6 address?
items.a: Depending on your device and network setup, either IPv4 (e.g., 123.45.67.89) or IPv6 (e.g., 2001:db8::1) may be shown. Both versions are supported; the address displayed corresponds to the protocol used for the connection.

items.q: Will my IP look different on a corporate or school Wi-Fi?
items.a: Yes. Corporate and school networks use NAT, so all devices on the network share a single public IP. This tool will show the network's public IP, not your device's private IP.

discountCalculator.title: Discount Calculator
discountCalculator.description: Calculate discount rates, discounted prices, and original prices easily.

basic.title: Basic Usage
items: Enter the original price and discount rate to calculate the final price
Use quick rate buttons to apply 10%-90% discounts instantly
Stacked discounts are applied sequentially (e.g., 20%+10% = 28%)

tips.title: Discount Tips
items: Buy 1 Get 1 Free is effectively a 50% discount
Buy 2 Get 1 Free is effectively a 33.3% discount
Stacked discounts are applied sequentially, not simply added

whatIs.title: What is the Discount Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool to calculate the final price after applying a discount rate to the original price, or to reverse-calculate the discount percentage when you know both the original and sale price. It helps you make informed purchasing decisions by showing exactly how much you save and what the final cost will be.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. 'Calculate discounted price' mode: Enter the original price and discount rate (%) to instantly see the discount amount and final price.
2. 'Reverse calculate discount' mode: Enter both the original price and the discounted price to find the actual discount percentage.
3. For stacked discounts, apply the first discount to get an intermediate price, then enter that as the new original price to apply the second discount.
4. Use the copy button to save results to your clipboard for sharing via message or notes.

items.q: Is a '30% off' deal or a 'buy one get one free' deal better?
items.a: Buy one get one free is effectively a 50% discount. If you plan to buy two items anyway, BOGO wins. If you only need one, 30% off is better. Use the calculator to compare unit costs for each scenario.

items.q: Which is better: a 10% coupon or a flat ₩3,000 coupon?
items.a: At a purchase price of ₩30,000, they are equal. Above ₩30,000 the percentage coupon saves more; below ₩30,000 the flat coupon wins. Run both through the calculator to compare exactly.

items.q: Should I account for VAT when calculating discounts?
items.a: Some online shops display the discount based on the pre-tax price, but the final checkout amount includes 10% VAT. Confirm whether the listed price is tax-inclusive or exclusive to get an accurate final figure.

typingTest.title: Typing Test
typingTest.description: Measure your typing speed and practice Korean/English typing.
typingTest.beginTyping: Look at the text above and start typing

howTo.title: How to Use
items: Select language, difficulty, and duration, then click Start
Type the text displayed on screen exactly as shown
Correctly typed characters appear green, errors appear red
Your typing speed and accuracy are shown when time runs out

speed.title: Speed Reference (WPM)
items: Under 20: Beginner - Basic typing practice needed
20-40: Intermediate - Suitable for general office work
40-70: Advanced - Fast work processing possible
70+: Expert - Professional typing level

whatIs.title: What is the Typing Test?
whatIs.description: A tool to measure your typing speed (WPM or keystrokes per minute) and accuracy in both Korean and English, and to improve through repeated practice. You type the given text as it appears, and the tool records your speed, error rate, and elapsed time so you can track your progress over time.

tips.title: Tips to Improve Your Typing
items: Focus on accuracy before speed. Reducing typos leads to faster long-term improvement than rushing and hitting errors.
Maintain the home row position (Korean: ㅏ·ㅓ keys, English: ASDF·JKL;). Keeping fingers close to the home row reduces travel distance and naturally increases speed.
Practicing 10–15 minutes daily is more effective than a single long session. Muscle memory is built through consistent repetition.

items.q: What is the average Korean typing speed for an adult?
items.a: The average adult types around 200–300 keystrokes per minute (타수). Professionals in office roles typically aim for 350+ keystrokes/min, while fast typists reach 600+ keystrokes/min.

items.q: What is the difference between WPM and keystrokes per minute?
items.a: WPM (Words Per Minute) is an English-centric metric counting words (standardized to 5 characters). Korean typing is traditionally measured in keystrokes per minute. Roughly, 60 WPM in English feels similar in effort to about 300 keystrokes/min in Korean.

items.q: Are my results saved?
items.a: Recent results are automatically saved to your browser's local storage so you can compare them on future visits. No data is sent to any server and no personal information is collected.

numberToKorean.title: Number to Korean
numberToKorean.description: Convert numbers to Korean currency text format. Useful for checks, contracts, and receipts.

usage.title: Use Cases
items: Writing check amounts in Korean (e.g., 금 삼백만원정)
Korean text notation for contracts and receipts
Accurate amount notation in legal documents
Government document preparation with number-Korean parallel notation

rules.title: Korean Amount Notation Rules
items: 'il' (one) can only be omitted at 10,000 units and above
Official documents use the format '금 OOO원정'
Chinese numerals: 壹(1) 貳(2) 參(3) 四(4) 五(5) 六(6) 七(7) 八(8) 九(9) 拾(10)
Financial institutions use Chinese character amounts to prevent forgery

whatIs.title: What is the Number-to-Korean Converter?
whatIs.description: A tool that converts Arabic numerals into Korean Hangul monetary notation (e.g., '금 삼백만 원 정'). Essential for official documents such as checks, contracts, promissory notes, and receipts where amounts must be written in Korean to prevent fraudulent alteration.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the number you want to convert in the input field. Commas are handled automatically.
2. The converted Korean text appears instantly in both colloquial form (e.g., '삼백이십만 오천 원') and formal document form (e.g., '금 삼백이십만 오천 원 정').
3. Click the copy button next to your preferred format to send it to the clipboard.
4. Decimal amounts (e.g., ₩12,500.50) are supported for sub-won denomination notation if needed.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Checks in Korea require the format '일금 ○○원 정'. Use the formal document result directly and paste it into your document.
Contracts conventionally include both numerals and Korean text, e.g., '3,000,000원(삼백만 원)'. This dual notation helps prevent disputes.
Amounts in the hundreds of millions or even trillions are accurately converted, making this tool reliable for high-value documents such as real estate contracts.

items.q: Is there a standardized format for Korean monetary notation?
items.a: The official format is '일금 ○○원 정'. '금 ○○원 정' is also widely accepted. Required formats may vary by document type (check vs. contract), so confirm with the relevant party.

items.q: What is the maximum number that can be converted?
items.a: Conversion is supported up to 9,999조 9,999억 9,999만 9,999원 (quadrillions of won), which covers virtually any practical use case.

items.q: Is Chinese financial notation (壹, 貳, 參 etc.) supported?
items.a: Currently only Korean Hangul notation is supported. For Chinese numeral notation, please use a dedicated tool or convert manually.

qrScanner.title: QR Code Scanner
qrScanner.description: Scan QR codes using your camera or image files.
qrScanner.dragDrop: Drag or click to upload an image

howTo.title: How to Use
items: In the Camera tab, point the QR code at your camera for auto-detection
In the Image tab, upload an image containing a QR code
Detected content is automatically displayed and can be copied
URLs can be opened directly

tips.title: Scanning Tips
items: The QR code should be clear and large enough
Scan in a well-lit environment
Minimize camera shake
Damaged or partially covered QR codes may be difficult to detect

whatIs.title: What is the QR Code Scanner?
whatIs.description: A browser-based tool that reads QR codes and barcodes using your device's camera or an uploaded image file, instantly displaying the encoded information (URL, text, contact info, etc.). No app installation required — works directly in your web browser.

items.q: Is camera permission required?
items.a: Camera permission is required for live scanning. Image file upload works without camera permission and supports screenshots, downloaded images, and gallery photos.

items.q: Can it scan QR codes in screenshots?
items.a: Yes. Use the image upload feature to scan QR codes from screenshots, downloaded images, or photos in your gallery. The image must be clear and the QR code must be fully visible.

items.q: Does it scan regular barcodes too?
items.a: Yes, common 1D barcode formats including Code128, EAN-13, UPC-A, and ITF are supported. Note that barcode scanning is more sensitive to angle and lighting than QR code scanning.

pyeongCalculator.title: Pyeong Calculator
pyeongCalculator.description: Convert between Pyeong (坪) and square meters (m²). Useful for real estate and interior design.

what.title: What is Pyeong?
items: Pyeong (坪) is a traditional area unit used in Korea and Japan
1 Pyeong = approximately 3.3058 m² (exactly 400/121 m²)
Commonly used in real estate and interior design
Officially m² is the standard, but Pyeong is still widely used

reference.title: Apartment Area Reference
items: Exclusive area: Actual usable space (rooms, living room, kitchen)
Supply area: Exclusive area + common residential area
Contract area: Supply area + other common areas (parking, management)
A '25-pyeong apartment' typically means about 82.6 m² in supply area

whatIs.title: What is the Pyeong Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool for converting between pyeong (坪), the traditional Korean area unit, and square meters (m²). Indispensable when browsing Korean real estate listings, planning home renovations, or evaluating apartment sizes. Also explains the difference between supply area (공급면적) and exclusive-use area (전용면적) commonly used in Korean property listings.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Pyeong → m²: Enter the number of pyeong and the equivalent square meters appear instantly. Example: 33 pyeong → 109.09 m².
2. m² → Pyeong: Enter square meters to convert to pyeong. Example: 84 m² → 25.41 pyeong.
3. Adjust the decimal precision setting to get the level of detail you need.
4. Refer to the reference table for common size thresholds: national housing standard (≤85 m²), and small/medium/large categories.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: A '33-pyeong apartment' in listings refers to the supply area. The actual living space (전용면적) is typically 10–20% smaller. Always compare both figures when evaluating an apartment.
A quick mental shortcut: 1 pyeong ≈ 3.3 m². Multiply pyeong × 3.3 for a rough estimate, or use this calculator for precision.
For renovation material estimates, always measure the exclusive-use area (전용면적) and cross-check with physical measurements to minimize waste.

items.q: How many m² is exactly 1 pyeong?
items.a: Exactly 400/121 m² ≈ 3.30579 m². In practice, 3.3058 m² or 3.306 m² are used for convenience.

items.q: What is the difference between supply area and exclusive-use area?
items.a: Exclusive-use area (전용면적) is the total of interior spaces — rooms, living room, kitchen, and bathrooms. Supply area (공급면적) adds each household's share of common spaces such as staircases, corridors, and elevators. Korean real estate ads usually quote the supply area.

items.q: Are other area units like square feet supported?
items.a: Yes. Conversions to and from square feet (ft²), square yards (yd²), and acres are also available, useful when browsing overseas real estate listings.

vatCalculator.title: VAT Calculator
vatCalculator.description: Calculate VAT (10%) from supply amount, or reverse-calculate from total amount.

basic.title: What is VAT?
items: Value Added Tax (VAT) is a tax on the supply of goods and services
Korea's VAT rate is 10%
Supply Amount × 10% = VAT
Total = Supply Amount + VAT

reverse.title: Reverse VAT Calculation
items: From total: Supply Amount = Total ÷ 1.1
From total: VAT = Total ÷ 11
Tax invoices require separate supply amount and VAT
Simplified taxpayers have different VAT rates by industry (1.5%-4%)

whatIs.title: What is the VAT Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool for calculating Korea's 10% value-added tax (VAT). Enter a supply price (pre-tax amount) to get the VAT and total amount, or reverse-calculate the supply price and VAT from a VAT-inclusive total. Ideal for issuing tax invoices, writing business quotes, or reconciling B2B transactions.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. 'Forward' mode: Enter the supply price (ex-VAT) and the 10% VAT and total are calculated automatically.
2. 'Reverse' mode: Enter the VAT-inclusive total to separate it into the supply price and the VAT amount.
3. For multiple line items, calculate each one and use the copy button to transfer the results to a quote or spreadsheet.
4. The copy button outputs the result as 'Supply price / VAT / Total' in a format ready to paste into documents.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Freelancers and sole traders: always state clearly whether your quote is VAT-inclusive or exclusive to avoid misunderstandings with clients.
Simplified taxpayers (연 매출 ₩80M or less) may apply a different effective VAT rate, and some industries are VAT-exempt. Confirm the applicable rate with your tax accountant.
For overseas purchases subject to customs duties and VAT, apply 10% to the taxable base (item price + shipping + insurance) to estimate your import VAT.

items.q: Is VAT 10% of the supply price or 10% of the total?
items.a: VAT is 10% of the supply price (pre-tax amount). As a share of the total price, it works out to approximately 9.09%. Example: supply price ₩1,000,000 → VAT ₩100,000 → total ₩1,100,000.

items.q: Can VAT-exempt businesses use this calculator?
items.a: Yes. Even VAT-exempt businesses may need to track input VAT on purchases. This calculator is useful for understanding the VAT component on invoices you receive.

items.q: How should sub-won amounts be handled on tax invoices?
items.a: Korean tax law generally requires rounding down to the nearest won for supply prices on tax invoices. Drop any decimal amounts before entering the figure on an official tax invoice.

alcoholCalculator.title: Blood Alcohol Calculator
alcoholCalculator.description: Estimate blood alcohol concentration (BAC) using the Widmark formula.
alcoholCalculator.warning: This calculator is for reference only and may differ from actual BAC. Never drive after drinking.

formula.title: Widmark Formula
items: BAC = (Alcohol(ml) × ABV × 0.7894) / (Weight(kg) × Body Water Ratio) - (Hours × 0.015)
Body water ratio: Male 0.68, Female 0.55
Alcohol metabolism rate: approximately 0.015% per hour
This formula provides estimates and individual results may vary

factors.title: Influencing Factors
items: Higher body weight results in lower BAC
Drinking on an empty stomach increases absorption and BAC
Women have lower body water ratio, resulting in higher BAC for same intake
Fatigue and medication can affect alcohol metabolism rate

whatIs.title: What is the Blood Alcohol Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that estimates blood alcohol concentration (BAC) after drinking using the Widmark formula. Enter your body weight, sex, number of drinks, and time of first drink to get an estimated current BAC, a comparison against legal thresholds (0.03% DUI limit, 0.08% high intoxication), and an estimated time until full sobriety.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your sex and enter your body weight (kg). The Widmark distribution factor (r) differs by sex.
2. Choose the drink type (beer/soju/wine/makgeolli, etc.) and number of drinks, or enter the pure alcohol amount (g) directly.
3. Set the time you started drinking to calculate your estimated current BAC.
4. The result shows your estimated BAC, legal status relative to the 0.03% driving limit, and the time until you reach zero BAC.

tips.title: Important Tips
items: This calculator provides an estimate based on average alcohol metabolism. Actual BAC varies significantly with individual health, fasting state, medications, and other factors.
Drinking on an empty stomach speeds alcohol absorption by 2–3×, causing BAC to rise much faster. Eating food and drinking water slows absorption.
Hangover remedies and coffee do not speed up alcohol metabolism. Only time breaks down alcohol — consult the calculator's estimated clearance time before driving.

items.q: What is the estimated BAC after drinking a full bottle of soju?
items.a: For a 70 kg male, one 360 ml bottle of soju (17%) contains about 48 g of pure alcohol. Immediately after drinking, estimated BAC is roughly 0.08–0.09% — above the high intoxication threshold. It takes approximately 4–5 hours to fall below the 0.03% driving limit.

items.q: Can I use this to decide whether it is safe to drive?
items.a: This calculator provides estimates only. Real BAC varies greatly between individuals, and the Widmark formula has a margin of error. Only a certified breathalyzer or blood test can measure BAC accurately. Never drive after drinking.

items.q: Why do women typically get more intoxicated than men from the same amount?
items.a: Women have a lower body water percentage and a smaller Widmark factor r (average 0.55 vs. 0.68 for men). The same amount of alcohol is distributed in less body water, resulting in a higher BAC.

fontPreview.title: Font Preview
fontPreview.description: Preview and compare text in various web fonts.
fontPreview.defaultText: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog 0123456789 다람쥐 헌 쳇바퀴에 타고파

usage.title: How to Use
items: Enter preview text and select your desired font
Adjust font size, weight, line height, and other settings
Use compare mode to view multiple fonts side by side
Copy CSS button lets you copy the style code directly

tips.title: Font Selection Tips
items: Body text: Use readable sans-serif fonts (Noto Sans KR, Pretendard)
Headings: Use bold, impactful fonts (Black Han Sans, Jua)
Coding: Use monospace fonts (JetBrains Mono, D2Coding)
Free web fonts: Available for free on Google Fonts

whatIs.title: What is the Font Preview Tool?
whatIs.description: A tool that lets you apply dozens of web fonts — including Google Fonts — to your own text in real time, adjusting size, weight, letter spacing, and line height to compare them side by side. Web developers, designers, and bloggers can quickly identify the right font for a project without switching between browser tabs or writing any CSS.

items.q: Can I apply a font I've chosen here directly to my website?
items.a: Yes. Copy the CSS snippet shown in the preview. For Google Fonts, add the provided `<link>` tag or `@import` rule to your HTML and set the `font-family` in your CSS.

items.q: Are Korean (Hangul) fonts available?
items.a: Yes. Popular Korean web fonts such as Noto Sans KR, Nanum Gothic, and Pretendard are included. Type Korean text to compare readability across different fonts.

items.q: Can I download font files from this tool?
items.a: Font downloading is not provided here. Visit Google Fonts (fonts.google.com) to download files directly, or obtain fonts from their official source after reviewing the license terms.

worldClock.title: World Clock
worldClock.description: View current time in major cities around the world at a glance.

usage.title: How to Use
items: Major city times are displayed by default
Use the Add City button to add your desired cities
Check time differences between each city and your local time
Switch between 12-hour and 24-hour formats

tips.title: Time Zone Reference
items: Korea (KST) is UTC+9
US Eastern (EST) is 14 hours behind Korea (13 hours during DST)
US Pacific (PST) is 17 hours behind Korea (16 hours during DST)
London (GMT) is 9 hours behind Korea (8 hours during BST)

whatIs.title: What is the World Clock?
whatIs.description: A tool that displays the current local time in major cities around the world in real time, all on one screen. Compare Seoul, New York, London, Tokyo, Sydney, and many more time zones at a glance — ideal for scheduling international meetings, timing calls to friends abroad, or checking stock exchange and crypto trading hours.

items.q: Is Daylight Saving Time (DST) automatically applied?
items.a: Yes. DST rules for each country and region are applied automatically. Countries observing DST (such as the US and Europe) will show times shifted forward by one hour during the applicable months.

items.q: What if the city I need is not in the list?
items.a: You can add a city by searching for its IANA timezone name (e.g., America/Chicago, Asia/Kolkata). Your custom city list is saved in your browser for future visits.

items.q: How is the International Date Line handled?
items.a: Locations east of the Date Line (e.g., Hawaii) are one calendar day behind, while locations to the west (e.g., Fiji) are one day ahead. The date is shown alongside each city's time so the difference is clear.

meetingPlanner.title: Meeting Planner
meetingPlanner.description: Find the overlap window where all selected cities are within working hours.
meetingPlanner.noOverlap: No overlap in working hours across selected cities
meetingPlanner.noCities: Add cities to enable the meeting planner

rentConverter.title: Rent Converter
rentConverter.description: Convert between Jeonse (lump-sum deposit) and monthly rent using conversion rate.
rentConverter.rateInfo: Based on Bank of Korea base rate, typically 4-6%

what.title: What is Rent Conversion?
items: Converting between Jeonse (lump-sum deposit) and monthly rent
Conversion rate is typically 4-6% annually, based on base rate + premium
Legal maximum conversion rate is base rate + 3.5%

example.title: Calculation Example
items: Jeonse 300M → Monthly (deposit 50M, rate 5%): (300M-50M) × 5% ÷ 12 ≈ 1.04M KRW
Monthly 1M (deposit 100M, rate 5%) → Jeonse: 100M + (1M × 12 ÷ 5%) = 340M
Higher conversion rate means higher monthly rent for the same Jeonse amount

whatIs.title: What is the Jeonse/Wolse Conversion Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool for converting between jeonse (전세, lump-sum deposit lease) and wolse (월세, monthly rent) using the legal conversion rate. Compare the true monthly cost of each arrangement — including the opportunity cost of a locked-up deposit — to decide which option is financially better for your situation.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. 'Jeonse → Wolse' mode: Enter the full jeonse deposit and the deposit you want to retain. The remainder is converted to monthly rent at the given conversion rate.
2. 'Wolse → Jeonse' mode: Enter the monthly deposit and monthly rent to calculate the equivalent jeonse amount.
3. Adjust the conversion rate field to use the current market rate or the legal maximum (Bank of Korea base rate + 2%).
4. Review the total monthly outflow comparison (opportunity cost of deposit + monthly rent) to choose the most cost-effective lease type.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The legal cap on the jeonse-to-wolse conversion rate is 'base rate + 2%' (Housing Lease Protection Act). If a landlord demands a higher rate, you have grounds to object.
Don't forget the opportunity cost of a jeonse deposit. A ₩200M deposit at 4% annual interest is ₩670,000/month you're forgoing in deposit interest.
Non-financial factors — jeonse insurance availability, lease fraud risk, and moving costs — should also factor into your jeonse vs. wolse decision.

items.q: What is the jeonse-to-wolse conversion rate?
items.a: The annual interest rate applied when converting part or all of a jeonse deposit into monthly rent. At 5% conversion rate, ₩10M of deposit converts to approximately ₩41,000/month (₩10M × 5% ÷ 12).

items.q: Is a contract that exceeds the legal maximum rate invalid?
items.a: The portion exceeding the legal cap is void. Overpaid monthly rent can be claimed back, so always verify the legal ceiling at the time of signing.

items.q: What is the difference between ban-jeonse and regular monthly rent?
items.a: Ban-jeonse (반전세) combines a high deposit — close to a full jeonse — with a smaller monthly payment. Regular wolse has a much lower deposit and higher monthly rent. Use this calculator to compare the actual monthly cost of both arrangements.

waterBill.title: Water Bill Calculator
waterBill.description: Calculate water bill based on monthly usage (m³) with progressive rates.

structure.title: Bill Structure
items: Basic fee: Fixed amount by pipe diameter (~1,080 KRW for residential 13mm)
Usage fee: Progressive rate based on consumption
Sewage fee: Based on water consumption
Water quality fee: Environmental improvement charge (~170 KRW/m³)

saving.title: Water Saving Tips
items: Install water-saving faucets and toilets to save 30%+
Batch laundry loads, use dishwashers
Reduce shower time (1 minute saves ~12 liters)
Regular leak checks (toilets, faucets)

whatIs.title: What is the Water Bill Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that estimates your monthly water bill by applying the progressive tariff structure of your local municipality based on your monthly water consumption in m³. It accounts for water supply charges, sewage charges, the water usage levy, and VAT to provide a realistic estimate aligned with your actual bill.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your region (city/district) — rates and tier thresholds vary by municipality.
2. Select the usage category (residential/commercial etc.) and enter your monthly consumption (m³).
3. Click Calculate to see the breakdown: water supply, sewage, levy, VAT, and total.
4. Drag the usage slider to see in real time how increasing or decreasing consumption changes your bill.

tips.title: Water-Saving Tips
items: Replacing a standard shower head (10–15 L/min) with a water-efficient model (3–5 L/min) can cut shower water use by more than 50%.
Placing a water-filled plastic bottle in the toilet tank saves about 1–1.5 L per flush — roughly 1–2 m³/month for a family of four.
Always run the washing machine with a full load. Doing one full load uses 30–40% less water than two half-loads, and washing in cold water saves energy too.

items.q: Why does the water bill vary by region?
items.a: Water tariffs are set independently by each local government, so rates and tier thresholds differ by city, county, and district. Select the correct region for an accurate estimate.

items.q: What is the average monthly water use for a family of four?
items.a: The national average for a 4-person household is about 20–25 m³/month. Reducing usage to below 15 m³ through conservation measures can lower your progressive tier and significantly cut costs.

items.q: Why can the sewage charge be higher than the water supply charge in some areas?
items.a: Sewage charges reflect wastewater treatment costs, which in some regions are comparable to or exceed supply costs. The variation depends on local infrastructure and treatment facility expenses.

cssGradient.title: CSS Gradient Generator
cssGradient.description: Generate CSS gradient code by selecting colors and direction.

types.title: Gradient Types
items: Linear: Colors change in one direction
Radial: Colors spread from center outward
Conic: Colors rotate around a center point

tips.title: Usage Tips
items: 2-3 colors produce the most natural gradients
High-contrast color combinations create striking effects
Use background-size and animation for animated effects
Use opacity for overlay effects on images

whatIs.title: What is the CSS Gradient Generator?
whatIs.description: A developer tool that lets you visually configure gradient colors, direction, and type (linear/radial/conic) and instantly generates the corresponding CSS code. No need to memorize complex CSS gradient syntax — design the effect you want through an intuitive UI and copy the ready-to-use code directly into your project.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the gradient type: linear, radial, or conic.
2. Add color stops and set each color (using the HEX, RGB, or HSL color picker) and its position (%).
3. For linear gradients, adjust the angle (0–360°) or click a direction shortcut button (top, bottom, left, right, diagonal).
4. Confirm the result in the live preview box, then click the CSS copy button to save the generated code to your clipboard.

items.q: Will the generated CSS work in all browsers?
items.a: `linear-gradient` and `radial-gradient` are supported in all modern browsers including IE11. `conic-gradient` requires Chrome 69+, Firefox 83+, or Safari 12.1+ and is not supported in IE11.

items.q: Can I create gradients with transparency (alpha)?
items.a: Yes. Use the rgba() color input or the opacity slider in the color picker to add transparent color stops. This is useful for creating semi-transparent overlays on top of images.

items.q: How do I combine a gradient with a background image?
items.a: Use CSS multiple backgrounds: `background: linear-gradient(...), url(image.jpg);` — separate values with a comma. Adapt the generated code to layer a gradient overlay on top of your image.

koreanSyllable.title: Korean Syllable Decomposer
koreanSyllable.description: Decompose Korean text into initial, medial, and final consonants/vowels.

structure.title: Korean Structure
items: Korean syllables consist of initial (consonant) + medial (vowel) + final (consonant)
19 initial consonants: ㄱ ㄲ ㄴ ㄷ ㄸ ㄹ ㅁ ㅂ ㅃ ㅅ ㅆ ㅇ ㅈ ㅉ ㅊ ㅋ ㅌ ㅍ ㅎ
21 medial vowels: ㅏ ㅐ ㅑ ㅒ ㅓ ㅔ ㅕ ㅖ ㅗ ㅘ ㅙ ㅚ ㅛ ㅜ ㅝ ㅞ ㅟ ㅠ ㅡ ㅢ ㅣ
27 final consonants: (none) ㄱ ㄲ ㄳ ㄴ ㄵ ㄶ ㄷ ㄹ ...

usage.title: Use Cases
items: Initial consonant search (e.g., 'ㄷㅎㅁㄱ' → '대한민국')
Korean character education materials
Text analysis and processing
Unicode Korean character testing

whatIs.title: What is the Korean Syllable Decomposer?
whatIs.description: A tool that decomposes Korean (Hangul) text into its constituent jamo — initial consonant (초성), vowel (중성), and final consonant (종성) — or extracts only the initial consonants. Useful for implementing initial-consonant search in apps, generating quiz hints, extracting acronyms, and analyzing Hangul string structure, all based on the Unicode Hangul syllable block decomposition formula.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Type or paste Korean text into the input field.
2. Click 'Extract initials' to get only the leading consonant of each character. Example: '안녕하세요' → 'ㅇㄴㅎㅅㅇ'.
3. Click 'Full decomposition' to split every character into its initial consonant, vowel, and final consonant.
4. Copy the result with the copy button, or export the decomposition as JSON for use in code.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: When building an initial-consonant search feature, use this tool to validate your extraction results and refine your regex patterns.
For initial-consonant quiz games or song lyrics hint features, the extracted initials can be used directly as the clue text.

The decomposition formula for a Hangul codepoint N (where N ≥ 0xAC00): initial = (N − 0xAC00) ÷ 588, vowel = ((N − 0xAC00) ÷ 28) % 21, final = (N − 0xAC00) % 28. Useful when implementing this in code.

items.q: Are standalone jamo characters (ㄱ, ㅏ etc.) handled correctly?
items.a: Yes. Both composed Hangul syllables (가, 나, 다) and standalone consonants (ㄱ, ㄴ) and vowels (ㅏ, ㅑ) are handled. Standalone jamo are already decomposed, so they are passed through as-is.

items.q: What happens to English letters, numbers, and special characters?
items.a: Non-Hangul characters are passed through unchanged in the output. Only Hangul syllables are decomposed; everything else is preserved as-is.

items.q: How do I find all words starting with 'ㄱ' using a regex?
items.a: Korean syllables beginning with ㄱ fall in the Unicode range U+AC00 (가) to U+AE3B (깋). The regex pattern is `/[가-깋]/`. Use the initial extraction results from this tool to determine the correct Unicode range for your target consonant.

romanNumeral.title: Roman Numeral Converter
romanNumeral.description: Convert between Arabic numbers and Roman numerals.
romanNumeral.invalidRoman: Please enter a valid Roman numeral

rules.title: Notation Rules
items: Same symbol can repeat up to 3 times (III=3, XXX=30)
Smaller value before larger means subtraction (IV=4, IX=9, XL=40, XC=90)
Larger value first means addition (VI=6, XI=11, LX=60)
Expressible range: 1 to 3,999

examples.title: Conversion Examples
items: 2024 = MMXXIV (M+M+X+X+IV)
1999 = MCMXCIX (M+CM+XC+IX)
58 = LVIII (L+V+III)
3999 = MMMCMXCIX (maximum value)

whatIs.title: What are Roman Numerals?
whatIs.description: Roman numerals are an ancient numeral system from Rome that uses seven symbols — I, V, X, L, C, D, and M — to represent numbers. They are still widely used today on clock faces, movie release years, legal documents, and chapter numbering. This tool instantly converts between Arabic numbers (1–3999) and Roman numerals in both directions.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter a number (1–3999) in the Arabic numeral field to automatically convert it to Roman numerals.
2. Enter a combination of I, V, X, L, C, D, M in the Roman numeral field to convert back to an Arabic number.
3. Click the copy button next to the result to copy it to your clipboard.
4. If the input is out of range or incorrectly formatted, an error message will be displayed.

tips.title: Tips
items: Use this tool to decode movie release years (e.g., MMXXIV = 2024) or clock numerals instantly.
For lowercase Roman numerals (i, ii, iii) used in book prefaces, convert the uppercase result then switch case manually.
Numbers above 3999 lack a standardized Roman numeral notation, which is why this tool supports up to 3999.

items.q: What is the difference between IV and VI?
items.a: In Roman numerals, placing a smaller value before a larger one indicates subtraction. IV = 5−1 = 4, while VI = 5+1 = 6.

items.q: Is IIII or IV correct for 4?
items.a: IV is the modern standard. However, some clock faces use IIII for historical reasons, so both forms exist in practice.

items.q: Can Roman numerals represent zero?
items.a: No. The Roman numeral system has no symbol for zero. Only numbers from 1 to 3999 can be expressed.

businessNumber.title: Business Registration Number Validator
businessNumber.description: Validate Korean business registration numbers (10 digits). Check if a number is valid using the checksum algorithm.
businessNumber.validMessage: This business registration number passes the checksum validation algorithm.
businessNumber.invalidMessage: This number failed the validation algorithm. Please double-check the number.

structure.title: Number Structure
items: 10 digits total (XXX-XX-XXXXX)
First 3: Regional tax office code
Middle 2: Individual/Corporate code
Last 5: Serial number + check digit (last 1)

validation.title: Validation Method
items: Checksum verification using weight array
Last digit is the check digit
This tool performs format validation only
Check actual business status at NTS (Korea)

whatIs.title: What is the Business Registration Number Validator?
whatIs.description: This tool instantly validates 10-digit Korean business registration numbers using the checksum algorithm defined by the National Tax Service (NTS). It helps prevent transcription errors when writing contracts, issuing tax invoices, or entering supplier information. All validation runs locally in the browser — no data is sent to any server.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the 10-digit business registration number in the input field. Hyphens (-) are automatically ignored.
2. Validation runs instantly as you type using the checksum algorithm.
3. A valid number shows a green check mark; an invalid one shows a red error message.
4. To check multiple numbers, simply clear the field and enter the next one.

tips.title: Tips
items: This tool validates format only. To confirm whether a business actually exists, use the NTS business inquiry service (Hometax).
When pasting numbers from Excel, leading zeros may be dropped — always confirm the number is exactly 10 digits.
Validate supplier numbers before issuing a tax invoice to avoid rejection errors.

items.q: Can a number pass validation but not actually exist?
items.a: Yes. The checksum only confirms the number structure is valid. You must check the NTS Hometax portal to confirm actual registration.

items.q: Does this work for corporation registration numbers too?
items.a: Corporate registration numbers (13 digits) follow a different format. However, business registration numbers (10 digits) — including those for corporations — are validated with the same algorithm.

items.q: Is my data sent to a server?
items.a: No. All validation is performed entirely within your browser and no input is transmitted externally.

dueDateCalculator.title: Due Date Calculator
dueDateCalculator.description: Calculate your estimated due date based on your last menstrual period (LMP) or ovulation date.

calculation.title: Calculation Method
items: Naegele's Rule: LMP + 280 days (40 weeks)
Ovulation-based: Ovulation + 266 days (38 weeks)
Average pregnancy duration: about 280 days
Actual delivery within ±2 weeks is normal

tips.title: Notes
items: Ultrasound can provide more accurate dating
First pregnancies may go past due date
Delivery between 37-42 weeks is normal range
Regular prenatal checkups are essential

whatIs.title: What is the Due Date Calculator?
whatIs.description: The due date calculator uses Naegele's Rule based on the Last Menstrual Period (LMP) or ovulation date to automatically calculate your estimated due date (EDD). It also shows the current gestational week, trimester periods, and key prenatal checkup milestones — all at a glance. Calculating in advance before your first prenatal visit makes consultations smoother.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select or type your last menstrual period (LMP) start date using the date picker.
2. Enter your average cycle length (default 28 days) to refine the due date based on ovulation timing.
3. Click Calculate to see your estimated due date, current gestational week, and trimester dates.
4. Save the result as a shareable link or screenshot to bring to your OB/GYN appointment.

items.q: What is Naegele's Rule?
items.a: It subtracts 3 months from the LMP start date and adds 7 days, assuming a 280-day (40-week) pregnancy. This is the worldwide standard used by obstetricians.

items.q: Can the actual birth date differ from the estimated one?
items.a: Yes. The due date is a statistical average. Births within ±2 weeks are considered normal. Always confirm with an ultrasound scan at your clinic.

items.q: What if my cycle is irregular?
items.a: Enter your average cycle length, or switch to ovulation date input mode for a more accurate result.
