flexboxGrid.title: CSS Flexbox & Grid Generator
flexboxGrid.description: Visually create CSS Flexbox and Grid layouts and copy the code

flexboxGuide.title: Flexbox Basics
items: flex-direction sets the main axis direction (row/column)
justify-content aligns items along the main axis
align-items aligns items along the cross axis
flex-wrap controls whether items wrap to new lines
gap sets spacing between items

gridGuide.title: Grid Basics
items: grid-template-columns defines the column structure
grid-template-rows defines the row structure
fr unit sets proportional sizes
grid-column/grid-row specifies item span
gap sets spacing between cells

tips.title: Usage Tips
items: Flexbox is ideal for 1D layouts (row or column), while Grid excels at 2D layouts (rows and columns)
Select a preset then customize properties to quickly build your desired layout
Use responsive preview to check how it looks on desktop, tablet, and mobile
Copy the generated CSS code and apply it directly to your project

whatIs.title: What is the CSS Flexbox & Grid Generator?
whatIs.description: The CSS Flexbox & Grid Generator is a developer tool that lets you visually design layouts using CSS Flexbox and Grid, generating ready-to-use CSS code in real time. No need to memorize complex property names — use buttons and dropdowns to set up your layout and copy the code directly into your project.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the Flexbox or Grid tab.
2. Configure container properties (flex-direction, justify-content, align-items, grid-template, etc.) using the controls.
3. Add or remove items and adjust individual item properties (flex-grow, grid-column, etc.).
4. Check the live preview on the right and copy the generated CSS code.

items.q: When should I use Flexbox vs Grid?
items.a: Flexbox is best for one-dimensional layouts (a row or a column). Grid excels at two-dimensional layouts (rows and columns simultaneously). Use Flexbox for navbars and card rows; use Grid for full-page layouts.

items.q: Does the generated code work in all browsers?
items.a: Flexbox and Grid are fully supported in all modern browsers. IE11 has partial Flexbox support and requires legacy Grid syntax.

items.q: Can I create responsive Grid layouts?
items.a: Yes. The tool supports auto-fill/auto-fit with minmax() for responsive grids that adapt without media queries.

svgEditor.title: SVG Editor & Optimizer
svgEditor.description: Edit and optimize SVG code. Export to PNG/JPEG supported.

dropzone.title: Drag and drop SVG file or click to upload
dropzone.subtitle: Or paste SVG code directly below

svgEditor.optimizeHint: Select options on the left and run optimization

features.title: Key Features
items: Real-time SVG code editing and preview
Remove unnecessary code: comments, metadata, empty groups
Optimize: shorten hex colors, remove XML declaration
Convert to PNG/JPEG (1x to 4x resolution)
Auto-detect and batch replace colors
Adjust width/height/viewBox dimensions
Drag and drop SVG file upload

tips.title: Optimization Tips
items: Removing comments and metadata alone can reduce size by 20-30%
Color shortening reduces file size with no visual difference
Use 2x resolution for PNG export to support Retina displays
Use color replacement to match icons to your brand colors

whatIs.title: What is the SVG Editor & Optimizer?
whatIs.description: The SVG Editor & Optimizer is a developer tool for editing SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics) code directly and reducing file size by stripping unnecessary metadata and empty elements. A live preview lets you see changes instantly as you edit. You can also export optimized SVG or convert it to PNG or JPEG raster images.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Upload an SVG file or paste SVG code directly into the editor.
2. View the live SVG rendering in the preview panel on the right.
3. Click Optimize to generate a cleaned, smaller SVG.
4. Download the optimized SVG or convert it to PNG/JPEG.

items.q: How much can optimization reduce file size?
items.a: Depending on SVG complexity, exports from Illustrator or Figma can often be reduced by 20–70%. Files with heavy metadata and extra layers benefit the most.

items.q: Is animated SVG supported?
items.a: You can edit CSS or SMIL-based SVG animation code. Whether the animation plays in the preview depends on browser support.

items.q: Can I specify the output dimensions for PNG conversion?
items.a: Yes. Enter a custom width and height before converting to PNG and the output will match your specified dimensions.

apiTester.bodyHint: Enter the request body in JSON format
apiTester.envVarsHint: Use {{variableName}} in URL, headers, and body
apiTester.corsExplanation: Browser security policy (CORS) restricts direct access to some API servers.
corsSuggestions: Route requests through a CORS proxy service
Set the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header on the API server
Use a proxy server during local development

features.title: Key Features
items: Supports GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS methods
Edit request headers and query parameters
JSON body editing with real-time validation
Basic Auth and Bearer Token authentication
Response status code, time, and size analysis
Auto-generate cURL, fetch(), and axios code
Environment variables for reusing API keys

tips.title: Usage Tips
items: Use environment variables to reuse API keys across requests
Click on previous requests in history to reload them
Press Enter to quickly send a request
Content-Type is automatically set when entering a JSON body

cors.title: About CORS
cors.description: This tool runs in the browser, so access to some API servers may be restricted by CORS policy. If you encounter CORS errors, use a proxy server or check the API server's CORS configuration.

whatIs.title: What is the API Tester?
whatIs.description: The API Tester lets you send HTTP requests to REST API endpoints directly from your browser — no separate client installation needed. Configure HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.), request headers, query parameters, and request body (JSON or form data). It provides Postman-like functionality entirely within your browser.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.).
2. Enter the API endpoint URL.
3. In the Headers tab, add headers like Authorization and Content-Type as needed.
4. For POST/PUT requests, switch to the Body tab, enter JSON or form data, then click Send.

items.q: What should I do about CORS errors?
items.a: Browsers block cross-origin API requests under the CORS policy. The server needs to include proper CORS headers, or you can route requests through a CORS proxy in development.

items.q: How do I pass an authentication token?
items.a: In the Headers tab, add 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' for bearer token auth, or use the header name required by your API for API key authentication.

items.q: Are my request histories saved?
items.a: Yes. Recent requests are automatically saved so you can quickly reuse them. Bookmark frequently used requests for even faster access.

budgetCalculator.title: Budget Calculator
budgetCalculator.description: Manage monthly expenses by category and plan your budget

chart.title: Expense Ratio
chart.noData: Enter expense data to see the chart

rule.title: 50/30/20 Rule Analysis
rule.description: Ideally, allocate 50% of income to needs, 30% to wants, and 20% to savings/investments.

rec.needsHigh: Needs exceed 55% of income. Consider reducing housing or communication costs.
rec.wantsHigh: Wants exceed 35% of income. Try cutting back on dining out or leisure spending.
rec.savingLow: Savings rate is below 15%. Consider setting up automatic savings transfers.
rec.good: Your budget aligns well with the 50/30/20 rule. Great job!
rec.overBudget: Total expenses exceed your income. Review all spending categories.

usage.title: How to Use
items: Enter your monthly income (after-tax salary, side income, etc.)
Enter amounts for each expense category or adjust with sliders
Review the donut chart and category bar graphs
Compare with the 50/30/20 rule to find improvements
Save your budget to load later

rule.title: What is the 50/30/20 Rule?
items: Needs (50%): Housing, food, transport, communication, insurance, medical - essential expenses
Wants (30%): Dining out, leisure, shopping, hobbies - discretionary spending
Savings (20%): Savings accounts, investments, emergency fund - building for the future

tips.title: Saving Tips
items: Food expenses should be 15-20% of income
Identify fixed costs (housing, communication) first
Budget for social events by dividing annual total by 12
Aim for at least 20% savings rate
Compare with Korean averages to find areas to cut back

whatIs.title: What is the Budget Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Budget Calculator is a personal finance tool for tracking monthly expenses by category — housing, food, transportation, communication, leisure, and more — to calculate total spending and monitor your budget at a glance. Guided by the 50/30/20 budgeting rule, it helps you understand your spending patterns and identify areas where you can save.

items.q: What is the 50/30/20 rule?
items.a: A simple personal finance guideline: allocate 50% of after-tax income to needs (housing, food, transport), 30% to wants (leisure, shopping), and 20% to savings and debt repayment.

items.q: Will my data be saved between visits?
items.a: Yes. Budget items are stored in browser localStorage, so they persist when you reload the page.

items.q: Can dual-income households calculate combined budgets?
items.a: Yes. Enter the combined household income and all shared household expenses in the respective fields.

loanSchedule.title: Loan Repayment Scheduler
loanSchedule.description: Generate loan repayment schedule and analyze interest
loanSchedule.compareDiffNote: Positive means Loan A pays more; negative means Loan B pays more.

types.title: Repayment Types
items: Equal Payment: Same amount each month, higher interest portion initially
Equal Principal: Equal principal portions, higher initial payments but less total interest
Bullet: Interest-only monthly payments, principal at maturity

tips.title: Tips
items: Minimize grace periods as they increase interest burden
Early repayment can significantly reduce interest
Equal Principal is more favorable in total interest
Recalculate when interest rates change

whatIs.title: What is the Loan Repayment Scheduler?
whatIs.description: The Loan Repayment Scheduler generates a full month-by-month repayment table showing principal, interest, and remaining balance for your loan. It supports equal principal+interest (annuity), equal principal, and deferred repayment structures, and lets you simulate the interest savings from early repayment. Essential for planning mortgages, auto loans, or personal loans.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the loan principal, annual interest rate, and loan term (in months).
2. Select a repayment method (equal principal+interest / equal principal / deferred).
3. Enter the loan start date and payment dates will be generated automatically.
4. Review the monthly schedule showing principal, interest, and balance — export to Excel if needed.

items.q: Which repayment method is better: equal P+I or equal principal?
items.a: Equal principal repayment results in less total interest and decreasing monthly payments but has higher initial payments. Equal P+I offers predictable monthly payments, making budgeting easier.

items.q: How much interest can I save with early repayment?
items.a: Enter a lump-sum early repayment at a specific month to simulate the interest savings and how much the loan term shortens.

items.q: Can it handle variable rate loans?
items.a: The calculator uses a fixed rate. For variable rate loans, recalculate the remaining schedule each time the rate changes.

result.title: Estimated Cost
result.empty: Set options on the left to see estimated costs

checklist.title: Moving Preparation Checklist
items: Compare quotes from at least 3 moving companies
Confirm moving date and time
Prepare address change registration
Apply for mail forwarding
Transfer internet/TV/phone service
Change utilities (electricity/gas/water)
Settle maintenance fees
Sort and dispose of unnecessary items
Separately store valuables and important documents
Prepare cleaning supplies for moving day
Clean and disinfect new home
Notify neighbors about moving (noise courtesy)
Unplug refrigerator 24 hours before move
Pack and secure electronics
Prepare pet transportation

priceRef.title: Base Cost by Size (Full Service)
items: Studio (6p): 300K-500K KRW
Self-Pack is 50-60% of full service cost
Semi Service is 70-80% of full service cost

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips
items: Moving in off-season saves 20-30%
Weekday moves are 10-20% cheaper than weekends
Get quotes from at least 3 companies
Decluttering beforehand reduces costs
Auspicious days have higher demand and prices
Separate AC service companies may be cheaper than movers

whatIs.title: What is the Moving Cost Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Moving Cost Calculator estimates your total moving expenses based on the move type (full-service packing, semi-packing, or basic transport), home size, distance, and timing (peak vs. off-peak season). It incorporates the latest 2025–2026 market rates and includes optional costs for ladder trucks, storage moves, and appliance reinstallation.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your current home size (pyeong or number of rooms) and select the move type.
2. Enter the origin and destination addresses or estimated distance.
3. Choose your preferred moving date — peak season and weekend surcharges apply automatically.
4. Check any add-ons (ladder truck, storage, AC reinstallation, etc.) to see the total estimated cost.

items.q: When is moving peak season?
items.a: March–April (spring) and September–October (fall) are peak seasons, with prices typically 20–30% higher. Month-end and weekends are also in high demand and may include surcharges.

items.q: What's the difference between full-service, semi, and basic moves?
items.a: Full-service includes packing, transport, and unpacking by the movers. Semi-service has movers pack large furniture/appliances only. Basic service covers transport only — you pack everything yourself.

items.q: Will the estimate match the actual quote?
items.a: This calculator uses average market rates as a reference. Actual costs depend on item types, access conditions, and the specific company. Always get at least 3 quotes to compare.

carMaintenance.title: Car Maintenance Calculator
carMaintenance.description: Calculate annual vehicle ownership costs including insurance, tax, fuel, maintenance, and depreciation all at once
carMaintenance.transitComparisonResult: Car maintenance costs approximately {ratio}x of public transit (based on Seoul transit card at 65,000 KRW/month)
carMaintenance.placeholderDescription: Enter vehicle details and cost items in the left panel, then click Calculate to see your annual maintenance costs.

taxSection.title: Korean Auto Tax
items: Non-commercial cars: 80 KRW/cc (up to 1000cc), 140 KRW/cc (up to 1600cc), 200 KRW/cc (over 1600cc)
30% education tax is added
5% annual discount after 3 years of age (max 50%)
Electric vehicles: flat 130,000 KRW/year
Prepay in January or March for ~10% discount

savingsSection.title: Cost Saving Tips
items: Light cars save significantly on tax and insurance
Eco-driving (avoid rapid acceleration/braking) saves 10-20% on fuel
Use insurance comparison sites to find the best rates
Self car wash and self-service fuel save costs
Use Hi-Pass discount time slots for toll savings

maintenanceSection.title: Maintenance Tips
items: Change engine oil every 10,000km or 6 months
Check tire pressure regularly
Inspect brake pad wear limit lines
Replace cabin air filter annually
Check coolant antifreeze ratio before winter

depreciationSection.title: Understanding Depreciation
items: Highest depreciation occurs in the first year (about 20-30%)
Depreciation slows down after 3-5 years
Imported cars depreciate faster than domestic ones
Popular models and white/black colors retain higher resale value
Regular maintenance records increase used car value

whatIs.title: What is the Car Maintenance Cost Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Car Maintenance Cost Calculator computes the total annual and monthly cost of owning a vehicle, covering insurance, vehicle tax, fuel, routine maintenance, and depreciation — all in one place. Enter your car model, year, fuel type, mileage, and fuel efficiency to get a realistic picture of total ownership costs. Useful for car purchase decisions and cost-reduction planning.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter vehicle details: model type, engine displacement (or EV), and model year.
2. Input annual mileage, fuel efficiency (or electricity consumption), and current fuel price.
3. Enter insurance type, estimated premium, and periodic service costs.
4. Review the breakdown of annual costs per category and the per-km cost.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: EVs have lower fuel and maintenance costs, but factor in the higher purchase price and potential battery replacement costs for a full TCO comparison.
Vehicle tax in Korea is charged at ₩180–220 per cc of displacement (approximately ₩520,000/year for a 2,000cc sedan), payable in June and December.
Drivers with under 10,000 km/year can save on insurance premiums with a low-mileage special policy.

items.q: Why is the actual cost higher than expected?
items.a: Commonly overlooked costs like depreciation, car washes, parking fees, highway tolls, tire replacements, and unexpected repairs significantly inflate the real total. This calculator includes these items for a realistic figure.

items.q: Are EVs actually cheaper to own?
items.a: EVs are clearly cheaper in fuel and maintenance, but the higher purchase price, depreciation, and post-warranty battery replacement mean total cost of ownership (TCO) varies by model.

items.q: Does it support business vehicle expense calculations?
items.a: Yes. Business vehicles can deduct up to ₩15 million per year in expenses. Enter fuel usage and mileage to see the deductible amount based on business use percentage.

memoryGame.title: Memory Tile Game
memoryGame.description: Flip cards to find matching pairs!

howToPlay.title: How to Play
items: Click a card to flip it over
If two cards match, they stay face up
If they don't match, they flip back after a short delay
Find all pairs to win!

scoring.title: Scoring System
items: Fewer moves earns you 3 stars
Match pairs consecutively for combo bonuses!
Best scores are saved per difficulty and theme
Time is also tracked - try to be fast!

whatIs.title: What is the Tile Matching Game?
whatIs.description: The Tile Matching Game (Memory Game) is a concentration and memory training game where you flip cards to find matching pairs. Flip two cards at a time — if they match, they're removed; if not, they flip back. The goal is to clear all pairs with as few attempts as possible. Great for improving cognitive skills in players of all ages.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: In the early game, flip cards freely while actively memorizing their positions — that stored knowledge is your greatest asset later.
Try to remember card positions using mental grid coordinates (row, column) for efficient recall.
Gradually increase difficulty to progressively train your working memory.

items.q: Can I change the number of cards (difficulty)?
items.a: Yes. Choose from 4×4 (16 cards), 4×6 (24 cards), or 6×6 (36 cards). Starting with 4×4 is recommended for beginners.

items.q: Is there a time limit?
items.a: Standard mode has no time limit. In Time Attack mode, you race to match as many pairs as possible before time runs out.

items.q: How is the score calculated?
items.a: Fewer attempts yield a higher base score. Consecutive match bonuses and time bonuses are added for the final score.

snakeGame.title: Snake Game
snakeGame.description: Classic snake game - 3 modes, 4 difficulty levels, custom skins!
snakeGame.startHint: Select mode and difficulty, then start

keyboard.title: Keyboard Controls
items: Arrow keys : Change direction
W A S D : Change direction (alternative)
Space / P : Pause/Resume

touch.title: Touch Controls
items: Swipe : Change direction
On-screen D-pad : Tap direction buttons
Center button : Pause/Resume

scoring.title: Scoring System
items: Normal food (red) : +1 point
Bonus food (orange) : +3 points, disappears after a while
Special food (yellow star) : +5 points, disappears after 3 seconds
Accelerating mode: speed increases with score

whatIs.title: What is Snake Game?
whatIs.description: Snake is a classic arcade game where you steer a growing snake to eat food, making it longer and the game progressively harder. Avoid colliding with the walls or your own body. This version offers three game modes (classic, borderless, obstacle), four difficulty levels, and multiple snake skins.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your game mode (classic/borderless/obstacle), difficulty level, and snake skin.
2. Click Start or press an arrow key to begin.
3. Use arrow keys (PC) or swipe gestures (mobile) to steer the snake.
4. Eat the red food to grow and score points — avoid hitting walls or yourself.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: As the snake grows, hugging the walls in a circuit pattern helps avoid collisions.
In borderless mode, passing through a wall lets you exit from the opposite side — use this for quick repositioning.
Plan your route to the food in advance rather than reacting at the last moment.

items.q: Is my high score saved?
items.a: Yes. High scores for each mode and difficulty level are saved in browser localStorage.

items.q: Can I play on mobile?
items.a: Yes. Swipe to change direction, or use the on-screen virtual directional buttons.

items.q: What makes the obstacle mode different?
items.a: Obstacle mode places fixed barriers on the field, requiring more careful path planning. It demands higher concentration than the standard modes.

faviconGenerator.title: Favicon Generator
faviconGenerator.description: Upload an image to generate favicons for all platforms - Web, iOS, Android, and Windows at once

upload.title: Upload Image
upload.dragDrop: Drag & drop or click to upload an image
upload.formats: PNG, JPG, SVG, WebP (512x512 or larger recommended)

result.title: Generated Favicons
result.emptyHint: Upload an image and click 'Generate Favicons'

sizes.title: Favicon Sizes by Platform
items: Web Standard: 16x16, 32x32, 48x48, 64x64, 96x96 (browser tabs, bookmarks)
Apple Touch Icon: 57~180px (iPhone, iPad home screen icons)
Android Chrome: 192x192, 512x512 (home screen icons, splash screens)
Microsoft Tile: 70~310px (Windows Start menu, tiles)
favicon.ico: Multi-size icon (legacy browser compatibility)

tips.title: Favicon Tips
items: Use a square PNG image of 512x512 or larger
Simple designs look better at small sizes
Use transparent background PNGs for any theme compatibility
Apple automatically applies rounded corners, so use square images
Put the downloaded ZIP in a /favicons/ folder and add the HTML code to <head>

whatIs.title: What is the Favicon Generator?
whatIs.description: The Favicon Generator creates all the favicon files needed for a website from a single image — browser tab icons (16×16, 32×32 ICO), iOS home screen icons (180×180 PNG), Android Chrome icons (192×192, 512×512 PNG), and Windows tile images — packaged into a single ZIP download. No design software needed.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Upload the image you want as your favicon (PNG or SVG recommended, minimum 512×512px square).
2. Set background color (transparent, white, etc.) and padding.
3. Select the target platform icon sets (Web / iOS / Android / Windows).
4. Click Generate and download the ZIP file containing all favicon sizes.

items.q: What image size works best?
items.a: A square image of at least 512×512px is ideal. SVG works even better since it's vector-based and scales to any size without loss of quality.

items.q: How do I apply the favicon to my website HTML?
items.a: Place the downloaded files in your project's public folder and add tags like <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico"> and <link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="/apple-touch-icon.png"> to the HTML <head>.

items.q: Is SVG favicon supported?
items.a: Modern browsers support SVG favicons. If you upload an SVG, the original SVG file is included in the package for modern browsers, while ICO files ensure compatibility with older ones.

section1.title: 1. Information We Collect
section1.content: ToolHub collects minimal information necessary to provide our services. Most tools can be used without any personal information collection.
items: Automatically collected: Visit logs, service usage records, device information (browser type, OS)
Cookies: Service settings (dark mode, language, favorites, etc.) are stored in your browser's localStorage and are not sent to our servers
Advertising: Google AdSense may use cookies for ad delivery

section2.title: 2. Purpose of Information Use
items: Service provision and operation
Statistical analysis for service improvement
Advertising (Google AdSense)

section3.title: 3. Data Retention Period
section3.content: ToolHub does not store personal information on servers. User settings are stored only in your browser's localStorage and are deleted when you clear your browser data.

section4.title: 4. Third-Party Sharing
section4.content: ToolHub does not share your personal information with third parties. However, Google's Privacy Policy applies when ads are served through Google AdSense.

section5.title: 5. Cookies and Advertising
section5.content: ToolHub serves ads through Google AdSense, which may use cookies in the process.
items: Google uses cookies for ad delivery
You can disable cookies in your browser settings
To opt out of personalized ads, visit Google Ad Settings (ads.google.com/settings)

section6.title: 6. Your Rights
items: You can delete or disable cookies in your browser settings
You can clear localStorage data to reset all settings
For privacy inquiries, please contact us using the information below

section7.title: 7. Technical Security Measures
items: HTTPS (SSL/TLS) encrypted communication
Client-side processing that avoids storing personal data on servers
Sensitive calculation data is processed only on your device

section8.title: 8. Contact Information
section8.content: For questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at toolhub.ai.kr@gmail.com.

salaryComparison.title: Salary Comparison Calculator
salaryComparison.description: Compare two or more salaries side by side to see differences in social insurance, income tax, and take-home pay at a glance.

usage.title: How to Use
items: Enter your current salary in Scenario A and a job offer salary in Scenario B.
Set different non-taxable allowances if they differ between companies.
Add up to 4 scenarios to compare multiple options at once.
Copy the comparison result to share via messenger or email.

tips.title: Comparison Tips
items: Companies with more non-taxable benefits yield higher take-home pay even at the same salary.
Consider non-taxable allowances (meal: 200K KRW, vehicle maintenance) during salary negotiation.
More dependents reduce income tax and increase take-home pay.
Due to progressive tax rates, the take-home increase rate decreases as salary rises.

standard.title: 2025 Deduction Standards
items: National Pension: 4.5% (cap: 2.43M KRW/month)
Health Insurance: 3.545%
Long-term Care: 12.95% of Health Insurance
Employment Insurance: 0.9%
Income Tax: 6-45% progressive rates
Local Income Tax: 10% of Income Tax

whatIs.title: What is the Salary Comparison Tool?
whatIs.description: The Salary Comparison Tool lets you compare two or more salary conditions side by side, showing the exact difference in national pension, health insurance, employment insurance, and income tax deductions — and the resulting monthly take-home pay. When negotiating a job offer or comparing compensation packages, this tool shows you exactly how much pre-tax salary differences translate to in actual monthly income.

items.q: How much does a ₩5M salary difference translate to in take-home pay?
items.a: It varies by bracket, but for mid-range salaries (₩30M–50M), a ₩5M annual difference typically means about ₩300,000–400,000 more per month after taxes. The difference changes near tax bracket thresholds.

items.q: How are non-taxable items handled?
items.a: You can enter non-taxable allowances (e.g., meal allowance up to ₩200,000/month, commuting allowance up to ₩200,000/month) to reduce the taxable income and increase take-home pay.

items.q: Does the number of dependents affect the tax?
items.a: Yes. Enter the number of dependents to apply personal deductions. More dependents result in lower income tax and higher take-home pay.

yamlJsonConverter.title: YAML ↔ JSON Converter
yamlJsonConverter.description: Convert between YAML and JSON in real time. Supports file upload, download, and key sorting.
yamlJsonConverter.yamlPlaceholder:
Enter YAML here...

Example:
name: John
age: 30
hobbies:
  - reading
  - coding

features.title: Key Features
items: Bidirectional real-time conversion: YAML → JSON and JSON → YAML
File upload (.json, .yaml, .yml) and result download
Indent (2/4/8 spaces) and key sorting (A-Z) options
Block/Flow style selection for JSON → YAML conversion
Copy conversion result to clipboard

useCases.title: Use Cases
items: Convert Kubernetes manifests (Deployment, Service, ConfigMap) between formats
Convert Docker Compose YAML to JSON for API requests
Edit GitHub Actions and GitLab CI/CD workflow files
Convert Ansible playbooks and Terraform configuration files
Convert Swagger/OpenAPI spec files between formats

yamlTips.title: YAML Tips
items: Always use spaces for indentation (tabs are not allowed)
Wrap strings containing special characters (: , # etc.) in quotes
Arrays start with - (dash + space)
Use | (literal) or > (folded) blocks for multi-line strings
Use anchors (&) and aliases (*) to reduce data duplication

whatIs.title: What is the YAML ↔ JSON Converter?
whatIs.description: The YAML ↔ JSON Converter is a developer tool that transforms YAML and JSON between each other in real time. Configuration files for tools like Kubernetes, Docker Compose, and GitHub Actions use YAML, while API responses use JSON — this tool bridges the gap seamlessly. File upload/download and key sorting are also supported.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Paste your YAML or JSON text into the input field, or upload a file.
2. Select the conversion direction (YAML → JSON or JSON → YAML).
3. Optionally enable 'Sort Keys' to receive alphabetically ordered output.
4. Copy the converted result or download it as a file.

items.q: What happens to YAML comments when converting to JSON?
items.a: JSON does not support comments, so all YAML comments (lines starting with #) are removed during conversion. They cannot be recovered if you convert back to YAML.

items.q: Are nested structures and arrays converted correctly?
items.a: Yes. YAML's indentation-based nesting and array notation (dash syntax) are accurately converted to JSON objects and arrays.

items.q: Are YAML anchors (&) and aliases (*) handled?
items.a: Yes. Anchors and aliases are resolved to their actual values before conversion, since JSON has no equivalent concept.

inheritanceGiftTax.title: Inheritance & Gift Tax Calculator
inheritanceGiftTax.description: Easily calculate Korean inheritance and gift tax based on 2025 rates. View detailed breakdown of all deductions.
inheritanceGiftTax.funeralHint: Up to 15M KRW deductible (10M direct + 5M cremation)
inheritanceGiftTax.financialHint: Financial assets - financial debts (up to 200M KRW deductible)
inheritanceGiftTax.useItemized: Use itemized deduction (200M basic + personal deduction)
inheritanceGiftTax.giftPeriodNote: Gift tax deductions are based on cumulative gifts from the same person over 10 years.
inheritanceGiftTax.inputPrompt: Enter an amount on the left to see the calculation result.
inheritanceGiftTax.disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Actual taxes may vary depending on real estate valuation, prior gifts aggregation, generation-skipping surcharge, and other factors. Please consult a tax professional for accurate calculations.

inheritance.title: Inheritance Tax Deductions
items: The larger of the lump-sum deduction (500M) or itemized deduction (200M basic + personal) is automatically applied.
If a spouse exists, a minimum spouse deduction of 500M (up to 3B) is added.
With a spouse, up to 1B KRW (500M lump-sum + 500M spouse) is inheritance tax-free.
20% of net financial assets (min 20M, max 200M) is deducted as financial asset deduction.
Funeral expenses are deductible up to 15M KRW (10M direct costs + 5M cremation facility).

gift.title: Gift Tax Deductions
items: From spouse: 600M KRW deduction over 10 years
From parents to adult children: 50M KRW deduction over 10 years
From parents to minor children: 20M KRW deduction over 10 years
From children (direct descendants): 50M KRW deduction over 10 years
Since 2024, an additional 100M KRW deduction for marriage/childbirth gifts from direct ascendants (separate limit)

tips.title: Tax Saving Tips
items: Utilize the 10-year gift deduction cycle to significantly reduce taxes over the long term.
Start gifting to children early for compound growth benefits and tax savings simultaneously.
Use spousal gifts (600M deduction) to distribute assets before inheritance for potential savings.
Real estate is assessed at official price rather than market value, which may affect the actual burden.

whatIs.title: What is the Inheritance & Gift Tax Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Inheritance & Gift Tax Calculator computes Korean inheritance tax (상속세) and gift tax (증여세) based on 2025 tax law. It applies available deductions — basic deduction, spouse deduction, dependent deduction, and financial asset deduction — to calculate the final tax liability. It also helps you understand the tax rate structure and legitimate tax-reduction strategies.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the Inheritance Tax or Gift Tax tab.
2. Enter the total inherited or gifted asset value (broken down by real estate, financial assets, and other assets if applicable).
3. Check applicable deductions (spouse deduction, child deduction, financial asset deduction, etc.) and confirm the deductible amounts.
4. The taxable base, applicable tax rate, and final payable tax (after 3% filing deduction) are calculated automatically.

items.q: What is the inheritance tax filing deadline?
items.a: Inheritance tax must be filed and paid within 6 months from the end of the month in which the death occurred. If the deceased or heirs lived abroad, the deadline extends to 9 months.

items.q: What are the gift tax exemption limits?
items.a: Over a 10-year period: ₩600M from a spouse, ₩50M (adults) or ₩20M (minors) from lineal ascendants (parents/grandparents), ₩50M to lineal descendants (children), and ₩10M from other relatives.

items.q: Can inheritance and gift taxes overlap?
items.a: Yes. Gifts made to heirs within 10 years before death are added back into the taxable estate for inheritance tax purposes. Any gift tax already paid on those amounts is credited against the inheritance tax.

freelancerTax.title: Freelancer Tax Calculator
freelancerTax.description: Compare 3.3% withholding tax with actual income tax to calculate expected refund or additional payment.
freelancerTax.dependentHint: Include self. Spouse, children under 20, parents over 60, etc.
freelancerTax.healthHint: Regional health insurance + long-term care insurance
freelancerTax.inputPrompt: Enter your annual revenue on the left to see the tax calculation.
freelancerTax.disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Actual taxes may vary depending on other income, tax credits, penalties, etc. Please consult a tax professional for accurate filing.

withholding.title: What is 3.3% Withholding?
items: When freelancers receive payment, 3% income tax + 0.3% local tax = 3.3% is withheld at source.
This is a 'prepaid tax' that gets settled during the annual tax filing in May.
If withholding > actual tax, you get a refund. If less, you pay the difference.
Income received without withholding must also be reported in the annual filing.

expenseRates.title: Expense Rate Selection Guide
items: Simple rate: Available if prior year revenue was under 24M KRW (non-professionals).
Standard rate: Applied to businesses above a certain size or those required to keep double-entry books.
Actual expenses: Enter real expenses with supporting documents for more accurate calculation.
Rates vary significantly by industry and are based on National Tax Service standards.

tips.title: Tax Saving Tips
items: Keep receipts for business expenses (transport, communication, equipment) to help reduce taxes.
National pension and health insurance are fully deductible - always include them in your filing.
If annual revenue exceeds 48M KRW, consider double-entry bookkeeping even if not required.
Freelancers can get up to 7M KRW tax credit through IRP (Individual Retirement Pension) contributions.

whatIs.title: What is the Freelancer Tax Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that calculates your actual comprehensive income tax from 3.3% withholding income, helping you predict whether you'll receive a refund or owe additional tax at year-end. It supports both standard expense rates (by industry) and actual documented expenses, so you can compare tax strategies. Use it before filing to avoid surprise tax bills.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your total annual income (before withholding).
2. Select the expense input method — standard expense rate (auto by industry), standard flat rate, or enter actual expenses directly.
3. Enter personal deductions: number of dependents, spouse deduction, etc.
4. Click 'Calculate' to see estimated income tax, withheld amount (3.3%), and net refund or additional payment.

items.q: What is the 3.3% withholding tax?
items.a: It's a combined withholding rate of 3% income tax + 0.3% local income tax. The client (payer) deducts this before paying you.

items.q: Which is better: standard expense rate or actual expenses?
items.a: If your income is modest and actual expenses are low, the standard rate is easier. If you have high verifiable expenses, claiming actual costs often reduces your tax more. Use this calculator to compare both scenarios.

items.q: Do I have to file a comprehensive income tax return in May?
items.a: Yes. Freelancers with income must file a comprehensive income tax return in May each year, even if tax was already withheld. The May filing is the final reconciliation.

subnetCalculator.title: IP Subnet Calculator
subnetCalculator.description: Calculate IPv4 subnets easily. Check CIDR, subnet mask, and host range.
subnetCalculator.inputPrompt: Enter an IP address and CIDR prefix to see the subnet details.
subnetCalculator.note: This calculator supports IPv4 only. IPv6 subnet calculation is not supported.
subnetCalculator.overlapDesc: Enter multiple CIDR ranges to check if any overlap with each other.
subnetCalculator.lookupIpDesc: Enter one IP address per line.
subnetCalculator.lookupPrompt: Enter CIDR ranges and IP addresses.

basics.title: Subnet Basics
items: A subnet divides a larger network into smaller segments.
The subnet mask separates the network portion from the host portion of an IP address.
The network address is the first address in the subnet (all host bits are 0).
The broadcast address is the last address in the subnet (all host bits are 1).
Usable hosts = total addresses - 2 (network + broadcast)

cidr.title: CIDR Notation
items: /24 = subnet mask 255.255.255.0 (254 hosts)
/8 = subnet mask 255.0.0.0 (~16.7 million hosts)
Higher numbers mean smaller networks; lower numbers mean larger networks.

whatIs.title: What is the IP Subnet Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that takes an IPv4 address in CIDR notation or subnet mask form and automatically calculates the network address, broadcast address, usable host IP range, and total host count. It's used by network engineers, server admins, and students to quickly validate IP designs and subnetting. Also useful when configuring AWS VPC CIDR blocks or firewall rules.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter an IP address (e.g., 192.168.1.0).
2. Select a CIDR prefix length (e.g., /24) or enter a subnet mask (e.g., 255.255.255.0) directly.
3. Click 'Calculate' to see the network address, broadcast address, and usable host range.
4. For subnet splitting, increase the CIDR value to see a list of subnets.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: /24 gives 256 IPs (254 usable), /16 gives 65,536 IPs — commonly used in cloud VPC design.
Always verify CIDR rules before entering them in AWS or GCP security groups to avoid unintended IP access.
/26 (62 hosts) or /27 (30 hosts) are typical for per-department VLAN segmentation.

items.q: What is CIDR?
items.a: CIDR stands for Classless Inter-Domain Routing. It represents an IP address and network size in the format '192.168.1.0/24'. A larger number after the slash means a smaller network.

items.q: Can I use the network or broadcast address for hosts?
items.a: No. The first IP (network address) and last IP (broadcast) in each subnet cannot be assigned to hosts. The usable host count is total IPs minus 2.

items.q: Does this support IPv6?
items.a: Currently only IPv4 is supported. For IPv6 subnetting, use a dedicated tool or refer to RFC 4291.

items.q: What is CIDR?
items.a: CIDR stands for Classless Inter-Domain Routing. It represents an IP address and network size in the format '192.168.1.0/24'. A larger number after the slash means a smaller network.

items.a: No. The first IP (network address) and last IP (broadcast) in each subnet cannot be assigned to hosts. The usable host count is total IPs minus 2.

htmlEntityConverter.title: HTML Entity Converter
htmlEntityConverter.description: Encode HTML special characters to entity codes or decode entities back to original characters.

what.title: What are HTML Entities?
items: HTML entities are escape codes used to safely represent special characters in HTML.
Characters like & < > " ' must be encoded as they conflict with HTML syntax.
Encoding user input is critical for preventing XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) attacks.

formats.title: Encoding Format Comparison
items: Named: © — Human-readable but only supports predefined characters
Decimal: © — Unicode code point in decimal, supports all characters
Hex: © — Unicode code point in hexadecimal, supports all characters
Named format only covers about 250 predefined entities.

usage.title: Usage Tips
items: Encoding is needed when embedding special characters in JavaScript or CSS within HTML.
Named entities have the best compatibility in HTML email templates.
When embedding HTML in JSON, also escape " and \ characters.
In RSS/XML feeds, & < > " ' are the 5 mandatory encoding targets.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the conversion direction — 'Characters → Entities (encode)' or 'Entities → Characters (decode)'.
2. Paste the text you want to convert into the input field.
3. Check the output field and use the copy button to save to clipboard.
4. Use 'All characters' or 'Special characters only' options to adjust the conversion scope.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Always encode user-supplied content before inserting it into HTML templates to prevent XSS attacks.
The 5 characters <, >, &, ", and ' must always be escaped as HTML entities.
Converting special characters to entities in HTML email bodies improves compatibility across email clients.

items.q: How is HTML entity encoding different from URL encoding?
items.a: HTML entities (e.g., &) represent special characters within HTML documents. URL encoding (e.g., %26) safely encodes special characters in URLs. They serve different contexts.

items.q: What's the difference between numeric (&#number;) and named (&) entities?
items.a: Both work the same way. Named entities (e.g., &) are more readable. Numeric entities (e.g., &) can represent any Unicode character, useful when no named entity exists.

items.q: Can Korean characters be converted to entities?
items.a: Yes, Korean characters can be expressed as numeric Unicode entities (e.g., 한). However, with modern UTF-8 encoding being standard, converting Korean to entities is rarely necessary.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the conversion direction — 'Characters → Entities (encode)' or 'Entities → Characters (decode)'.
2. Paste the text you want to convert into the input field.
3. Check the output field and use the copy button to save to clipboard.
4. Use 'All characters' or 'Special characters only' options to adjust the conversion scope.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Always encode user-supplied content before inserting it into HTML templates to prevent XSS attacks.

items.q: How is HTML entity encoding different from URL encoding?

items.q: What's the difference between numeric (&#number;) and named (&) entities?
items.a: Both work the same way. Named entities (e.g., &) are more readable. Numeric entities (e.g., &) can represent any Unicode character, useful when no named entity exists.

exerciseCalorie.title: Exercise Calorie Calculator
exerciseCalorie.description: Calculate calories burned during exercise based on MET (Metabolic Equivalent of Task).
exerciseCalorie.metDescription: MET (Metabolic Equivalent of Task) measures exercise intensity. Resting energy expenditure = 1 MET. Calories = MET × weight(kg) × time(h). Source: Compendium of Physical Activities.
exerciseCalorie.inputPrompt: Enter your weight and add exercises to calculate calories burned.

formula.title: Calorie Formula
items: Calories (kcal) = MET × weight (kg) × time (hours)
Example: 70kg, 30min jogging (MET 7.0) → 7.0 × 70 × 0.5 = 245kcal
1kg body fat ≈ 7,700 kcal (basis for fat conversion)
1 bowl of rice ≈ 300 kcal

tips.title: Effective Exercise Tips
items: High-intensity exercises (running, jump rope) burn more calories in the same time.
Strength training increases metabolic rate even after exercise (EPOC effect).
Combining cardio and strength training is effective for weight loss and muscle maintenance.
Fasted cardio may help fat burning, but high intensity risks muscle loss.

whatIs.title: What is the Exercise Calorie Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that calculates precise calorie burn by inputting your body weight and exercise duration, using the MET (Metabolic Equivalent of Task) formula. It supports 40+ exercises including walking, running, swimming, and cycling, and accounts for how heavier individuals burn more calories for the same activity. Use it for diet planning or tracking daily activity levels.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your body weight (kg) — heavier people burn more calories for the same exercise.
2. Select or search for the exercise type (e.g., walking, running, swimming, yoga).
3. Enter the duration in minutes.
4. View the calorie burn result and the estimated fat burned (in grams).

items.q: What is a MET value?
items.a: MET stands for Metabolic Equivalent of Task. Resting energy expenditure (3.5 ml O₂/kg/min) is defined as 1 MET. Light walking is about MET 3.5; running at 10 km/h is about MET 10.

items.q: Why does the result differ from my smartwatch?
items.a: Smartwatches incorporate heart rate, age, gender, and proprietary algorithms. MET-based calculations are standardized estimates with ±10–15% variance.

items.q: How much exercise is needed to burn 1 kg of fat?
items.a: 1 kg of fat equals approximately 7,700 kcal. For example, a 70 kg person running (burning ~6 kcal/min) would need about 21 hours. Combining exercise with dietary control is far more efficient.

whatIs.title: What is the Exercise Calorie Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that calculates precise calorie burn by inputting your body weight and exercise duration, using the MET (Metabolic Equivalent of Task) formula. It supports 40+ exercises including walking, running, swimming, and cycling, and accounts for how heavier individuals burn more calories for the same activity. Use it for diet planning or tracking daily activity levels.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your body weight (kg) — heavier people burn more calories for the same exercise.
2. Select or search for the exercise type (e.g., walking, running, swimming, yoga).
3. Enter the duration in minutes.
4. View the calorie burn result and the estimated fat burned (in grams).

items.q: What is a MET value?
items.a: MET stands for Metabolic Equivalent of Task. Resting energy expenditure (3.5 ml O₂/kg/min) is defined as 1 MET. Light walking is about MET 3.5; running at 10 km/h is about MET 10.

items.q: Why does the result differ from my smartwatch?
items.a: Smartwatches incorporate heart rate, age, gender, and proprietary algorithms. MET-based calculations are standardized estimates with ±10–15% variance.

items.q: How much exercise is needed to burn 1 kg of fat?
items.a: 1 kg of fat equals approximately 7,700 kcal. For example, a 70 kg person running (burning ~6 kcal/min) would need about 21 hours. Combining exercise with dietary control is far more efficient.

noiseMeter.title: Noise Meter
noiseMeter.description: Measure ambient noise levels in real-time using your microphone. Displayed in dB (decibels).
noiseMeter.micPermissionDenied: Microphone access was denied. Please allow microphone permission in your browser settings.
noiseMeter.micError: Cannot access the microphone. Please check if a microphone is connected.

how.title: How to Use
items: Press the Start button to request microphone access.
Once permission is granted, noise levels are measured in real-time.
View max, average, and min dB statistics.
The history graph shows noise level changes over time.

tips.title: Tips for Accurate Measurement
items: Removing your phone case may improve microphone sensitivity.
Measurement may be inaccurate in windy environments.
Point the microphone toward the noise source for better accuracy.
Values are for reference only and cannot replace professional sound meters.

whatIs.title: What is the Noise Meter?
whatIs.description: A tool that measures ambient noise levels in real-time in decibels (dB) using your smartphone or PC microphone. No app installation required — works directly in the browser. Use it for checking everyday noise levels, assessing work environments, measuring floor-noise complaints, or monitoring background noise in music practice rooms, libraries, and offices.

items.q: Is microphone permission required?
items.a: Yes, microphone access is needed for measurement. Click 'Allow' when the browser requests permission. You can revoke the permission in browser settings after use.

items.q: Can the result differ from a professional sound level meter?
items.a: Yes. Smartphone microphones are less precise than professional meters and have different frequency response characteristics. Use this tool for relative noise assessment and everyday checks. For legally required measurements, use calibrated professional equipment.

items.q: What dB level counts as noisy?
items.a: Below 40 dB is quiet (library-like); 60 dB is normal conversation; 80 dB is a loud office; 90 dB+ risks hearing damage. Korean apartment noise standards are 43 dB (daytime) / 38 dB (nighttime).

spiritLevel.title: Spirit Level & Compass
spiritLevel.description: Measure tilt and direction in real-time using your smartphone sensors.
spiritLevel.permissionDenied: Sensor access was denied. Please allow sensor permission in your browser settings.
spiritLevel.noSensor: Gyroscope/accelerometer sensor not detected on this device. Please use a smartphone.
spiritLevel.sensorError: Cannot access sensors. Please try on a smartphone.
spiritLevel.iosHint: On iOS, enable Settings > Safari > Motion & Orientation Access.
spiritLevel.startHint: Press Start to activate device sensors.

level.title: Spirit Level Usage
items: Place your smartphone on the surface you want to measure.
When the green circle is centered, the surface is level.
Left/right and front/back tilt angles are shown in real-time.
Use the lock button to freeze the current reading.

compass.title: Compass Usage
items: Hold your smartphone horizontally.
The red needle points north.
Heading angle and direction are shown below.
Keep away from magnets and electronics for accuracy.

whatIs.title: What is the Spirit Level & Compass?
whatIs.description: A tool that uses your smartphone's built-in gyroscope, accelerometer, and magnetometer to measure tilt angle (level/plumb) and compass bearing in real-time. No separate tool needed — just use your smartphone for everyday DIY tasks like assembling furniture, hanging picture frames, installing shelves, or leveling a camera.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Place your smartphone flat and the tilt angle is shown in real-time.
2. When perfectly level, the bubble in the center turns green.
3. Switch to the compass tab to view current bearing (N/S/E/W) and angle.
4. If calibration is needed, slowly move the phone in a figure-8 motion.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Keep the compass away from metal objects and electronics for more accurate bearing readings.
Thick phone cases or cases with magnets may reduce compass accuracy.
Use the level while taking photos to get a straight horizon without tilted shots.

items.q: Does it work on both iOS and Android?
items.a: Yes, it works on most smartphones with a gyroscope and magnetometer. Some budget devices may lack sensors, limiting compass functionality.

items.q: How accurate is the level reading?
items.a: Typically ±1–2 degrees depending on the device. Sufficient for DIY tasks; use a professional laser level for precision construction work.

items.q: What if the sensor gives erratic readings?
items.a: Refresh the page or place the phone on a flat surface and restart. For compass errors, calibrate by slowly rotating the phone in a figure-8 pattern.

metronome.title: Metronome
metronome.description: Practice with an online metronome. BPM control, time signatures, tap tempo, vibration support.

how.title: How to Use
items: Press the play button to start the metronome.
Use +/- buttons or the slider to adjust BPM.
Tap the Tap Tempo button rhythmically to auto-detect BPM.
Select a time signature (2/4, 3/4, 4/4, 6/8) to change the beat pattern.
Enable vibration feedback on mobile devices for haptic beats.

tempos.title: Classical Tempo Markings
items: Grave: ~40 BPM - Very slow, solemn
Largo: ~50 BPM - Slow, broad
Adagio: ~66 BPM - Slow, comfortable
Andante: ~76 BPM - Walking pace
Moderato: ~108 BPM - Moderate speed
Allegro: ~132 BPM - Fast, lively
Vivace: ~160 BPM - Lively, fast
Presto: ~184 BPM - Very fast

whatIs.title: What is the Metronome?
whatIs.description: An online metronome that provides a steady beat for music practice. Adjust BPM (beats per minute), set time signatures like 4/4 or 3/4, and use the tap function to measure the tempo of any song. Works for all instruments — piano, guitar, drums, voice, and dance practice. No installation required; runs directly in your browser.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: When learning a new piece, start at 70–80% of your target BPM and gradually increase as accuracy improves.
Use the tap tempo feature to measure the BPM of any song you want to play along with.
Use accent settings to emphasize backbeats (beats 2 and 4) for a more natural groove feel.

items.q: The metronome seems to drift — is this normal?
items.a: With many open browser tabs or high system load, timing can become slightly irregular. Close unnecessary tabs or enable vibration mode for a more consistent beat feel.

items.q: What BPM range is supported?
items.a: Generally 20 BPM (very slow Largo) to 240 BPM (very fast Prestissimo).

items.q: What are the BPM ranges for classical tempo markings?
items.a: Largo (40–60), Adagio (66–76), Andante (76–108), Moderato (108–120), Allegro (120–156), Presto (168–200) are the commonly accepted BPM ranges.

whiteNoise.title: White Noise Generator
whiteNoise.description: White noise generator for sleep and focus. 6 noise types with sleep timer.

typeDesc.white: Equal energy across all frequencies. Great for focus and concentration.
typeDesc.pink: Stronger low frequencies for a natural feel. Helps with deep sleep.
typeDesc.brown: Deep, rumbling low tones. Ideal for meditation and relaxation.
typeDesc.rain: Gentle rain sound effect. Popular for ASMR and sleep.
typeDesc.wind: Soft wind sounds. Creates a natural, relaxing atmosphere.
typeDesc.wave: Rhythmic ocean wave sounds. Recreates the calm of a beach.

benefits.title: Benefits of White Noise
items: Masks ambient noise to improve sleep quality
Helps improve focus and productivity
Used for baby sleep and tinnitus relief
Suitable as background sound for meditation, yoga, and relaxation

tips.title: Tips for Effective Use
items: Pink/brown noise is more effective for sleep than white noise.
Keep volume below conversation level (~60dB).
Set a sleep timer to avoid playing all night.
Speakers provide a more natural feel than earphones.

whatIs.title: What is the White Noise Generator?
whatIs.description: A browser-based tool that generates background sounds to aid sleep, focus, and meditation. It offers white noise along with brown noise, rain, waves, and other nature sounds. A sleep timer lets it stop automatically after a set period. Using earphones enhances the ambient noise-masking effect.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your preferred sound type (white noise, brown noise, rain, ocean waves, etc.).
2. Adjust the volume slider to a comfortable level.
3. For sleep use, set a timer for auto-stop.
4. Press play. Using earphones is recommended for best results.

items.q: What's the difference between white, pink, and brown noise?
items.a: White noise has equal energy across all frequencies (like TV static). Pink noise emphasizes lower frequencies naturally (wind, waterfall). Brown noise is deeper and bass-heavy (thunder, ocean waves).

items.q: Which noise type is best for sleep?
items.a: Individual preference varies, but research suggests brown noise and rain sounds tend to be effective for sleep induction. Try different types to find what works best for you.

items.q: Is long-term use safe for hearing?
items.a: At moderate volume (below 60–70 dB) it's safe. Keep the volume low, especially with earphones. Prolonged exposure above 85 dB carries a risk of hearing damage.

pensionCalculator.title: National Pension Calculator
pensionCalculator.description: Estimate your Korean National Pension (NPS) monthly benefit, total contributions, and income replacement rate.
pensionCalculator.notice: This calculator provides estimates based on 2024 Korean NPS guidelines. Actual benefits may vary based on contribution history, credits, and other factors.

formula.title: Pension Calculation Formula
items: Basic Pension = 1.2 × (A + B) × (contribution months / 480)
A value = average income of all subscribers in the 3 years before pension eligibility (~2.86M KRW in 2024)
B value = average standard monthly income during your contribution period
5% additional payment for every 12 months beyond 20 years of contributions
This calculator assumes your current income remains constant throughout

contribution.title: Contribution Rate Guide
items: Total contribution rate: 9% of income
Employee share: 4.5%
Employer share: 4.5%
Self-employed pay the full 9%
2024 upper income limit: 6.17M KRW per month

tips.title: How to Maximize Your Pension
items: Longer contribution periods increase your benefit (minimum 10 years, up to 40 years)
Voluntary or continued voluntary enrollment can extend your contribution period
Use the retroactive payment system to pay premiums for exemption periods
Military service, childbirth, and unemployment credits add to your contribution period
Higher income increases your B value, but the upper income limit applies

whatIs.title: What is the National Pension Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that estimates your expected monthly National Pension (국민연금) benefit based on your contribution history and current income. Compare early withdrawal (reduced), normal-age, and deferred (increased) payout options, and view the income replacement rate relative to contributions. Use it to plan your retirement finances.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your current age and enrollment start year (or total contribution period).
2. Enter your current monthly salary (standard monthly income).
3. Set your expected retirement age or end of contributions.
4. Compare projected monthly benefits for normal, early (reduced), and deferred (increased) payout options.

items.q: Why is the pension age rising?
items.a: Those born before 1953 receive benefits from age 60. Those born 1969 or later receive benefits from age 65, with gradual phase-in in between. This is a financial stability measure due to aging demographics.

items.q: How much is deducted for early pension?
items.a: Each year you claim early, benefits are permanently reduced by 6%. Claiming 5 years early means a 30% reduction for life.

items.q: What happens if I stop paying?
items.a: Your eventual benefit decreases. You must contribute for at least 10 years (120 months) to receive pension payments. Otherwise, a lump-sum refund is given.

pomodoroTimer.notificationBreakDone: Break over! Time to focus again.
pomodoroTimer.taskEmpty: No tasks yet. Add one above.

Set the timer to 25 minutes and start.
Work on the task until the timer rings.
After 4 Pomodoros, take a 15-minute long break.
guideTipsItems: Eliminate distractions before starting (turn off notifications, tidy your space).
Focus on only one task at a time.
Fully rest your mind during breaks.
Plan your daily Pomodoro goal in advance.
Tracking completed Pomodoros helps with motivation.

breathingExercise.title: Breathing Exercise Guide
breathingExercise.description: Follow scientifically proven breathing techniques like 4-7-8, box breathing, and relaxing breath with real-time animation.
breathingExercise.pattern478Desc: Dr. Andrew Weil's technique effective for anxiety relief and sleep induction.
breathingExercise.pattern478Guide: Inhale 4s, hold 7s, exhale 8s. Use before sleep or when feeling anxious.
breathingExercise.patternBoxDesc: Used by Navy SEALs to improve focus and reduce stress in high-pressure situations.
breathingExercise.patternBoxGuide: Inhale 4s, hold 4s, exhale 4s, hold 4s. Great as a pre-work focus routine.
breathingExercise.patternRelaxingDesc: A simple everyday breathing technique that activates the parasympathetic nervous system through a long exhale.
breathingExercise.patternRelaxingGuide: Inhale 4s, hold 2s, exhale slowly for 6s. Quickly relieve tension anytime, anywhere.
guideBenefitsItems: Reduces stress hormones (cortisol) and relieves anxiety
Stabilizes heart rate and blood pressure
Enhances sleep quality and reduces time to fall asleep
guideTipsItems: Start in a quiet, comfortable seated or lying position.
Inhale through the nose and exhale through the mouth is generally recommended.
Practice 2–3 times daily for 5–10 minutes for best results.
If you feel dizzy, pause and return to normal breathing.
Use it immediately when you encounter a stressful situation.

speedTest.title: Internet Speed Test
speedTest.description: Measure download speed and ping (latency) in real time.
speedTest.slowDesc: Under 25 Mbps — basic browsing, SD video
speedTest.corsNote: Results may be approximate due to CORS policies with external servers.
speedTest.disclaimer: Results may vary based on network conditions, server location, and device performance. For reference only.
guideSection1Items: Download Speed: how fast data is received from the internet (Mbps)
Ping: round-trip time for a request to reach a server and return (ms — lower is better)
Mbps: Megabits per second, the unit of transfer rate
guideSection2Items: A wired Ethernet connection gives more accurate results than Wi-Fi.
Measure when other devices or apps are not using the network.
Run multiple tests and average the results for reliability.
VPN usage may cause lower-than-actual speed readings.

cssUnitConverter.title: CSS Unit Converter
cssUnitConverter.description: Instantly convert between CSS units: px, rem, em, vw, vh, %, pt, cm, mm, in.
cssUnitConverter.quickRefDesc: Quick reference table for frequently used CSS unit conversions.
guideAbsoluteItems: px (pixels): Smallest screen unit. Fixed size. On retina displays, 1px maps to 2–3 physical pixels.
cm / mm / in: Physical size units. Mainly used in print CSS rather than screen layouts.
guideRelativeItems: rem: Relative to the root (<html>) font size. Default is usually 16px. Great for global scaling.
em: Relative to the current or parent element's font size. Can compound when nested — use carefully.
% (percent): Relative to the parent element's corresponding property. For font-size, % is relative to parent font size.
guideViewportItems: vw: 1% of the viewport width. 100vw = full screen width. Ideal for responsive layouts.
vh: 1% of the viewport height. 100vh = full screen height. Great for full-screen sections.
svh/dvh: New viewport units addressing mobile browser address bar issues (modern browser support).
guideTipsItems: Font sizes: Use rem — respects user browser settings and improves accessibility.
Layout spacing: Use rem or px — maintains consistency.
Component inner spacing: Use em — scales with the component's font size.
Responsive widths: Use vw, %, or min/max combinations for flexible layouts.
Media query breakpoints: Use px or em — em responds to user font size preferences.
Print CSS: Use pt, cm, mm — guarantees physical dimensions.

guideFastingItems: Diabetes: 126 mg/dL or higher (confirmed on 2+ occasions)
guideTipsItems: Fasting blood sugar should be measured after at least 8 hours without food.
Post-meal blood sugar is typically measured 2 hours after starting a meal.
Regular exercise and a balanced diet help maintain healthy blood sugar levels.
Consult a doctor immediately if your blood sugar is consistently abnormal.
This tool is for reference only and does not replace medical diagnosis.

fancyText.noInput: Enter text above to see converted styles
fancyText.asciiNote: Only ASCII letters (a-z, A-Z) are converted. Numbers and symbols stay unchanged.

guide.title: Usage Guide
usageItems: Stand out on Instagram, X (Twitter), or Facebook profiles and posts
Add unique fonts to YouTube or TikTok channel names and usernames
Combine with emojis for eye-catching decorative text effects
Use hard-to-read styles for password hints or secret notes
Test special font ideas for web design mockups or logo sketches
howItems: Uses characters from the Unicode Mathematical Symbols block (U+1D400 to U+1D7FF)
Only ASCII letters a-z and A-Z are converted; Korean, Chinese, and digits remain unchanged
Strikethrough and underline add Unicode combining characters to each letter
Upside-down and mirror styles use special Unicode character mappings

Screen readers and assistive technology may not read these as intended
Not all fonts support Mathematical Unicode blocks - unsupported characters may appear as boxes
Copied text can be pasted anywhere that accepts Unicode

whatIs.title: What is the Unicode Text Styler?
whatIs.description: A tool that converts regular English text into various Unicode styles — mathematical symbols, double-struck, fraktur, bold, italic, and more. Since it uses actual Unicode code points rather than font changes, you can copy and paste the styled text into any platform that renders Unicode: Instagram, X (Twitter), KakaoTalk, LinkedIn profiles, and game nicknames.

items.q: Why doesn't Korean text convert?
items.a: This tool only supports Latin (English) character conversion. The Unicode mathematical character blocks are only defined for Latin letters. For Korean text decoration, use emojis or dedicated font apps.

items.q: The converted text looks broken on some platforms
items.a: If a platform or device doesn't include fonts covering the Unicode mathematical symbol range, characters may display as □ or ?. Modern iOS, Android, and Windows 10+ environments generally display them correctly.

items.q: Will search engines recognize this styled text?
items.a: Search engines often don't recognize Unicode mathematical symbols as regular text. Avoid using styled text for SEO content — it's best for visual design purposes only.

investmentCalculator.title: Investment Return Calculator
investmentCalculator.description: Calculate CAGR, compare lump sum vs DCA investments, and inflation-adjusted real returns
investmentCalculator.noResult: Set investment conditions and click Calculate

vs.title: Comparing lump sum vs DCA with the same total amount

concepts.title: Key Concepts
items: CAGR: Compound Annual Growth Rate, assumes steady annual growth
Lump Sum: Investing all money at once for long-term holding
DCA: Dollar Cost Averaging, investing fixed amounts monthly
Real Return: Actual purchasing power return after deducting inflation

formulas.title: Formulas
items: Compound: FV = PV × (1 + r/12)^(12×n)
CAGR = (Final/Initial)^(1/years) - 1
Real Return = (1+Nominal)/(1+Inflation) - 1

tips.title: Investment Tips
items: Lump sum tends to outperform DCA over longer periods
DCA reduces risk by averaging out market volatility
Consider inflation rate of 2-3% for realistic projections
Actual returns should account for taxes and fees

whatIs.title: What is the Investment Return Calculator?
whatIs.description: An investment analysis tool that calculates CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate), lump-sum vs. installment investment comparisons, and inflation-adjusted real returns in one place. You can back-calculate the required monthly contribution to reach a target amount, simulate compound growth, and clearly see the difference between nominal and real returns. Use it to compare stocks, ETFs, funds, and deposits over the long term.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the calculation type — CAGR, compound growth simulation, target amount back-calculation, etc.
2. Enter initial investment, monthly contribution (optional), annual return rate, and investment duration.
3. To account for inflation, enter the annual inflation rate for inflation-adjusted real return.
4. Review the result graph showing principal vs. gains and year-by-year portfolio growth.

items.q: What's the difference between CAGR and simple return?
items.a: Simple return compares only start and end values. CAGR expresses the compound annual growth rate — how much the investment grew per year on average. CAGR becomes more meaningful over longer investment periods.

items.q: Why does real return matter?
items.a: A 7% nominal return with 3% inflation means only ~4% actual purchasing power growth. Long-term investment performance must always be evaluated using real (inflation-adjusted) returns.

items.q: Which is better: lump-sum or installment investing?
items.a: Lump-sum generally outperforms in steadily rising markets; installment investing (dollar-cost averaging) is advantageous in volatile markets. Compare both directly with this calculator.

imageCompressor.title: Image Compressor
imageCompressor.description: Compress JPG, PNG, WebP images. Adjust quality, convert formats, and batch process multiple files.
imageCompressor.dropzone: Drag images or click to upload

features.title: Features
items: Drag and drop for easy upload
Quality slider to balance quality and size
Convert between JPG, PNG, and WebP
Set max width/height to resize images
Batch compress multiple images at once

formats.title: Format Comparison
items: JPEG: Best for photos, high compression, no transparency
PNG: Supports transparency, ideal for logos and icons
WebP: Next-gen format, 25-35% smaller than JPEG

tips.title: Compression Tips
items: 70-85% quality is optimal for web images
Converting PNG to WebP gives significant size savings
Max width of 1200px is sufficient for social media
Always keep backup copies of original files

whatIs.title: What is the Image Compressor?
whatIs.description: A browser-based image optimization tool that reduces file size for JPG, PNG, and WebP images. All processing happens on your device — images are never uploaded to a server, protecting your privacy. Use it for web image optimization, reducing email attachment sizes, pre-processing SNS uploads, and batch compressing multiple images at once.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Drag and drop image files or click 'Select Files' to upload (multiple files supported).
2. Adjust the compression quality (0–100%) — around 80% is a good balance of quality and size.
3. Select the output format (keep original, convert to WebP, etc.).
4. Click 'Compress', review the before/after results, and download.

items.q: Is WebP supported in all browsers?
items.a: Most modern browsers support WebP — Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari 14+. IE and some older browsers don't support it. For public websites, consider providing JPG fallbacks.

items.q: Can I restore the original after compression?
items.a: Lossy compression (JPG, WebP) is irreversible. Always keep a backup of originals before compressing important files.

items.q: Which compresses better: PNG or JPG?
items.a: For photos, JPG/WebP compress much smaller. For images with text, logos, or transparent backgrounds, PNG preserves quality without loss. Choose the right format for your use case.

screenRecorder.title: Screen Recorder
screenRecorder.description: Record your screen, window, or tab directly in the browser without installing anything
screenRecorder.readyToRecordDesc: Click the button below to select what to record. You can choose entire screen, specific window, or browser tab.
screenRecorder.permissionDeniedDesc: Please allow screen sharing in your browser settings, or try again.
screenRecorder.notSupportedDesc: Your browser does not support screen recording. Please use a modern browser like Chrome, Edge, or Firefox.

howto.title: How to Use
items: Select quality, frame rate, and audio settings
Click the red record button
Choose what to record (screen/window/tab)
Pause and resume during recording
Click stop to view the preview
Download the recording as a WebM file

formats.title: Recording Specs
items: Output format: WebM (VP9 codec)
Resolution: 480p / 720p / 1080p selectable
Frame rate: 30fps or 60fps
Audio: System sound, microphone, or both

tips.title: Useful Tips
items: Tab recording includes system audio
Lower quality for longer recordings to save space
WebM files can be played in VLC, browsers, etc.
Disable notifications before recording for cleaner videos

whatIs.title: What is the Screen Recorder?
whatIs.description: A tool that lets you record your screen, a specific window, or a browser tab directly from the browser — no software installation required. It uses the Screen Capture API and supports microphone audio and system audio (tab sound) recording. Perfect for creating online tutorials, capturing bug reproductions, recording work demos, and more.

items.q: Which browsers are supported?
items.a: Chrome, Edge, and Firefox with Screen Capture API support. Safari has limited support; most mobile browsers don't support screen recording.

items.q: Are recordings saved to a server?
items.a: No. All processing happens in the browser and files are not uploaded to any server. Recordings are downloaded directly to your local device.

items.q: Can I record system audio (video/game sound)?
items.a: In Chrome, choosing 'Tab sharing' captures the tab's audio along with the screen. Full-screen or window capture may have limitations on system audio capture depending on the OS.

sleepCalculator.title: Sleep Calculator
sleepCalculator.description: Calculate optimal bedtime and wake-up times based on 90-minute sleep cycles
sleepCalculator.cycleExplanation: Sleep cycles last about 90 minutes. Waking at the end of a cycle helps you feel refreshed. It takes an average of 14 minutes to fall asleep.
guideHabitsItems: Go to bed and wake up at the same time every day
Reduce screen time 1 hour before bed
Keep your bedroom dark and cool (64-68°F / 18-20°C)
Exercise regularly but finish 3 hours before bedtime
guideScienceItems: Waking during REM: remember dreams. During deep sleep: feel groggy
First 2 cycles (3 hours) have the most deep sleep
Adequate sleep is essential for memory, immunity, and mood
guideTipsItems: A warm shower before bed triggers cooling that induces sleep
Alcohol induces sleep but reduces sleep quality
Naps should be under 20 minutes, avoid after 3 PM
Use your bed only for sleep and relaxation

voiceMemo.description: Record, play, and download voice memos directly in your browser
voiceMemo.notSupported: Microphone is not available in this browser
voiceMemo.notSupportedDesc: Please use a modern browser like Chrome, Edge, or Firefox.
voiceMemo.micPermissionDenied: Microphone access was denied. Please allow microphone in browser settings.
voiceMemo.micError: An error occurred while accessing the microphone
voiceMemo.disclaimer: Recordings are stored in the browser session only and will be deleted when you close the page.

howto.title: How to Use
items: Click the red record button
Allow microphone access when prompted
Pause and resume during recording
Click stop to save the recording
Play back or download your recordings

formats.title: Supported Formats
items: WebM/Opus: Supported in most modern browsers
OGG/Opus: Default format in Firefox
All files can be played back in the browser
Compatible with VLC and other media players

tips.title: Recording Tips
items: Record in a quiet environment for better quality
Keep a proper distance from the microphone (15-30cm)
Download important recordings immediately
Use the pause feature for long recordings

whatIs.title: What is Voice Memo?
whatIs.description: A tool that lets you record audio using your microphone, play it back, and download it as MP3/WAV — directly in the browser without any app installation. Quickly capture meeting notes, ideas, lectures, or anything you want to revisit later. Useful for personal memos, interview recordings, and podcast draft recordings.

items.q: What format are recordings saved in?
items.a: Files are saved in WebM (Opus), MP4, or OGG format depending on the browser and device. Most media players can play these formats; use a converter tool if needed.

items.q: Which microphone is used if I have multiple?
items.a: The system default microphone is used. You can select a specific microphone device in the browser's permission settings.

items.q: What happens if I lose connection or close the browser during recording?
items.a: Recordings are stored in browser memory. If the browser closes or the page refreshes, unsaved recordings may be lost. Save important recordings periodically.
