nutritionCalculator.title: Nutrition Calculator
nutritionCalculator.description: Calculate calories and macronutrients (carbs, protein, fat) for meals using a Korean food database.
nutritionCalculator.mealEmpty: Click a food item to add it to your meal

summary.title: Summary
summary.driNote: * Adult standard: 2,000 kcal / Carbs 324g / Protein 55g / Fat 54g (KNS)

guide.title: How to Use the Nutrition Calculator

howToUse.title: Usage
items: Use category buttons or the search bar to find foods
Click a food card to add it to 'My Meal'
Adjust portions (0.5x – 3x) to match actual intake
Check the charts for macro ratio and % of daily recommended intake
Use the copy button to save a meal summary to clipboard

macros.title: What are Macronutrients?
items: Carbs (C): 4 kcal/g, primary energy source. Daily goal: 324g (55-65% of total calories)
Protein (P): 4 kcal/g, builds muscle and enzymes. Daily goal: 55g (7-20% of total calories)
Fat (F): 9 kcal/g, hormones and cell membranes. Daily goal: 54g (15-30% of total calories)

tips.title: Healthy Eating Tips
items: Distribute daily calories: breakfast 30%, lunch 40%, dinner 30%
Adequate protein helps you feel full longer
Soups and stews can be high in sodium — limit broth intake
Balance high-fat foods like pork belly or fried chicken with vegetables
Don't forget drink calories — lattes, soju, and beer add up quickly

whatIs.title: What is the Nutrition Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that calculates calories and three macronutrients (carbohydrates, protein, fat) for meals using a Korean food database. Enter your daily meals to see total nutritional intake and how it compares to recommended daily allowances. Use it for diet management, muscle building, or dietary control for conditions like diabetes and hypertension.

items.q: What are the recommended macronutrient ratios?
items.a: For average adults: carbohydrates 50–60%, protein 15–20%, fat 20–30%. Adjust protein higher for muscle gain and reduce carbohydrates for weight loss.

items.q: What if a food isn't in the database?
items.a: Substitute with a similar food or manually enter nutritional values from the product label. For branded processed foods, check the manufacturer's website for exact figures.

items.q: What is the daily calorie recommendation?
items.a: Generally 2,500 kcal for adult males and 2,000 kcal for adult females, but it varies by BMR and activity level. Check your personalized recommendation with the Calorie Calculator first.

presentationTimer.title: Presentation Timer
presentationTimer.description: Countdown timer optimized for presentations, meetings, and lectures

howto.title: How to Use
items: Set your presentation time in minutes
Configure warning and danger times for color changes
Click Start to begin the countdown
Use fullscreen mode for visibility during presentations
Adjust time on the fly with +1/-1 minute buttons

features.title: Features
items: Circular progress ring: Visual time remaining indicator
Color phases: Green (normal) → Yellow (warning) → Red (danger)
Sound alerts: Audio notifications at warning, danger, and end
Overtime counter: Keeps counting after time expires
Fullscreen: Full-screen display using Fullscreen API

tips.title: Presentation Tips
items: Allocate 80% of time to content and 20% to Q&A
Set warning at 20% and danger at 10% of total time
Use quick presets for commonly used durations
Practice with the timer to develop time awareness

whatIs.title: What is the Presentation Timer?
whatIs.description: A professional countdown timer optimized for presentations, lectures, and meetings. Features include color-changing visual warnings as time runs low, full-screen mode, and a last-minute alert. Works for solo presentation practice or as an MC tool to manage multiple speakers' time.

items.q: Can I hide the timer from the audience?
items.a: In full-screen mode on a dual-monitor setup, the presenter can see the timer while the audience sees only the presentation. Alternatively, open the timer on a separate smartphone screen for personal monitoring.

items.q: What happens when time runs out?
items.a: The display turns red and begins counting up (overtime), showing how much over time you've gone. Useful for tracking how long over schedule a presentation runs.

items.q: Are sound alerts supported?
items.a: Yes, sound alerts can be set for remaining time milestones (e.g., 5 minutes, 1 minute). During a live presentation, visual warnings or vibration are recommended to avoid disrupting the audience.

unemploymentBenefit.title: Unemployment Benefits Calculator
unemploymentBenefit.description: Calculate unemployment benefits (구직급여) based on 2026 Korean employment insurance law
unemploymentBenefit.voluntaryWarning: Voluntary resignation generally does not qualify for unemployment benefits. However, if you had justifiable cause (unpaid wages, labor law violations, etc.), you may still be eligible.
unemploymentBenefit.voluntaryBlocked: Voluntary resignation is not eligible for benefits. Please change the separation reason.
unemploymentBenefit.placeholder: Enter your information on the left and click Calculate

form.title: Benefit Information
form.avgDailyWageHint: Total salary for last 3 months ÷ total days (usually 90)

result.title: Benefit Calculation Result
result.capNotice: 60% of average wage exceeds the daily cap (₩66,000). The cap amount has been applied.
result.floorNotice: 60% of average wage is below the daily floor (₩63,104). The floor amount has been applied.

eligibility.title: Eligibility Requirements
items: At least 180 insured days within 18 months before separation
Involuntary separation (layoff, dismissal, contract expiration)
Willing and able to work but currently unemployed
Actively seeking reemployment

howToApply.title: How to Apply
items: Register as job seeker on WorkNet (work.go.kr)
Visit employment center or apply online
After qualification approval, attend unemployment certification every 1-4 weeks
Benefits paid at each certification visit

extended.title: Extended Benefits
items: Training extension: Up to 2 years while in vocational training
Individual extension: 60 additional days for financial hardship
Special extension: 60 additional days by government decree

caution.title: Important Notes
items: Voluntary resignation generally not eligible (exceptions for just cause)
Benefits stop when you become reemployed
Fraudulent claims: up to 5x repayment + criminal penalties
This calculator is for reference only. Verify with your local employment center

whatIs.title: What is the Unemployment Benefit Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that automatically calculates expected unemployment benefit (구직급여) amounts and duration based on 2026 Employment Insurance regulations. Enter your average salary before leaving, insured period, and age to get daily benefit amount and total benefit duration. Use it during job transition planning to budget for living expenses.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your average monthly salary for the last 3 months before leaving (pre-tax).
2. Enter the total Employment Insurance contribution period in months.
3. Select your current age and reason for leaving (layoff, contract expiry, voluntary resignation, etc.).
4. View the daily benefit amount, total benefit duration, and estimated total payout.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Voluntary resignation is generally ineligible, but exceptions exist (workplace harassment, wage theft, etc.).
Applications must be filed within 12 months of leaving — delays may reduce total benefit days.
During the benefit period, proof of job-seeking activity is required — minimum 2 activities per 4-week cycle.

items.q: How is the benefit duration determined?
items.a: Based on age and insured period, benefits last 120–270 days. Those 50+ or with disabilities may qualify for up to 270 days.

items.q: Is there a daily benefit cap?
items.a: As of 2026, the maximum daily benefit is 66,000 KRW (pre-tax). The minimum is 80% of the minimum wage. Even if your salary was high, you cannot exceed the cap.

items.q: What happens to remaining benefit days after reemployment?
items.a: If you have 50% or more of your benefit days remaining upon reemployment, you may apply for the Early Reemployment Allowance. The faster you find a job, the more you receive.

weeklyHolidayPay.title: Weekly Holiday Pay Calculator
weeklyHolidayPay.description: Calculate weekly holiday pay (주휴수당) including weekly, monthly, and annual salary based on hourly wage

result.title: Pay Calculation Result
result.chartTitle: Base Pay vs Holiday Pay Ratio

warning.title: Not Eligible for Holiday Pay
warning.message: Workers with less than 15 hours per week are not eligible for weekly holiday pay. You must work 15 or more hours per week.

info.title: What is Weekly Holiday Pay?
items: Workers who work 15+ hours per week are entitled to 1 paid day off (Labor Standards Act Art. 55)
Holiday hours = (total weekly hours ÷ 40) × 8 hours (max 8 hours)
Holiday pay = hourly wage × holiday hours
Applies regardless of social insurance enrollment if 15+ hours per week
Failure to pay holiday pay constitutes wage theft

calc.title: Calculation Method
items: Total weekly hours = daily hours × working days
Holiday hours = (weekly hours ÷ 40) × 8, max 8 hours
Holiday pay = hourly wage × holiday hours
Monthly pay = (weekly base + holiday pay) × 4.345
Annual pay = monthly pay × 12

notes.title: Important Notes
items: Must have perfect attendance on scheduled workdays in the week
Ultra-short-time workers (avg. < 15 hrs/week over 4 weeks) are excluded
Freelancers and self-employed are not eligible (based on employment contract)
For 5 days × 8 hours (40 hrs), maximum holiday hours is 8
Holiday pay applies during probation period as well

whatIs.title: What is the Weekly Holiday Pay Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that automatically calculates the mandatory weekly holiday allowance (주휴수당) owed to workers who work 15 or more hours per week under the Labor Standards Act. Enter your hourly wage and weekly hours to get weekly holiday pay, total weekly pay including holiday pay, monthly salary, and annual salary. Useful for part-time and hourly workers checking they're paid correctly.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your hourly wage (KRW).
2. Enter your actual weekly working hours (e.g., 5 hours/day × 5 days = 25 hours).
3. See the weekly holiday pay (equivalent to 1 day's pay) and effective hourly rate including holiday pay.
4. Monthly and annual salary are automatically calculated based on 4.345 weeks/month.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Weekly holiday pay does not apply to workers under 15 hours/week — employers sometimes intentionally schedule below this threshold.
Weekly holiday pay must be paid separately — job ads claiming hourly wages 'including holiday pay' may actually be below legal minimum.
Always confirm weekly holiday pay is listed as a separate line item on your pay stub.

items.q: Are part-time convenience store workers entitled to weekly holiday pay?
items.a: Yes, any worker who works 15 or more hours per week is entitled to weekly holiday pay regardless of industry or employer size.

items.q: Does being late or leaving early affect holiday pay?
items.a: Weekly holiday pay requires full attendance during the scheduled workweek. Unauthorized absences in the same week may result in forfeiture of that week's holiday pay.

items.q: How do I report unpaid weekly holiday pay?
items.a: File a complaint with the Ministry of Employment and Labor or call 1350. Unpaid wages from the past 3 years are eligible for claims.

items: JSON Array: Array of objects [{"key": "value"}, ...]
CSV: Header row + comma-separated data (tab-delimited also supported)
Drag & Drop: Drop .json or .csv files directly
Numeric columns are auto-detected and suggested for Y axis

chartTypes.title: Chart Types
items: Bar: Compare values by category, supports horizontal/vertical/stacked
Line: Show trends over time, supports smooth curves
Area: Line chart with filled area, great for stacked comparisons
Pie: Show proportions of a whole, single Y field only
Scatter: Analyze correlation between two variables
Radar: Compare multi-dimensional data

tips.title: Tips
items: Select multiple Y fields for multi-series charts
Copy the ECharts Option code directly into your project
React Component code requires the echarts-for-react library
PNG download saves at 2x resolution for crisp images

whatIs.title: What is Chart Studio?
whatIs.description: A data visualization tool that instantly converts pasted JSON or CSV data into bar, line, pie, and other chart types, with copy-ready chart code. Visualize and share data directly in the browser without BI tools or Excel. Use it for presentations, analysis reports, and dashboards.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Paste JSON or CSV data into the input field.
2. Select a chart type (bar, line, pie, scatter, etc.).
3. Configure X-axis, Y-axis columns, colors, and labels.
4. Save the chart as an image or copy the code to use in your project.

items.q: What data format should I use?
items.a: CSV expects the first row as headers in standard format. JSON should be an array ([{"name": "A", "value": 100}, ...]). Exporting from Excel as CSV then pasting is the easiest method.

items.q: Can I save charts as images?
items.a: Yes, download as PNG or SVG. High-resolution images ready to insert directly into presentations or reports.

items.q: Is data sent to a server?
items.a: No, all processing happens in the browser. Sensitive business data can be visualized securely.

emailTemplate.title: Business Email Template Generator
emailTemplate.description: Select a category and tone, fill in the variables, and get a professionally formatted business email ready to copy.

guide.title: Email Writing Guide
tips: Choose a tone that matches the recipient's role and your relationship
Keep the subject line concise and focused on the key point
State the purpose of the email clearly in the first sentence
Include specific dates, numbers, and conditions for clarity
End with a clear call to action (reply, review, etc.)
etiquette: Send business emails during working hours when possible
Mention attachments in the body if files are attached
Avoid emojis in formal business communications

whatIs.title: What is the Business Email Template Generator?
whatIs.description: A tool that automatically generates professional business emails by selecting a category (request, thank you, apology, proposal, etc.), tone (formal/friendly), and filling in key variables. It reflects Korean and English business email conventions to suggest appropriate phrasing for each situation. Useful for new employees and anyone who struggles with formal email writing.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the email type (meeting request, proposal submission, thank you, apology, etc.).
2. Select the relationship with the recipient and desired tone (formal/informal).
3. Enter key variables: recipient name, company, date, etc.
4. Review the generated email, edit as needed, and copy to use.

items.q: Can I edit the generated email?
items.a: Yes, the generated text is fully editable. Use the template as a base and customize freely.

items.q: Can it generate English business emails?
items.a: Yes, select English in the language option to generate templates following English-language business email conventions.

items.q: Can I include attachment references or follow-up requests?
items.a: Yes, depending on the email type, attachment mentions, reply requests, and follow-up meeting suggestions are automatically included.

promptGenerator.title: AI Prompt Generator
promptGenerator.description: Select role, task, tone, and format to automatically generate AI prompts

text.taskPlaceholder: Describe the task for the AI

image.subject: Subject
image.negativePromptPlaceholder: Enter elements to exclude from generation

output.empty: Select options on the left to generate a prompt

textTips.title: Text AI Prompt Tips
items: Specifying a role helps get more specialized responses
Write tasks clearly and specifically
Providing context helps generate relevant answers
Specifying output format gives you structured responses
Use 'Improve Quality' to auto-add examples and step-by-step instructions

imageTips.title: Image AI Prompt Tips
items: The more specific your subject description, the better the results
Combining style and medium gives consistent outputs
Adding quality tags improves image quality
Use negative prompts to exclude unwanted elements
Choose aspect ratio based on intended use (Social: 1:1, Wallpaper: 16:9)

whatIs.title: What is the AI Prompt Generator?
whatIs.description: A tool that automatically generates optimized prompts for AI language models like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. Select a role, task, tone, and output format to produce a structured, effective prompt. Also supports image generation AI prompts for Midjourney, DALL-E, and Stable Diffusion. Ideal for AI beginners and anyone who needs consistent, high-quality prompt structures.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the AI type — text AI (ChatGPT/Claude etc.) or image AI (Midjourney etc.).
2. Choose the role/persona, task description, and desired tone (professional/casual/creative).
3. Select output format (list/table/essay/code) and add special requirements.
4. Copy the generated prompt and paste it into your AI tool.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Setting a role (e.g., 'As a 10-year marketing veteran') significantly improves the expertise and direction of AI responses.
Specifying output format clearly (e.g., '5 bullet points', 'table format') makes it much easier to get the result you want.
Adding 'explain step by step' or 'use chain of thought' to prompts is effective for complex problem-solving.

items.q: Do different AI models give different results for the same prompt?
items.a: Yes. Each AI model has different training data and characteristics. Try the same prompt across multiple models and choose the best one for your use case.

items.q: How are image generation prompts different?
items.a: Image prompts focus on visual elements: style (e.g., watercolor, photorealism), lighting, composition, resolution, and artist style references. Unlike text prompts, comma-separated keywords work better than full sentences.

items.q: Can I save and reuse prompts?
items.a: Copy generated prompts to a separate document, or use browser bookmarks for quick access. Templating frequently used prompt types makes your workflow much faster.

whatIs.title: What is the Gantt Chart Generator?
whatIs.description: A project management tool that visualizes task lists, start/end dates, assignees, and progress percentages as Gantt charts. Quickly visualize schedules in the browser and share with team members without complex PM software. Use it for freelance project planning, team sprint planning, and personal goal management.

items.q: Can I export the Gantt chart as an image or PDF?
items.a: Yes, export as PNG image or PDF. Useful for sharing via email or inserting into presentations.

items.q: Can I set task dependencies?
items.a: Yes, set predecessor relationships (B starts after A completes) to display dependency arrows. Use it to identify your project's critical path.

items.q: Is real-time collaborative editing supported?
items.a: Currently a single-user editing tool. For collaboration, share the exported data or combine with collaborative tools like Notion or Asana.

invoiceGenerator.title: Invoice / Tax Invoice Generator
invoiceGenerator.description: Create professional invoices and export them as PDF
invoiceGenerator.notesPlaceholder: Payment terms, delivery info, or any other notes

sections.title: Required Fields
items: Business registration number is mandatory for tax invoices
Clearly state invoice date and due/validity date
List item name, quantity, unit price, and amount accurately
Distinguish between taxable and VAT-exempt items

sections.title: VAT Information
items: General taxpayers charge 10% VAT on the supply amount
Uncheck VAT for exempt items (medical, education, etc.)
Simplified taxpayers may have limited invoice issuance rights
Electronic tax invoices are legally binding official documents

sections.title: PDF Export Tips
items: The PDF export uses ASCII-safe rendering for compatibility
Korean characters may appear as '?' in PDF — use Print for Korean output
The Print button opens your browser's native print dialog
Enable 'Background graphics' in print settings for best results

sections.title: Save & Manage
items: Use Save to store your current invoice in the browser
Use Load to restore a previously saved invoice
Reset generates a new invoice number and clears all fields
Always export important invoices as PDF for record-keeping

whatIs.title: What is the Invoice / Tax Invoice Generator?
whatIs.description: A tool for freelancers and small business owners to quickly create professional invoices and tax invoices and export them as PDF. Enter your business info, client details, line items and amounts, and VAT (10%) is automatically calculated with a total sum. No accounting software needed — generate and email or print the PDF directly.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the issuer information (business name, registration number, address, etc.).
2. Enter the client information (company name, contact, email, etc.).
3. Add line items with quantity and unit price — subtotal and VAT (10%) are auto-calculated.
4. Click 'Save as PDF' to generate the print-ready document.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Save frequently used line items as templates for recurring clients to speed up the process.
For tax invoices, clearly distinguish between the supply date and the invoice issue date (legally important).
After issuing a quote, convert the same content to a tax invoice upon order confirmation for consistency.

items.q: Can I issue legally valid electronic tax invoices here?
items.a: Legally valid e-tax invoices must be issued through the National Tax Service's Hometax. This tool generates document-format invoices for sharing with clients. Use Hometax for official tax reporting.

items.q: Can tax-exempt businesses use this?
items.a: Yes, set VAT to 0% for tax-exempt transactions. Tax-exempt businesses should issue a 'bill of supply' (계산서) without VAT rather than a tax invoice.

items.q: Can I save client information for reuse?
items.a: Frequently used info is saved via browser localStorage and can be reloaded. Enter regular clients once and reuse conveniently.

meetingMinutes.title: Meeting Minutes
meetingMinutes.description: Record meeting agenda, attendees, discussions, and action items systematically
meetingMinutes.speaker: Speaker
meetingMinutes.decisionsPlaceholder: Record decisions reached during the meeting
meetingMinutes.nextMeetingPlaceholder: e.g. March 7, 2025 at 10:00 AM, same location

sections.title: Quick Start
items: Use a template button to auto-populate agenda items for your meeting type
Fill in basic info (title, date, location, organizer) first
Add attendees and mark their attendance status
Set time allocations per agenda item to manage the meeting schedule

sections.title: Managing Action Items
items: Add action items immediately as decisions are made during the meeting
Assign a clear owner and due date to each task
Update statuses (Pending / In Progress / Done) regularly
Review action item completion before the next meeting

sections.title: Export & Share
items: Copy Text: paste into email or messaging apps
Export MD: use in GitHub, Notion, Confluence, etc.
Export HTML: open in browser or paste as email body
Print: switch to Preview tab first for clean print output

sections.title: Save & Auto-Save
items: Your content is auto-saved to the browser (survives tab close)
Use named save slots to keep multiple meeting minutes
Type a slot name and press Enter or click Save
Use Load to restore any previously saved minutes

whatIs.title: What is the Meeting Minutes Maker?
whatIs.description: A digital meeting minutes tool for systematically recording meeting date/time, attendees, agenda items, discussion points, decisions, and action items, then exporting as PDF or text. The structured template supports quick note-taking during meetings or polishing notes afterward. Saves in a format easy to share via email or messenger.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter basic meeting info (date/time, location, title, facilitator).
2. Add attendee list (name, department, role).
3. Record discussion points and decisions for each agenda item.
4. Add action items (assignee, deadline, task) and save as PDF or text.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Pre-entering agenda items before the meeting helps keep discussion on track.
Write action items clearly in 'who does what by when' format for easier follow-through.
Complete and distribute meeting minutes within 30 minutes of the meeting ending for best results.

items.q: Can I email meeting minutes directly from here?
items.a: Direct email sending isn't available. Save as PDF or text, then attach or paste the content into your email.

items.q: Can I load previous minutes to continue?
items.a: Load a saved JSON file using the import function, or use previous minutes as a template for recurring meetings.

items.q: Can I export in Markdown format?
items.a: Yes, Markdown export is available. Paste directly into GitHub, Notion, Confluence, or any Markdown-supporting collaboration tool.

kanbanBoard.description: Drag-and-drop Kanban board for managing project tasks
kanbanBoard.subtitle: {count} cards total · {cols} columns
kanbanBoard.confirmDeleteColumn: Delete this column and all its cards?
kanbanBoard.importError: Failed to load the JSON file. Please check that it is a valid board export.

howTo.title: How to Use
items: Click 'Add Column' to create stages like To Do, In Progress, Done
Click 'Add Card' at the bottom of any column to create a task card
Drag cards to move them between columns or reorder within a column
Hover over a card to reveal edit and delete buttons
Use search and filter to show only cards with a specific priority

tips.title: Tips
items: Export the board as JSON to back it up or share with your team
Cards with a past due date display the date in red
Use color labels to categorize work types (feature, bug, docs, etc.)
Use the priority filter to focus on the most important tasks
Board data is auto-saved to your browser's local storage

dragDrop.title: Drag & Drop
dragDrop.desc: Click and drag a card to move it to another column or to a different position within the same column. On mobile, columns stack vertically — use the Add Card button to create new tasks.

whatIs.title: What is the Kanban Board?
whatIs.description: A visual task management tool where you move task cards by drag and drop through stages: To Do → In Progress → Done. Manage personal work or team projects directly in the browser without complex project management apps. Data is stored in the browser, so no account is needed to start immediately.

items.q: Where is task data stored?
items.a: Data is stored in browser localStorage. Tasks persist when you revisit the same browser on the same device. Use the export function to transfer between devices.

items.q: Can I share the board with team members?
items.a: Real-time sharing is not supported with local storage. Export data as JSON to share, or use cloud-based tools like Trello or Notion for team collaboration.

items.q: Can I add or rename columns?
items.a: Yes, you can add custom columns beyond the default 3 stages — such as 'Review', 'QA', or 'Deployed' — and rename existing ones.

menuPicker.title: What Should I Eat?
menuPicker.description: Can't decide what to eat? Spin the roulette to pick today's menu!
menuPicker.tournamentDesc: Pick the one you prefer between two menus!
menuPicker.notEnoughItems: Not enough items in selected categories ({current}/{required})
menuPicker.notEnoughTop3: Not enough items in selected categories (minimum 3 required)

q1.question: How is the menu selected?
q1.answer: It's completely random. Menus are randomly drawn from selected categories and displayed on the roulette. The menu where the roulette stops is chosen.

q2.question: What happens when I change categories?
q2.answer: Only foods from selected categories appear on the roulette. You can select multiple categories or narrow down to specific ones.

q3.question: Will I get the same result every time?
q3.answer: No, the menu composition and result change with every spin. Even with the same settings, you'll get different results each time.

section1.title: How to Use
items: Select your preferred categories (default: all)
Choose a situation tag (solo, group, date, etc.)
Click 'Spin' or tap the roulette to start spinning
After the result, use 'Find Restaurants' to search nearby places
Use 'Shuffle' to change the menu items on the roulette

section2.title: Category Guide
items: 🍚 Korean: Kimchi stew, Bibimbap, Yukgaejang, Tteokgalbi etc. (36 items)
🥩 Meat/BBQ: Samgyeopsal, Galbi, Bulgogi, Jokbal, Bossam etc. (24 items)
🐟 Seafood: Sashimi, Maeuntang, Ganjang Gejang, Grilled Eel etc. (22 items)
🥡 Chinese: Jajangmyeon, Malatang, Sweet & Sour Pork, Mala Xiangguo etc. (20 items)
🍣 Japanese: Sushi, Ramen, Tonkatsu, Gyukatsu etc. (20 items)
🍝 Western: Pasta, Steak, Pizza, Tacos etc. (22 items)
🍢 Street Food: Tteokbokki, Sundae, Ramyeon, Jjolmyeon etc. (20 items)
🍗 Chicken: Fried, Seasoned, Soy Sauce, Pa-Dak etc. (16 items)
🍔 Fast Food: McDonald's, Burger King, Pizza Hut etc. (14 items)
🍜 Asian: Pho, Pad Thai, Curry, Hotpot etc. (20 items)

whatIs.title: What is 'What Should I Eat Today'?
whatIs.description: A random meal suggestion tool to solve the daily meal decision dilemma. Spin a roulette wheel, pick via tournament (repeatedly choose between two options), or let it auto-pick the top 3. Over 350 built-in options covering Korean, Chinese, Japanese, and Western cuisines, with the ability to add your own custom menu items.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select a mode — Roulette (random), Tournament (pick between two options repeatedly), or Top 3 (automatic top 3).
2. Filter by category (Korean/Chinese/Japanese/Western) or add custom menu items.
3. Click 'Spin' or 'Start'.
4. If you don't like the result, spin again.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: For team lunches or office dinners, use tournament mode to fairly aggregate everyone's preferences.
Remove or uncheck items you want to avoid — they'll be excluded from results.
Add your favorite delivery options to quickly resolve delivery app decision fatigue.

items.q: Are custom menu items saved?
items.a: Yes, saved in browser localStorage and persisted on next visit. Clearing browser data resets them.

items.q: The same menu keeps coming up — is it broken?
items.a: The roulette is completely random, so the same item can appear repeatedly. Deactivate specific items you don't want to see.

items.q: Does it work well on mobile?
items.a: Yes, a responsive design optimized for mobile is applied. Spin the roulette or select options by touch.

breakoutGame.title: Breakout
breakoutGame.description: Classic arcade brick-breaking game. Enjoy multiple levels and power-up items!

rules.title: Game Rules
items: Move the paddle left and right to bounce the ball
Break all bricks at the top to advance to the next level
Losing the ball costs one life
Game over when all 3 lives are lost
Some bricks require 2 hits to break

powerups.title: Power-ups
items: 🟢 Wide Paddle - Makes the paddle wider
🔵 Multi Ball - Splits into 3 balls
❤️ Extra Life - Adds one extra life

tips.title: Strategy Tips
items: Hitting the ball with the edge of the paddle changes its angle
Power-ups must be caught with the paddle to activate
Focus on one side to let the ball auto-clear bricks above

whatIs.title: What is this game?
whatIs.description: Breakout is a classic arcade game where you move a paddle to bounce a ball and break bricks. Features 5 levels, 2-hit bricks, 3 power-up types, and particle effects.

howToUse.title: How to Play
items: Click start or tap the screen to begin.
Move your mouse or touch to control the paddle.
The ball breaks bricks on contact for points.
Catch falling power-ups with your paddle for special effects.

items.q: How many levels are there?
items.a: There are 5 unique brick patterns. After level 5, patterns repeat with increasing ball speed.

items.q: What are the power-up types?
items.a: W(Wide): Paddle expands for 10 seconds. M(Multi): Ball splits into 3. +(Life): Adds 1 life (max 5).

items.q: What happens when the ball falls?
items.a: You lose 1 life and a new ball appears on the paddle. Game over when all lives are lost.

solitaire.title: Solitaire
solitaire.description: Classic Klondike Solitaire. Drag and drop cards to sort them!
solitaire.congratulations: Congratulations! You sorted all the cards!
basicRulesItems: Stack cards A through K by suit (♠♥♦♣) on the 4 foundations to win
Alternate colors (red↔black) in descending order on tableau
Only Kings can be placed on empty tableau columns
Click the stock to flip cards
Click face-down cards to reveal them
controlsItems: Drag and drop cards to move them
Double-click a card to auto-move to foundation
Ctrl+Z: Undo / H: Hint / N: New Game
tipsItems: Move Aces and 2s to the foundation immediately
Prioritize columns with more face-down cards
Don't create empty columns until you have a King
Use the hint button to find valid moves

crontabGenerator.title: Crontab Generator
crontabGenerator.description: Build cron expressions visually with Korean descriptions and next execution time preview.

format.title: Cron Expression Format
items: Minute(0-59) Hour(0-23) Day(1-31) Month(1-12) Weekday(0-7)
* = every, */n = interval, n-m = range, n,m = specific values
Weekday: 0 and 7 are Sunday, 1=Mon, 2=Tue, ..., 6=Sat

examples.title: Common Examples
items: * * * * * → Every minute
0 9 * * 1-5 → Weekdays at 9 AM
0 0 1 * * → 1st of every month at midnight
*/10 * * * * → Every 10 minutes

whatIs.title: What is the Crontab Generator?
whatIs.description: A tool that helps you easily generate crontab expressions for Linux/Unix scheduled task automation using a visual UI. Click sliders and checkboxes for minute, hour, day, month, and weekday fields to auto-generate the cron expression, with plain-language descriptions and a preview of the next execution times. Set up schedules intuitively without knowing cron syntax — useful for developers and server administrators alike.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select a recurrence preset — 'Every minute', 'Hourly', 'Daily', 'Weekly', 'Monthly', etc., or
2. Fine-tune each field (minute, hour, day, month, weekday) by clicking or typing directly.
3. Copy the generated cron expression (e.g., 0 9 * * 1).
4. On the server, run `crontab -e` and paste the expression followed by your command.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Daily at 9 AM: `0 9 * * *` — commonly used for batch jobs and report emails.
*/5 means 'every 5 minutes', 1-5 means '1 through 5', 1,3,5 means '1, 3, and 5' — combine ranges and lists.
Before editing, run `crontab -l` to review existing schedules and avoid duplicates.

items.q: Can I use crontab on Windows?
items.a: Windows doesn't have crontab. Use Windows Task Scheduler, or run cron inside WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) or a Docker container.

items.q: How can I see cron job execution logs?
items.a: Append `>> /var/log/mycron.log 2>&1` to your cron command to log stdout and stderr to a file. Also check `/var/log/syslog` or `/var/log/cron` for execution history.

items.q: Can I run jobs with sub-minute (second) precision?
items.a: Standard cron has a 1-minute minimum interval. For sub-minute execution, use a loop inside the cron job, or switch to `systemd timer` or language-specific scheduler libraries.

regexBuilder.title: Regex Builder
regexBuilder.description: Build and test regular expressions visually. Pattern library, real-time match highlighting, and string replacement support.

whatIs.title: What is this tool?
whatIs.description: The Regex Builder lets you visually create and test regular expressions. It includes a pattern library for common patterns (email, phone, URL, IP) and auto-explains regex in plain language.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Type a regex pattern or select from the pattern library.
Set flags (g: global, i: case-insensitive, m: multiline, etc.) as needed.
Enter test text to see matches highlighted in real-time.
View the pattern explanation for each token in plain language.

tips.title: Tips
items: Start by loading common patterns to learn regex structure.
The g (global) flag finds all matches; without it, only the first match is returned.
Use the copy button to paste completed regex directly into your code.

items.q: What is regex?
items.a: Regular expressions (regex) are rules for finding or validating patterns in text strings. Widely used in programming, data processing, and text editors.

items.q: How do I match phone numbers?
items.a: Use the 'Phone Number' pattern from the library to match various formats like 010-1234-5678 or 01012345678.

items.q: Can I use the same regex in JavaScript and Python?
items.a: Basic syntax is the same, but some flags and features may differ between engines. This tool uses the JavaScript regex engine.

envEditor.title: Env Editor
envEditor.description: Edit .env files and convert to various formats.
envEditor.rawPlaceholder:
# Paste your .env file contents here
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=5432
DB_NAME=myapp

format.title: .env File Format
items: Define environment variables as KEY=value
Lines starting with # are comments
Wrap values with spaces in double quotes: KEY="hello world"
Use empty lines to separate sections

usage.title: Usage Tips
items: Never commit .env files to Git
Document required variables in .env.example
Docker Compose uses the env_file option
Node.js loads .env with the dotenv package

whatIs.title: What is the .env Editor?
whatIs.description: A developer tool for editing .env files in the browser and converting them to various formats: dotenv, JSON, YAML, and shell export. Manage KEY=VALUE environment variables in a convenient table view, mask sensitive values to prevent accidental exposure during screen sharing, and compare or convert between different environment configs (dev/staging/production).

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Paste .env file contents into the text input, or drag and drop the file.
2. Edit, add, or delete key-value pairs in the table view.
3. Select the output format (dotenv, JSON, YAML, shell export, etc.).
4. Copy the converted result or download it as a file.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Mask API keys and passwords using the masking feature to keep them hidden during screen sharing.
When creating .env.example files, use empty values with descriptive comments and verify the format here.
Convert to Docker Compose environment variable format to use directly in your docker-compose.yml.

items.q: Are entered environment variable values sent to a server?
items.a: No. All processing happens in the browser only — nothing is transmitted to any server. Sensitive values like API keys and passwords can be handled safely.

items.q: How are comments (lines starting with #) handled?
items.a: Lines starting with # are recognized as comments and displayed separately. Comments are excluded in JSON conversion but preserved in dotenv conversion.

items.q: Are multiline values (containing newlines) supported?
items.a: Yes, multiline values enclosed in quotes (e.g., PEM certificates) can be parsed. However, newline handling may differ across output formats — verify after conversion.

syntax.title: Basic Syntax
items: # Heading 1 ~ ###### Heading 6
**Bold**, *Italic*, ~~Strikethrough~~
- List, 1. Ordered list, - [ ] Task list
[Link](URL), ![Image](URL)

advanced.title: Advanced Syntax
items: ```language for code blocks
| Col1 | Col2 | for tables
> Blockquote, --- Horizontal rule
Empty lines separate paragraphs

whatIs.title: What is this tool?
whatIs.description: The Markdown Editor lets you write Markdown with real-time preview. It includes a formatting toolbar, auto-generated table of contents (TOC), and file upload support for quickly creating GitHub READMEs, blog posts, and technical documentation.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Write Markdown text in the left editor panel. Use toolbar buttons for bold, italic, links, etc.
View the real-time rendered output in the right preview panel.
Use the TOC feature to auto-generate a heading-based table of contents.
Copy the Markdown source or HTML when done.

tips.title: Tips
items: Wrap code in triple backticks with a language name for syntax highlighting.
Upload existing .md files to edit them directly.
Use checkboxes (- [ ]) and tables (| | |) for well-structured GitHub READMEs.

items.q: What is Markdown?
items.a: Markdown is a lightweight markup language using simple symbols (#, *, - etc.) for formatting. It's widely used on GitHub, Notion, blogs, and more.

items.q: Is content auto-saved?
items.a: Auto-save is not currently available. Please copy or download your work regularly.

items.q: Can I insert images?
items.a: Yes, use ![alt text](image URL) syntax with externally hosted image URLs.

gifMaker.title: GIF Maker
gifMaker.description: Create animated GIFs from multiple images.
gifMaker.dragDrop: Drag or click to upload images

howTo.title: How to Use
items: Upload multiple images
Drag to reorder frames
Set frame delay and dimensions
Click Generate to create and download the GIF

whatIs.title: What is GIF Maker?
whatIs.description: A tool that creates animated GIFs from multiple uploaded images. All encoding is done directly in the browser using the GIF89a format — no external server needed, keeping your images private. Use it for simple animations, meme creation, and frame sequence compositing. Adjust frame rate and loop count to create different effects.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Short frame intervals (under 100ms) create smooth animations; longer intervals (500ms+) produce a slideshow effect.
GIFs are limited to 256 colors, so simple images (logos, illustrations) render better quality than photos.
Large images produce large GIF files — resize to under 500px width before converting for easier sharing.

items.q: How many images can I add?
items.a: You can add images up to your browser's memory limit. Staying under 30–50 frames is generally stable. More frames mean larger file sizes, which can be inconvenient to share.

items.q: Can I make a GIF with a transparent background?
items.a: Using PNG images with transparent backgrounds preserves transparency in the GIF. Note that GIF only supports fully transparent or fully opaque — semi-transparency cannot be accurately represented.

items.q: Where can I use the created GIF?
items.a: Use on any platform that supports GIF: KakaoTalk, email, Slack, websites. Great for social media memes, product demo animations, and sticker creation.

backgroundRemover.title: Background Remover
backgroundRemover.description: Remove image backgrounds using color-based chroma key. Processed entirely in your browser — no server upload required.
backgroundRemover.preview: Background removed preview will appear here
backgroundRemover.clickToSelect: Picking color — click on background area...
backgroundRemover.dragDrop: Drag or click to upload (PNG, JPG, WEBP)

howToUse.title: How to Use
steps: Upload your image by dragging it onto the upload area or clicking to browse.
Click the 'Pick Background Color' button, then click on the background in the original image.
Adjust the tolerance slider — higher values remove more similar colors.
Use edge softening to smooth out the edges of the cutout.
When satisfied, click 'Download Transparent PNG' to save your result.

tips.title: Tips & Recommendations
items: Works best with solid-color backgrounds (white backgrounds, green screen, etc.).
Try clicking different parts of the background and adjust tolerance for better results.
Edge softening of 1–2px creates a natural-looking edge.
Lower the tolerance when the subject has similar colors to the background.
The checkerboard pattern in the result represents transparency — actual PNG will have no background.

limitations.title: Limitations & Notes
items: Gradient or patterned backgrounds are difficult to remove completely.
Color-based removal may accidentally remove parts of the subject if it shares colors with the background.
Large high-resolution images may take a few seconds to process.
Unlike AI-based tools (remove.bg), this uses simple color comparison and works best on uniform backgrounds.

whatIs.title: What is Background Remover?
whatIs.description: A browser-based tool that makes image backgrounds transparent using color-based chroma key technology. Unlike AI-powered background removal, you specify the color range directly — making it very effective for solid-color backgrounds (white, green screen, etc.). Images are never uploaded to a server, protecting your privacy. Download as a PNG with transparent background.

items.q: How is this different from AI background removal?
items.a: AI tools automatically detect object/person edges and can handle complex backgrounds but may be inaccurate. This tool uses chroma key — specialized for solid-color background removal with fine control over tolerance/range.

items.q: What if the background color is similar to the subject?
items.a: Lower the tolerance setting to minimize removal of subject areas that match the background color. For precise work, professional editing software like Photoshop is recommended.

items.q: Can it remove gradient or complex backgrounds?
items.a: Chroma key is optimized for solid-color backgrounds. It has limitations with gradients or complex patterns. For complex backgrounds, use an AI-specialized service like remove.bg.

collageMaker.title: Photo Collage
collageMaker.description: Combine multiple photos into various collage layouts.
collageMaker.dragDrop: Drag or click to upload photos

howTo.title: How to Use
items: Upload multiple photos
Choose a layout template
Adjust spacing and background color
Download the finished collage

whatIs.title: What is Photo Collage?
whatIs.description: A browser-based collage tool that combines multiple photos into a single image using various grid layout templates. Choose from 5 layout templates, adjust photo order, set gaps between images, and download the finished collage. Perfect for SNS posts, photo memory organization, and product comparison images.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Photos with similar color tones or mood placed together create a cohesive, unified collage.
For Instagram posts, save in square (1:1) or portrait (4:5) ratio for optimal display.
Setting the gap (spacing) to 0 creates a seamless, clean grid-style result.

items.q: How many photos can I combine?
items.a: Depending on the layout, 2–6 photos can be combined. For more photos, create multiple collages and combine them in a subsequent step.

items.q: What resolution is the output?
items.a: The original photo resolution is maintained in the composite. Using high-resolution photos produces a collage suitable for printing.

items.q: Can I add text or stickers to the collage?
items.a: Currently only photo composition is supported. For text or sticker additions, do further editing in an image editor after exporting the collage.

fifteenPuzzle.title: 15 Puzzle
fifteenPuzzle.description: Slide numbered tiles to arrange them in order.

rules.title: Rules
items: Click a tile next to the empty space to slide it
Arrange numbers 1 to N in order to win
Try to finish in the fewest moves possible

whatIs.title: What is the 15 Puzzle?
whatIs.description: A classic sliding puzzle game where you push numbered tiles (1–15) into the empty space on a 4×4 grid to arrange them in order. Invented in the 1880s, this puzzle develops spatial reasoning and logical thinking. Choose grid sizes from 3×3 (8-puzzle) to 5×5, and challenge yourself to solve it in the fewest moves.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select difficulty — 3×3 (easy), 4×4 (medium), 5×5 (hard).
2. Click or drag a tile adjacent to the empty space to slide it in that direction.
3. You win when all numbers are in order (1–15) with the empty space in the bottom-right.
4. Minimize your move count and time to set a new best record.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The basic 'row/column' strategy: complete the top row first (1,2,3,4), then the first column.
The final 2×2 corner (13,14,15) requires a special rotation technique — look up a 15-puzzle tutorial on YouTube.
If more than half the tiles appear severely scrambled, the puzzle might seem unsolvable — but this game only generates solvable configurations.

items.q: Can an unsolvable configuration appear?
items.a: No. This game only generates solvable configurations using a parity-checking algorithm to filter out impossible arrangements.

items.q: How do I find the optimal (minimum move) solution?
items.a: The optimal solution for 15-puzzle can be found with A* or IDA* algorithms, but the computation is very complex. Under 80–100 moves is generally considered a skilled solve.

items.q: Can I play with the keyboard?
items.a: Yes, use arrow keys (↑↓←→) to slide tiles adjacent to the empty space. Keyboard control can be faster and more precise than clicking.

rules.title: Controls
items: Click/tap to make the bird fly up
Pass through pipes to score points
Hit a pipe or the ground and it's game over!

whatIs.title: What is Flappy Bird?
whatIs.description: A one-tap arcade game where tapping the screen makes the bird flap upward, and releasing causes it to fall with gravity. Each time you pass through the gap between pipes, you score a point. Touching a pipe or the ground ends the game. Simple but intensely addictive, this reaction-speed game will keep you coming back for a higher score.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Click the screen or press Space bar to start the game and make the bird flap.
2. Each pipe gap you pass through scores 1 point.
3. Touching a pipe or falling to the ground is game over — click to restart.
4. Tap in a rhythmic pattern to maintain a consistent altitude.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Aim for the middle of each pipe gap — this gives you equal margin above and below.
Flying too high or too low is dangerous — practice staying in the middle third of the screen.
A steady, consistent tap rhythm is more stable than rapid bursts.

items.q: What score is considered good?
items.a: 10+ is intermediate, 30+ is advanced, 50+ is highly skilled. Even 5 points can be challenging at first — don't give up!

items.q: Does it work on touchscreen devices?
items.a: Yes, touch input is supported. Play on smartphones or tablets by tapping the screen.

items.q: Is my high score saved?
items.a: Yes, your best score (Best Score) is saved in browser localStorage. It persists between sessions so you can track your improvement.

hangman.title: Hangman
hangman.description: Guess Korean words letter by letter.

rules.title: Rules
items: Guess the hidden Korean word
Press consonant and vowel buttons to select letters
Wrong guesses reduce your remaining tries
Guess the word before running out of tries

whatIs.title: What is Hangman (Word Guessing)?
whatIs.description: A traditional word-guessing game where you reveal a hidden Korean word one character (jamo) at a time. Each wrong guess adds a piece to the gallows drawing, and you must complete the word before the figure is fully drawn. The game uses correct Korean jamo (consonant/vowel) decomposition to handle Korean script properly, making it a fun way to build vocabulary.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. When the game starts, the hidden word is shown as blank spaces indicating its length.
2. Click consonant/vowel buttons on screen or type a character on the keyboard.
3. Correct characters appear in their position; wrong guesses add to the gallows drawing.
4. Keep guessing until you complete the word or fail 7 times.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: In Korean, frequently occurring jamo like 'ㅇ', 'ㄴ', 'ㅣ', 'ㅏ' are good first guesses.
Use the word length and revealed characters to guess the part of speech (noun, verb, etc.).
When a category hint (food, animals, countries) is shown, focus guesses on vocabulary from that category.

items.q: How do I adjust word difficulty?
items.a: Select Easy/Normal/Hard difficulty levels — ranging from everyday vocabulary to challenging words.

items.q: How many wrong guesses are allowed?
items.a: 7 wrong guesses are allowed by default (head, body, 2 arms, 2 legs = 7 stages). This can be changed in settings.

items.q: Can I see the correct answer after losing?
items.a: Yes, the answer is revealed after game over. Use it as a natural vocabulary learning opportunity.

whatIs.title: What is Hangman (Word Guessing)?
whatIs.description: A traditional word-guessing game where you reveal a hidden Korean word one character (jamo) at a time. Each wrong guess adds a piece to the gallows drawing, and you must complete the word before the figure is fully drawn. The game uses correct Korean jamo (consonant/vowel) decomposition to handle Korean script properly, making it a fun way to build vocabulary.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. When the game starts, the hidden word is shown as blank spaces indicating its length.
2. Click consonant/vowel buttons on screen or type a character on the keyboard.
3. Correct characters appear in their position; wrong guesses add to the gallows drawing.
4. Keep guessing until you complete the word or fail 7 times.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: In Korean, frequently occurring jamo like 'ㅇ', 'ㄴ', 'ㅣ', 'ㅏ' are good first guesses.
Use the word length and revealed characters to guess the part of speech (noun, verb, etc.).
When a category hint (food, animals, countries) is shown, focus guesses on vocabulary from that category.

items.q: How do I adjust word difficulty?
items.a: Select Easy/Normal/Hard difficulty levels — ranging from everyday vocabulary to challenging words.

items.q: How many wrong guesses are allowed?
items.a: 7 wrong guesses are allowed by default (head, body, 2 arms, 2 legs = 7 stages). This can be changed in settings.

items.q: Can I see the correct answer after losing?
items.a: Yes, the answer is revealed after game over. Use it as a natural vocabulary learning opportunity.

pacMan.title: Pac-Man
pacMan.description: Classic arcade maze game eating dots and avoiding ghosts.

rules.title: Controls
items: Use arrow keys to move Pac-Man
Eat all dots to clear the level
Getting caught by ghosts costs a life
Eat power pellets to temporarily eat ghosts

whatIs.title: What is Pac-Man?
whatIs.description: The classic arcade game developed by Namco in 1980, where you navigate a maze eating dots while evading 4 ghosts. Eating a power pellet (large dot) lets you turn the tables and eat the ghosts. Each of the 4 ghosts (Blinky/Pinky/Inky/Clyde) uses a different AI strategy, and understanding their patterns is key to achieving high scores.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Use arrow keys (↑↓←→) or WASD to control Pac-Man. On mobile, swipe to change direction.
2. Eat all dots (·) in the maze to advance to the next stage.
3. Eat a power pellet (●) in the corners to turn ghosts blue and eat them.
4. Touching a ghost loses one life; losing all 3 lives is game over.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Ghosts predict Pac-Man's direction at intersections — unpredictable direction changes are effective.
Power pellet effects are short, so eat them when multiple ghosts are clustered together for combo points.
Using the maze edge tunnels makes it easier to lose pursuing ghosts.

items.q: How are the 4 ghosts' AI strategies different?
items.a: Blinky (red) directly chases Pac-Man. Pinky (pink) targets 4 tiles ahead. Inky (cyan) uses a complex calculated path. Clyde (orange) chases from far away but retreats when close.

items.q: How does the game get harder as stages progress?
items.a: Ghost movement speed and aggression increase, and power pellet effect duration shortens. At high stages, power pellets barely affect the ghosts.

items.q: Is my high score saved?
items.a: Yes, the high score is saved in browser localStorage and persists after closing the page.

llmTokenCalculator.title: LLM Token Calculator
llmTokenCalculator.description: Estimate LLM token counts and calculate API call costs.
llmTokenCalculator.inputPlaceholder: Enter or paste text to calculate token count...
llmTokenCalculator.fileUploadDesc: Drag or click to upload .txt, .md, .json, .csv files
llmTokenCalculator.noText: Enter text to see token count.
llmTokenCalculator.koreanNote: Korean text uses ~1.5-3x more tokens than English.

whatIsToken.title: What is a Token?
items: Tokens are the basic units LLMs use to process text
In English, ~4 characters ≈ 1 token; in Korean, ~1-2 characters ≈ 1 token
The same text may have different token counts depending on the model's tokenizer
Special characters, spaces, and line breaks also count as tokens

koreanTokens.title: Korean Token Characteristics
items: Korean is less token-efficient than English, consuming more tokens
Claude has relatively good Korean token efficiency
Frequently used Korean words require fewer tokens
Mixing English can reduce overall token count

costTips.title: Cost Saving Tips
items: Writing concise prompts reduces input token usage
System prompts consume tokens on every request — optimize them
Setting appropriate max_tokens saves unnecessary output costs
Batch APIs can save up to 50% on costs (OpenAI, Anthropic)

whatIs.title: What is the LLM Token Calculator?
whatIs.description: The LLM Token Calculator estimates how many tokens your text consumes when processed by AI language models like ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini, and calculates the resulting API call cost. It helps developers and businesses forecast costs before building AI-powered applications and budget projects accordingly. Compare pricing across different models at a glance to choose the most cost-effective option.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Paste or type the text you want to tokenize into the input box.
2. Select the AI model (GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, Gemini, etc.) you plan to use.
3. Set the ratio of input (prompt) tokens to output (completion) tokens.
4. Enter your expected number of API calls to see the estimated monthly cost.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Non-English languages (Korean, Japanese, Chinese) are less token-efficient than English. Korean text typically uses 1.5–2× more tokens for the same content.
System prompts are sent on every API call. Keeping them concise can significantly reduce your monthly bill.
Lightweight models like GPT-4o mini or Claude Haiku are 10–20× cheaper and sufficient for simple tasks.
Output tokens usually cost 2–3× more than input tokens. Use max_tokens to cap response length.

items.q: How accurate is the token estimate?
items.a: The tool provides estimates. Actual token counts depend on the model's specific tokenizer (e.g., tiktoken) and may vary by ±10%.

items.q: What is a context window?
items.a: The maximum number of tokens a model can process at once. GPT-4o supports 128K and Claude 3.5 supports 200K tokens. Content exceeding this limit gets truncated.

items.q: Can I calculate embedding token costs too?
items.a: Embedding APIs use a separate pricing structure. This tool calculates costs for text generation (Chat Completions) API calls only.

webserverConfig.title: Web Server Config Generator
webserverConfig.description: Generate Nginx and Caddy config files with best-practice presets.
webserverConfig.analyzeDesc: Paste your Nginx or Caddy config file to analyze it automatically.
webserverConfig.analyzePlaceholder: Paste your Nginx or Caddy configuration here...
webserverConfig.analyzePrompt: Paste a config file to analyze security and performance.

analysisAdvice.ssl: Enable HTTPS to encrypt data transmission. Get free certificates from Let's Encrypt.
analysisAdvice.http2: Enable HTTP/2 to improve page loading speed.
analysisAdvice.gzip: Enable Gzip compression to reduce transfer size by 60-80%.
analysisAdvice.securityHeaders: Add security headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options) to prevent XSS and clickjacking.
analysisAdvice.hsts: Add HSTS header to force browsers to always use HTTPS.
analysisAdvice.serverTokens: Set server_tokens off to hide the Nginx version number.
analysisAdvice.sslProtocols: Disable old protocols (TLSv1.0, SSLv3) — use TLSv1.2+ only for security.
analysisAdvice.cors: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * allows all origins. Restrict to specific domains for safety.

presets.fileServerDesc: Directory listing + large file download optimization

nginx.title: Nginx Tips
items: Save config files in /etc/nginx/conf.d/ or /etc/nginx/sites-available/
After changes, run nginx -t to validate, then systemctl reload nginx
Adjust worker_connections and worker_processes based on server specs
Get free SSL certificates with Let's Encrypt (use certbot)

caddy.title: Caddy Tips
items: Caddy has automatic HTTPS by default — minimal SSL config needed
Caddyfile goes in /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
After changes, run systemctl reload caddy
Use reverse_proxy directive for simple proxy setup

security.title: Security Best Practices
items: Always use HTTPS and set up HTTP→HTTPS redirects
Add security headers (X-Frame-Options, CSP) to prevent XSS, clickjacking
Use server_tokens off to hide server version (Nginx)
Block unnecessary HTTP methods (TRACE, DELETE, etc.)

whatIs.title: What is the Web Server Config Generator?
whatIs.description: The Web Server Config Generator automatically creates Nginx and Caddy configuration files using best-practice presets for common use cases. Generate reverse proxy, SSL/TLS, static file serving, and security header configurations in just a few clicks. Even developers unfamiliar with server configuration can instantly obtain safe, optimized settings.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your web server type (Nginx or Caddy).
2. Choose your use case (reverse proxy, static files, PHP, Node.js, etc.).
3. Enter basic information such as domain name and port number.
4. Copy the generated configuration file and paste it into your server's config directory.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Caddy automatically provisions and renews Let's Encrypt SSL certificates. For small projects, Caddy is the simpler choice.
In Nginx reverse proxy configs, the presence or absence of a trailing slash in proxy_pass significantly affects URL routing. Always review the comments in the generated config.
Security headers (X-Frame-Options, CSP, HSTS, etc.) are included by default. Adjust the CSP policy to match your service's requirements.
Always validate your configuration with nginx -t before applying changes.

items.q: Should I choose Nginx or Caddy?
items.a: Choose Nginx for high traffic and fine-grained control. Choose Caddy for quick setup and automatic SSL. Nginx has a larger ecosystem, but Caddy's configuration is significantly simpler.

items.q: Is it safe to use the generated config as-is?
items.a: The config reflects security best practices, but production environments may need additional tuning. Especially CSP, CORS, and rate limiting should be customized for your service.

items.q: How do I enable HTTP/2 or HTTP/3?
items.a: For Nginx, add listen 443 ssl http2; to your config. Caddy supports both HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 automatically by default.

housingSubscription.homelessStartTooltip: Enter the date you became homeless. If you previously owned a home, enter the date you disposed of it.
housingSubscription.homelessYearsTooltip: Enter the homeless period directly in years. Decimal values accepted (e.g., 2.5)
housingSubscription.birthDateTooltip: Unmarried individuals under 30 receive 0 points for homeless period. Your birth date is used to determine this automatically.
housingSubscription.marriedTooltip: Married applicants can earn homeless period points regardless of age.
housingSubscription.under30Notice: Unmarried applicants under 30 receive 0 points for the homeless period.
housingSubscription.dependentTooltip: Includes spouse, lineal ascendants (parents, grandparents), and lineal descendants (children, grandchildren) who have been registered in the same household for 3+ years. Self is excluded.

guideAItems: Homeless period points apply from age 30 and above
Married applicants count from marriage registration date or homeless start date
Under 1 year = 2 pts; increases by 2 pts per year; max 32 pts at 15+ years
guideBItems: Excludes self; includes spouse, lineal ascendants, and lineal descendants
Lineal ascendants: parents, grandparents (must be on same household register for 3+ years)
Lineal descendants: children, grandchildren (unmarried children have no time requirement)
0 persons = 5 pts; +5 pts per person; max 35 pts at 6+ persons
guideCItems: All types accepted: savings, comprehensive savings, deposit, installment
Under 6 months = 1 pt; 6 mo–1 yr = 2 pts; +1 pt per year thereafter
Converted or transferred accounts count the combined duration

capitalGainsTax.title: Capital Gains Tax Calculator

capitalGainsTax.guideTitle: How Capital Gains Tax is Calculated
guideSteps: Capital Gain = Sale Price − Acquisition Price − Expenses
Apply long-term holding deduction (if held 3+ years)
Taxable Gain = Capital Gain − Long-Term Deduction
Tax Base = Taxable Gain − Basic Deduction
Calculated Tax = Tax Base × Rate − Progressive Deduction
Total Tax = Calculated Tax + Local Income Tax
guideExemptItems: Regulated area: also requires 2+ years of actual residence
Sale price ≤ KRW 1.2 billion: fully exempt
Sale price > KRW 1.2 billion: only the excess portion is taxed
Only applies when a household owns exactly one home

averageCalc.title: Average Calculator
averageCalc.description: Calculate 4 types of averages and basic statistics
averageCalc.inputPlaceholder:
Enter numbers separated by commas or newlines
e.g. 85, 90, 78, 92
averageCalc.geometricDesc: nth root of product of n values

arithmetic.title: Arithmetic Mean
items: Most common average
Sum all values and divide by count
Sensitive to outliers

weighted.title: Weighted Mean
items: When values have different importance
Used for GPA calculation
Weighted survey analysis

geometric.title: Geometric Mean
items: Best for growth rates, returns
Only positive numbers
Essential for compound returns

harmonic.title: Harmonic Mean
items: Best for rates and ratios
Average speed over same distance
Cannot include zero

whatIs.title: What is the Average Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Average Calculator computes all four types of averages—arithmetic, weighted, geometric, and harmonic—along with basic statistics like standard deviation, variance, median, and mode in one place. It is useful for grade calculations, investment return analysis, speed averaging, and many other real-world scenarios. The tool also helps you decide which type of average is appropriate for your data.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter numbers separated by commas, spaces, or line breaks.
2. For weighted average, also enter corresponding weights for each number.
3. Select the average type (arithmetic, weighted, geometric, or harmonic).
4. View the calculated result along with additional statistics like standard deviation and median.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Use weighted average when items have different importance, such as course grades weighted by credit hours.
Geometric mean is the right choice for averaging ratios and growth rates, such as compound annual growth rates (CAGR).
Harmonic mean is correct for averaging rates with an inverse relationship, such as speed, unit prices, or resistance.
When outliers are present, the median is more representative than the arithmetic mean.

items.q: Which is more accurate: arithmetic mean or median?
items.a: If the data has no extreme values, arithmetic mean works well. For skewed distributions like income or real estate prices, the median is more representative.

items.q: When should I use geometric mean?
items.a: Use it for investment returns, population growth rates, or any continuous change expressed as ratios or multiples over time.

items.q: Can I calculate with negative numbers or zeros?
items.a: Arithmetic and weighted averages work with negatives and zeros. Geometric mean requires positive numbers only. Harmonic mean cannot be calculated if zero is included.

gradeCalc.title: Grade/Rank Calculator
gradeCalc.description: Calculate grade (9-tier), percentile from rank and total students

gradeSystem.title: 9-Grade System
items: Grade 1: Top 4% (highest)
Grade 2: Top 4~11%
Grade 3: Top 11~23%
Grade 4: Top 23~40%
Grade 5: Top 40~60% (middle)
Grades 6-9: Bottom 40%

usage.title: Usage
items: College admission: Calculate transcript grades
Mock exams: Raw score → rank → grade
Rank percentile = rank/total × 100

whatIs.title: What is the Grade/Rank Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Grade/Rank Calculator automatically computes your Korean school grade (1st through 9th) and percentile from your rank and total number of test-takers. It is ideal for high school GPA management, university admissions planning, and analyzing relative performance on norm-referenced tests. It also reverse-calculates the rank range needed to reach a target grade.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your rank (position in class or exam).
2. Enter the total number of students or test-takers.
3. Click Calculate to instantly see your grade and percentile.
4. Use the reverse calculation to find what rank you need for a target grade.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Grade 1 is the top 4%, Grade 2 is 4–11%, and Grade 3 is 11–23% by percentile cutoff.
When multiple students share the same score, they receive the same rank. The worst-case rank is used for grade boundary calculation.
CSAT (수능) grades use standard scores and percentiles, which differ from the school GPA grade method used here.
If you only know your score, not your rank, you can estimate your percentile using the class average and standard deviation.

items.q: Can I compare grades between subjects with different class sizes?
items.a: Since grades are determined by relative percentage, the same grade represents the same relative standing regardless of class size. Percentile provides the most intuitive comparison.

items.q: How do school grades affect university admissions?
items.a: Grade-based admissions use the average grade across subjects. Each university has different subject weighting policies, so check each school's admissions criteria.

items.q: Where exactly is the boundary between Grade 1 and Grade 2?
items.a: The top 4% earns Grade 1. For example, ranks 1–4 out of 100 students are Grade 1, ranks 5–11 are Grade 2. Fractional boundaries are rounded up.

whatIs.title: What is the GPA Converter?
whatIs.description: The GPA Converter converts grades between 4.3 and 4.5 GPA scales and also converts them to a 100-point percentage scale. It is useful when applying to graduate schools, studying abroad, or applying for jobs that require a different grading scale. It supports both letter grades (A+, A0, B+) and numeric GPA input.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your current GPA scale (4.3 or 4.5 maximum).
2. Enter the GPA as a number (e.g., 3.8) or letter grade (e.g., A+).
3. View the converted GPA in the other scale and the equivalent 100-point score.
4. For batch conversion, enter multiple course grades to get an overall converted GPA.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The 4.3 scale uses A+(4.3), A0(4.0), B+(3.3), while 4.5 uses A+(4.5), A0(4.0), B+(3.5). Differences appear at A0 and below.
Many US graduate programs use a 4.0 scale. A rough 4.5→4.0 conversion can be done by multiplying by 4.0/4.5.
Each institution has its own 100-point conversion table. Check the scale on your official transcript and disclose it to the receiving institution.
Highlighting strong grades in core major courses can strengthen your application even if your overall GPA is moderate.

items.q: Is a 4.3-scale 3.5 the same level as a 4.5-scale 3.5?
items.a: No. A 3.5 on a 4.3 scale is about 81.4%, while 3.5 on a 4.5 scale is about 77.8%. The same number means different things depending on the scale.

items.q: How are Pass/Fail courses handled?
items.a: P/F courses are typically excluded from GPA calculation. They are recorded as completion only and do not affect the grade average.

items.q: Is there an internationally recognized conversion standard?
items.a: There is no single universal standard. Services like WES (World Education Services) provide internationally recognized conversions. For important applications, using an official credential evaluation service is recommended.

whatIs.title: What is the GPA Converter?
whatIs.description: The GPA Converter converts grades between 4.3 and 4.5 GPA scales and also converts them to a 100-point percentage scale. It is useful when applying to graduate schools, studying abroad, or applying for jobs that require a different grading scale. It supports both letter grades (A+, A0, B+) and numeric GPA input.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your current GPA scale (4.3 or 4.5 maximum).
2. Enter the GPA as a number (e.g., 3.8) or letter grade (e.g., A+).
3. View the converted GPA in the other scale and the equivalent 100-point score.
4. For batch conversion, enter multiple course grades to get an overall converted GPA.

items.a: P/F courses are typically excluded from GPA calculation. They are recorded as completion only and do not affect the grade average.

pcElectricity.title: PC Electricity Calculator
pcElectricity.description: Calculate monthly/yearly electricity cost from PC component power consumption

section1.title: Power Consumption Basis
items: TDP approximates maximum power consumption
Actual consumption varies by load level
Gaming: ~85%, Normal: ~50%, Idle: ~20% of max

section2.title: Power Saving Tips
items: Turn off when not in use — standby power adds up
Use high-efficiency PSU (80 Plus Gold or above)
Lower monitor brightness — saves 10~20% power
Undervolting GPU reduces power consumption

whatIs.title: What is the PC Electricity Cost Calculator?
whatIs.description: The PC Electricity Cost Calculator estimates monthly and annual electricity bills by inputting the power consumption (watts) of each PC component—CPU, GPU, memory, storage, and more. It applies Korean residential and commercial electricity rate tiers to produce estimates close to your actual bill. It is also useful for calculating savings when upgrading to more energy-efficient components.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the power consumption (W) for each PC component: CPU, GPU, motherboard, etc.
2. Set the average daily usage hours and load profile (regular use, gaming, rendering, etc.).
3. Select residential or commercial electricity rate plan.
4. View the estimated monthly and annual electricity cost along with total kWh usage.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The GPU is the largest power consumer in a gaming PC. A high-end GPU under full load can draw more power than all other components combined.
Real-world power draw is often lower than the TDP rating. Use monitoring tools like GPU-Z or HWiNFO64 to measure actual consumption for greater accuracy.
Korean electricity uses a tiered (progressive) rate. Exceeding 200 kWh/month significantly increases the per-unit rate, so be sure to check your billing tier.
Standby power consumption also adds up to thousands of won per year. Get into the habit of using sleep mode or full shutdown when not in use.

items.q: Is the PSU wattage rating the same as power consumption?
items.a: No. The PSU's rated wattage is the maximum it can supply. Actual consumption is the sum of what components are drawing at any given moment. Use the sum of component TDPs as your baseline.

items.q: Does the result include VAT?
items.a: Yes. The calculated cost includes VAT (10%) and the electricity industry infrastructure fund (3.7%), representing the final amount you would pay on your bill.

items.q: Can I calculate electricity costs for a crypto mining rig?
items.a: Yes. Set usage to 24 hours a day at full load to simulate mining conditions. Also check whether a commercial electricity plan applies to your situation.

salesCommissionCalc.title: Sales Commission Calculator
salesCommissionCalc.description: Compare Coupang, SmartStore, and 11st commission rates by category

result.noInput: Enter selling price to compare fees across 3 platforms

notes.title: Notes
items: Coupang: Commission on total payment (incl. shipping), monthly fee 55K (if sales >1M)
SmartStore: No sales commission on shipping, only NaverPay fee
11st: Rates vary by sub-category and seller grade, shown rates are averages
Actual rates may differ based on sub-category, seller grade, and promotions

commission.title: Fee Structure
items: Coupang: 4~10.9% sales fee + 55K monthly
SmartStore: NaverPay (1.98~3.63%) + Sales (2.73%)
11st: 5~12% by category, tiered by seller grade

settlement.title: Settlement Calculation
items: Settlement = Price + Shipping - Commission
Coupang applies commission on shipping-inclusive amount
SmartStore/11st apply commission on selling price only

tips.title: Fee Reduction Tips
items: Electronics: Coupang lowest at 5%
Books: SmartStore lowest at 3%
List on multiple platforms and optimize by category

whatIs.title: What is the Sales Commission Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Sales Commission Calculator compares and calculates the category-specific selling fees for major Korean e-commerce platforms including Coupang, Naver Smart Store, and 11Street. Enter a selling price to instantly see the fee deduction and net settlement amount, helping online sellers set prices and choose the most profitable sales channel. Rates are updated for 2025.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the selling price of your product.
2. Select the platform (Coupang, Smart Store, 11Street, etc.) and product category.
3. Choose your shipping policy (free shipping or buyer-paid shipping).
4. Compare commission amounts and net settlement amounts across platforms in a side-by-side table.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Commission rates vary widely by platform and category. Fashion accessories often do better on Smart Store, while electronics can favor Coupang—always compare before listing.
Coupang Rocket Delivery (consignment) has higher fees than standard marketplace, but the increase in exposure can outweigh the cost. Compare total profitability.
Check whether VAT is included or excluded. If the listed commission rate is VAT-exclusive, your actual fee burden is 10% higher.
Some platforms offer volume discounts above certain monthly sales thresholds. Take advantage of partner benefits once you qualify.

items.q: Are advertising costs included in the commission?
items.a: No. This tool calculates selling commissions only. Keyword ad costs (CPC) and display advertising fees are separate and should be factored into your true profitability analysis.

items.q: Do settlement periods differ by platform?
items.a: Yes. Coupang settles weekly (Tuesdays), Smart Store settles 2–3 business days after purchase confirmation, and 11Street settles twice a month. Plan your cash flow accordingly.

items.q: What happens to commissions on returns or cancellations?
items.a: Cancellations before purchase confirmation incur no commission. For returns after confirmation, platform policies vary—some refund part or all of the commission.
