acquisitionTaxCalc.title: Acquisition Tax Calculator
acquisitionTaxCalc.description: Auto-calculate real estate acquisition tax, special tax, and education tax (2025)

result.title: Results
result.heavyWarning: Heavy tax rate applied. Multi-home owners in speculative areas may face significantly higher acquisition tax.

acquisitionTaxCalc.disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Verify actual amounts with your local government.

acquisitionTax.title: Housing Acquisition Tax Rates
items: Speculative zone 2 houses: 8%
Speculative zone 3+ houses: 12%
Non-speculative 2 houses: 1~3%, 3+ houses: 8%
Corporation: 12% (regardless of zone)

specialTax.title: Rural Development Tax
items: Exclusive ≤85㎡: Exempt
Exclusive >85㎡: 10% of acquisition tax
Land/Commercial: 10% of acquisition tax

educationTax.title: Local Education Tax
items: Standard: 10% of acquisition tax
Same ratio applies for surcharge cases

tips.title: Tax Saving Tips
items: Check first-time homebuyer reduction benefits
Zone deregulation removes surcharge — check zone status
1 house under 600M has lowest tax burden

whatIs.title: What is the Acquisition Tax Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Acquisition Tax Calculator automatically computes acquisition tax (취득세), agricultural special tax (농어촌특별세), and local education tax (지방교육세) for real estate purchases using 2025 tax rates. It accounts for multi-home surcharges, first-time buyer discounts, and temporary two-home exemptions, giving you an accurate picture of total tax liability before signing a contract.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the property type (apartment, detached house, land, commercial, etc.).
2. Enter the acquisition price (actual transaction price or assessed value).
3. Enter your current number of homes and your count after this purchase.
4. Set additional conditions (first-time buyer, regulated area, etc.) to get the final tax amount.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: First-time homebuyers can receive an acquisition tax reduction (up to 2 million KRW). Apply within 60 days of the acquisition date.
Temporary two-home owners who sell their existing home within 3 years (2 years in regulated areas) qualify for the standard rate instead of the surcharge.
For gift or inheritance acquisitions, the tax base is the market value, not the assessed price. Gift acquisition tax uses market value.
The acquisition tax filing deadline is 60 days from the acquisition date (balance payment date). Late filing triggers additional penalties.

items.q: Do subscription rights (분양권) count toward housing for acquisition tax?
items.a: Yes, since August 2020, subscription rights are included in the housing count for acquisition tax surcharge purposes. The tax itself is paid upon final balance payment based on the completed home.

items.q: Is the tax rate different for corporations purchasing homes?
items.a: Yes. Corporations pay a 12% surcharge regardless of the number of homes held, with limited exceptions for public interest entities.

items.q: Where do I file the acquisition tax return?
items.a: File at the city/county/district tax office where the property is located, or online at Wetax (wetax.go.kr).

dsrCalc.title: DSR Calculator
dsrCalc.description: Auto-calculate Debt Service Ratio (DSR) based on 2025 regulations

result.title: Results
result.cautionDesc: DSR 40~50% — Bank loan difficult, non-bank possible

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.text: This calculator is for reference only. Actual loan approval depends on financial institution criteria.

whatIsDsr.title: What is DSR?
items: DSR = Annual total loan repayment / Annual income × 100
Banks: DSR ≤40%, Non-banks: ≤50%
Applies when total loans exceed 200M KRW
Excludes: jeonse loans, loans under 3M, insurance loans

stressDsr.title: Stress DSR (2025 Phase 3)
items: Variable rate: +1.50%p surcharge
Fixed rate: No surcharge
Non-capital mortgages: 0.75%p temporary rate

tips.title: Tips to Increase Loan Limit
items: Fixed rate = no stress surcharge
Longer term = lower monthly payment = lower DSR
Personal loans count as 5yr, card loans as 3yr — reducing balance helps
Credit lines count at full limit — consider reducing

whatIs.title: What is the DSR Calculator?
whatIs.description: The DSR (Debt Service Ratio) Calculator computes the ratio of all loan principal and interest payments to annual income, using the strengthened Stress DSR Phase 2 rules effective in 2025. It covers mortgage loans, credit loans, and other debts to help you determine loan eligibility and maximum borrowing capacity before applying. Essential for home purchase planning.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your annual income (pre-tax salary, business income, etc.).
2. Enter details for existing loans (mortgage, credit loans, auto installments)—balance, rate, and remaining term.
3. Enter the amount, rate, and term for the new loan you want to take out.
4. Select whether to apply the stress rate add-on to see your DSR ratio and maximum loan amount.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The DSR 40% cap means annual principal and interest payments on all loans cannot exceed 40% of your annual income at most banks.
Stress DSR Phase 2 (effective September 2024) adds up to 1.5%p to the actual rate for a more conservative limit calculation.
Credit loans are converted to a 10-year term for DSR purposes, making even short-term loans carry a large annualized burden.
Including a spouse's income increases the DSR limit. Joint mortgages use combined household income.

items.q: What is the difference between DSR and DTI?
items.a: DTI (Debt-to-Income ratio) only counts principal and interest on the specific mortgage being applied for, while DSR sums up all loan payments. DSR is the stricter standard.

items.q: Is jeonse loan (전세대출) included in DSR?
items.a: Jeonse loans count only their interest (not principal) in DSR calculation, making them carry less DSR burden than a mortgage.

items.q: Are there loans exempt from DSR regulation?
items.a: Social finance products (Sunshine Loan, New Hope Holssi, etc.), government welfare loans, and small loans under 100 million KRW are exempt. Some non-bank lenders also apply different standards.

marginCalc.title: Margin Calculator
marginCalc.description: Auto-calculate profit margin for online sellers

margin.title: Margin vs Markup
items: Margin = (Price - Total Cost) / Price × 100
Markup = (Price - Cost) / Cost × 100
30% margin ≈ 43% markup
Margin rate reflects actual profitability more accurately

reverse.title: Reverse Calculation
items: Enter target margin to auto-calculate minimum selling price
Accounts for commission, shipping, and VAT
Find the minimum price to avoid losses

platform.title: Platform Fees
items: Coupang: 4~10.9% by category, monthly fee 55K (if sales >1M)
SmartStore: NaverPay + sales commission ≈5.5%
11st: 5~12% by category, avg 10%
Fees based on final payment amount (incl. shipping)

tax.title: VAT Handling
items: General: ~10% of margin (sales price - cost)
Simplified: Price × sector value-added rate × 10%
Exempt: No VAT (agricultural products, basic necessities)
Simplified status available if annual sales <104M

whatIs.title: What is the Margin Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Margin Calculator helps online sellers and retailers automatically compute net profit and margin rate by factoring in product cost, selling price, platform commissions, shipping, advertising, and VAT. Use the reverse-calculation mode to find the minimum selling price needed to achieve a target margin rate—an essential tool for e-commerce pricing strategy.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the product cost (purchase price or manufacturing cost).
2. Enter the selling price and platform commission rate.
3. Add additional costs: seller-paid shipping, packaging, and advertising fees.
4. View net profit, margin rate, and markup rate. Use reverse-calculation to find the target margin price.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Margin and markup are different: margin rate is profit ÷ selling price; markup rate is profit ÷ cost. E-commerce typically uses margin rate.
When offering free shipping, include shipping costs in your cost base—overlooking shipping is a common cause of unexpected losses.
VAT-registered sellers remit 1/11 of the selling price as tax. Decide upfront whether to include or exclude VAT in listed prices.
Break-even analysis: divide your fixed costs by unit margin to find how many units you must sell to break even.

items.q: What is a good margin rate?
items.a: It depends on the industry. Online stores typically target 20–40%, food 50–60%, and apparel 50–70%. After fixed costs like advertising and labor, aim for a minimum net profit margin of 10%.

items.q: Can I factor in return rates?
items.a: Yes. Enter an expected return rate (%) to calculate effective margin accounting for return processing costs and inventory loss. Fashion categories with 20–30% return rates make this crucial.

items.q: How do I calculate margin for coupon or discount promotions?
items.a: Enter the actual amount received after discount as the selling price, or use the discount rate (%) field to auto-calculate the post-discount margin.

jeonseLoanCalc.title: Jeonse Loan Calculator
jeonseLoanCalc.description: Auto-calculate jeonse loan rates, limits, and monthly payments (2025)

result.title: Calculation Results
result.ltvWarning: Desired loan amount exceeds LTV limit
result.noResult: Enter information on the left to see results

jeonseLoanCalc.disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Please verify actual terms with your financial institution.

overview.title: Loan Type Comparison
items: General Beotimok: Income ≤50M, Capital max 120M, LTV 70%
Youth Beotimok: Age 19~34, max 150M, LTV 80%, SME discount
Newlywed Beotimok: Within 7yr marriage, Capital max 250M, LTV 80%, child discount
Commercial Bank: No income limit, max 500M, LTV 80%, higher rates

rates.title: Rate Factors
items: Income bracket: Lower income = lower rate
Deposit size: Smaller deposit = lower rate
Location: Non-capital regions get 0.2%p discount
Preferences: SME employee (-0.3%p), number of children, etc.

tips.title: Tips
items: Beotimok loans are exempt from DSR regulation
Youth should check SME discount (-0.3%p extra)
Newlywed with 3+ children: up to -0.7%p discount
Bullet repayment has lower monthly cost but higher total interest

whatIs.title: What is the Jeonse Loan Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Jeonse Loan Calculator automatically computes interest rates, loan limits, and monthly interest for Korean jeonse (deposit-based rental) loans based on 2025 guidelines. Compare products from Bokeummok (government), internet banks (Kakao, K-Bank), and commercial banks to see your actual interest burden before signing a jeonse contract.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the jeonse deposit amount and desired loan amount.
2. Select the loan product type (Bokeummok, commercial bank, internet bank) and rate type (fixed/variable).
3. Choose the loan term (in 2-year increments) and repayment method.
4. Review the monthly interest payment, total interest, and loan limit range.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The Bokeummok (보금자리론) government jeonse loan offers rates 1–2%p lower than commercial banks for qualifying borrowers. Check eligibility first.
Jeonse loans are typically interest-only, so monthly payments are lower than mortgages. However, the full principal is due in a lump sum at maturity.
Jeonse deposit insurance (HUG or SGI) affects your loan limit—with insurance you can borrow up to 80% of the deposit.
In a rising rate environment, fixed-rate loans may be preferable. Consider interest rate change risk over the 2-year contract period.

items.q: Are jeonse loans subject to DSR regulation?
items.a: Jeonse loans count only their interest portion in DSR calculation (not principal), making them carry less DSR burden than mortgages—generally easier to obtain within limits.

items.q: Is the loan automatically renewed when the jeonse contract renews?
items.a: No. You must apply for a separate loan extension review 1–2 months before the loan matures. The bank will re-evaluate your income and credit.

items.q: How do I protect against 'empty jeonse' (깡통전세) risk?
items.a: If the jeonse-to-sale-price ratio exceeds 80%, risk is high. Jeonse deposit insurance is essential, along with checking the property registry for prior liens.

incomeTaxCalc.title: Income Tax Calculator
incomeTaxCalc.description: Auto-calculate 2025 comprehensive income tax for freelancers and business owners
incomeTaxCalc.disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Please consult the NTS Hometax or a tax advisor for actual filing.
incomeTaxCalc.otherIncomeDesc: Combined if >3M annually (22% withholding per transaction)
incomeTaxCalc.withholdingTaxDesc: 3.3% of revenue (3% income tax + 0.3% local tax)
incomeTaxCalc.comparisonDesc: Compare tax amounts between simple and standard expense rates
guideExpenseItems: Simple Rate: Prior year revenue <24M (personal services), auto-calculated from revenue × rate
Standard Rate: Above simple threshold, verified major expenses + revenue × standard rate
Books: Record actual income/expenses, double-entry gets 20% bookkeeping credit (max 1M)
guideDeadlineItems: Filing period: May 1 - May 31 every year
Honest filing targets: Extended to June 30
guideTipItems: Pension + IRP up to 9M for tax credit (save up to 1.485M)
Choose favorable rate between simple and standard (without books)
National pension and health insurance are fully deductible — always input
Other income ≤3M can be separately taxed (no aggregation)

yearEndTaxCalc.title: Year-End Tax Settlement Calculator
yearEndTaxCalc.description: Automatically calculate your 2025 tax year (2026 filing) year-end settlement refund
yearEndTaxCalc.disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Please consult the NTS Hometax or a tax advisor for actual filing.
yearEndTaxCalc.prepaidTaxDesc: Total income tax withheld from monthly salary over the year
yearEndTaxCalc.cardDeductionInfo: Deduction applies to spending exceeding 25% of gross salary
yearEndTaxCalc.medicalExpenseDesc: 15% credit on amount exceeding 3% of gross salary

flow.title: Calculation Flow
items: Subtract non-taxable income from gross salary to get total salary
Apply earned income deduction to get earned income amount
Subtract income deductions to get tax base
Apply tax rates to tax base to get calculated tax
Subtract tax credits to get determined tax
Compare with prepaid tax to determine refund/additional payment

changes.title: 2025 Tax Year Key Changes
items: Child tax credit increased: 1 child 250K, 2 children 550K
Rent credit limit raised to 10M, salary threshold to 80M
Marriage tax credit 500K newly introduced (2024-2026 registration)
Housing subscription limit raised to 3M, spouse now eligible
Medical expense limit removed for children under 6
Childcare allowance non-taxable limit raised to 200K/month
Gym/swimming pool expenses now eligible for 30% card deduction

tips.title: Tax Saving Tips
items: Spending over 25% of salary: debit cards/cash receipts get 2x rate (30%)
Pension savings + IRP: up to 9M eligible for tax credit
Medical expenses: only amount exceeding 3% of salary is deductible—concentrate in one year
Traditional markets and public transport each have separate additional 1M limit

whatIs.title: What is the Year-End Tax Calculator?
whatIs.description: This tool estimates your year-end tax settlement refund (or additional payment). Enter gross salary, personal deductions, social insurance, card spending, pension savings, medical/education expenses to calculate your final tax and refund amount.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Enter basic info: gross salary, non-taxable income, dependents, prepaid tax.
Enter income deductions: social insurance, housing savings, credit/debit card spending.
Enter tax credits: pension savings/IRP, medical, education, and donation amounts.
Review taxable income, final tax amount, and estimated refund.

savingTips.title: Tips
items: Debit card/cash receipts have 30% deduction rate vs 15% for credit cards. Use credit cards up to 25% of salary threshold, then switch to debit.
Pension savings (up to 6M KRW) and IRP (up to 9M combined) qualify for 13.2-16.5% tax credit.
For dual-income couples, assign dependents to the higher earner for maximum benefit.

items.q: When do I receive my refund?
items.a: Usually reflected in February salary. Additional tax due is deducted from salary.

items.q: Income deduction vs tax credit - what's the difference?
items.a: Income deductions reduce your taxable income base, while tax credits directly reduce the calculated tax amount. Tax credits have a more direct impact.

items.q: Who qualifies as a dependent?
items.a: Spouse (income under 1M KRW), parents (age 60+), children (under 20 or disabled), siblings (under 20 or 60+, income under 1M KRW).

healthInsurance.title: Health Insurance Premium Calculator
healthInsurance.description: Calculate 2025 workplace/regional subscriber premiums, dependent eligibility, and compare plans

workplace.rateInfo: 2025 Health Insurance Rate: 7.09% (Employee 3.545% + Employer 3.545%), Long-term Care 12.95%

regional.minimumApplied: Income below threshold; minimum premium (19,780 KRW) applied.

dependent.mustPayRegional: Must pay premiums as regional subscriber
dependent.hasBusinessIncomeDetail: Has business income; not eligible as dependent
dependent.cohabitationRequired: Siblings must live together to be eligible

comparison.ceoNote: As a corporate CEO, you effectively bear both employee and employer portions of health insurance and national pension.
comparison.corporateNote: Incorporating may reduce health insurance premiums, but you must consider total costs including incorporation fees, tax accountant fees, corporate tax, and employer-side insurance contributions.
comparison.ceoEmployerNote: CEO effectively pays both employee + employer portions
comparison.regionalPensionNote: Regional subscriber pays full 9% national pension

placeholder.enterSalary: Enter monthly remuneration on the left to calculate premiums.
placeholder.enterIncome: Enter income or property information on the left.
placeholder.enterDependentInfo: Enter dependent information on the left to check eligibility.
placeholder.enterComparison: Enter income information and CEO salary on both sides to compare.

rates.title: 2025 Premium Rates
items: Health Insurance: 7.09% (Employee 3.545% + Employer 3.545%)
Long-term Care: 12.95% of health insurance premium
Regional subscriber point value: 208.4 KRW
National Pension: 9% (Employee 4.5% + Employer 4.5%), cap 6.17M KRW/month
Employment Insurance: 0.9% (employee portion)

dependentRules.title: Dependent Eligibility Criteria
items: Annual income under 20 million KRW
No registered business (business income must be 0)
Property tax base 540 million KRW or less
Between 540M~900M: additional requirement of income under 10M KRW
Siblings: must live together, under 30 or over 65 or disabled

regionalCalc.title: Regional Subscriber Calculation
items: Income premium = Monthly income x 7.09% (minimum 19,780 KRW)
Property premium = Property score x 208.4 KRW
Property amount = Tax base + Deposit x 30% - Basic deduction 100M KRW
Monthly premium = Income premium + Property premium + Long-term care

tips.title: Useful Tips
items: Workplace subscribers can voluntarily continue for 2 months after leaving (same premium)
If regional premiums are burdensome, consider registering as spouse's workplace dependent
Incorporating should be compared comprehensively including taxes and operating costs
Annual remuneration report in April settles previous year's premiums

whatIs.title: What is the Health Insurance Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Health Insurance Calculator accurately computes national health insurance and long-term care insurance premiums for both employee-insured and locally-insured individuals based on 2025 rates. It also determines dependent eligibility, compares employee versus local insurance status, and reflects property and financial income, giving you a clear view of your actual monthly premium.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your insurance type (employee-insured, locally-insured, or dependent).
2. For employees: enter your monthly salary (pre-tax).
3. For locally-insured: enter annual income, assets (real estate, vehicles, financial), and number of dependents.
4. View your health insurance premium, long-term care premium, and total monthly payment.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Employers pay 50% of the employee's health insurance premium. When calculating total compensation cost, include the employer's share.
Dependent eligibility requires meeting income and asset thresholds. In 2025, annual income over 20 million KRW or real estate assessed value over 900 million KRW disqualifies dependents.
Locally-insured individuals receive a basic 50 million KRW property deduction. The assessed real estate value minus 50 million KRW is the premium calculation base.
The voluntary continuation system (임의계속가입) lets retirees maintain their prior employee-rate premium for up to 36 months, often cheaper than switching to local insurance.

items.q: Are non-salary incomes (rental, financial) also subject to premiums?
items.a: Yes. Employees with annual non-salary income exceeding 20 million KRW are assessed additional premiums on rental income, financial income, and business income.

items.q: Is long-term care insurance billed separately?
items.a: No, it is collected together with the health insurance premium. The 2025 long-term care rate is 12.95% of the health insurance premium.

items.q: Are freelancers employee-insured or locally-insured?
items.a: Freelancers not obligated to enroll in workplace insurance are locally-insured. Even those with 3.3% withholding are classified as locally-insured based on business income.

weddingCalculator.title: Wedding Cost Planner
weddingCalculator.description: From budget planning to congratulatory money estimates and family cost splitting

averageCosts.title: 2025 Average Wedding Costs
items: Seoul/Metro average: 35M~50M won (excl. housing)
Regional average: 20M~30M won (excl. housing)
Including housing: 100M~300M+ won

savingTips.title: Saving Tips
items: Small wedding: under 50 guests, save up to 50%
Off-season (Jan-Feb, Jul-Aug) venue discounts
Compare package vs individual vendor quotes
DIY invitations/return gifts save 300K~500K won

whatIs.title: What is the Wedding Cost Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Wedding Cost Calculator helps you plan and manage costs across every stage of wedding preparation—venue, wedding photos/dress/makeup package (스드메), honeymoon, household items, wedding rings, and the new home deposit. Using average cost data as a reference, it calculates your budget, estimates expected gift money (축의금) from guests, and shows each family's share of the expenses.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Set your target wedding date and total budget.
2. Enter estimated costs for each category: venue, photo/dress/makeup, honeymoon, household items, etc.
3. Enter the expected number of guests for an automatic gift money estimate.
4. Adjust each family's cost-sharing ratio to see the net amount each side bears.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The 스드메 package price is often negotiable. Itemized contracts can sometimes cost less than a package, so get individual quotes too.
Booking a venue for weekend mornings (before 11 AM) or off-peak months (January–February, July–August) can save 20–30%.
Average gift amounts: coworkers and acquaintances typically give 50,000–100,000 KRW; close friends and relatives give 100,000–200,000 KRW.
The new home deposit is the largest single wedding expense. Compare jeonse loan interest against monthly rent to choose the best option.

items.q: What is the average cost of a wedding in 2025?
items.a: Statistics suggest the average total is approximately 200–300 million KRW including a new home. Excluding the home, ceremony, photo/dress/makeup, household items, and honeymoon typically total around 50–80 million KRW.

items.q: Is parental financial support subject to gift tax?
items.a: Up to 50 million KRW over 10 years from direct lineal ascendants is tax-free. Amounts above that require a gift tax filing. A special marriage gift deduction allows an additional 100 million KRW tax-free.

items.q: What is the cancellation fee policy for wedding venues?
items.a: Per consumer dispute resolution guidelines: 10% penalty 1+ year out, 20% at 6 months–1 year, 30% at 3–6 months, 50% at 1–3 months, and 80% within 1 month of the event.

parentalLeave.title: Parental Leave Benefit Calculator

results.placeholder: Enter your wage on the left to calculate monthly benefits.

couple.childBirthHint: Enter child's birth month for 6+6 eligibility check.
couple.placeholder: Enter both parents' wage and leave start month to see simulation results.

reducedHours.title: Reduced Work Hours Benefit
reducedHours.description: Calculate government benefits and company pay when using reduced work hours for childcare. Minimum 15 hours per week required.

warnings.childOver18Months: Child is over 18 months old. The 6+6 Parental Leave system does not apply.

changes2025.title: Key Changes in 2025
items: Parental leave benefit cap significantly raised (up to 2.5M KRW/month)
Post-payment (25%) system abolished — full payment during leave
6+6 system months 1-2 cap raised to 2.5M KRW
Month 7+ cap raised from 1.5M to 1.6M KRW
Reduced work hours benefit cap raised to 550K KRW

comparison.title: Standard vs 6+6 Comparison
comparison.note: * Cap amounts in 10K KRW, minimum benefit 700K KRW for all

eligibility.title: 6+6 Eligibility Requirements
items: Child under 18 months old
Both parents take parental leave
Employment insurance coverage for 180+ days
Leave starts in 2025 or later

whatIs.title: What is the Parental Leave Pay Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Parental Leave Pay Calculator fully implements the expanded 6+6 Parental Leave Program effective in 2025, calculating monthly parental leave pay in detail. Simulate benefits for both parents taking leave simultaneously or sequentially, including deferred payment rules and ordinary wage calculations, to accurately determine the total amount receivable. An essential tool for couples planning financially around a new child.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select who is taking leave (mother, father, or both simultaneously).
2. Enter ordinary monthly wage.
3. Set the start timing (child's age) and duration of the leave.
4. View monthly pay details (including 6+6 special benefit periods) and total payout.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: 6+6 Program: when both parents take leave, each receives up to 100% of ordinary wage (max 4.5 million KRW/month) for the first 6 months. If only one parent takes leave, this benefit does not apply.
Deferred payment: 25% of pay is held back and paid after 6+ months of return to work. Failing to return forfeits the deferred amount.
You need at least 180 days of Employment Insurance enrollment to qualify. Verify eligibility if you recently changed jobs.
Spousal birth leave (10 days) is separate from parental leave and must be applied for within 90 days of birth.

items.q: Can freelancers or self-employed individuals receive parental leave pay?
items.a: Self-employed individuals who voluntarily enrolled in Employment Insurance can receive it. Freelancers or sole proprietors not enrolled in Employment Insurance are not eligible.

items.q: Is parental leave pay taxable?
items.a: Parental leave pay is a social insurance benefit, not employment income, and is therefore tax-exempt. It does not appear in year-end tax settlements and does not affect health insurance premiums.

items.q: For twins, can leave be taken for each child separately?
items.a: No. Parental leave is granted per parent, not per child. Each parent can take up to 1 year total, regardless of the number of children. A couple with twins can each take up to 1 year.

curlBuilder.title: cURL Command Builder
curlBuilder.description: Generate cURL commands and convert to code

errors.bodyWithGet: GET requests typically do not include a body

devtools.title: Copy cURL from Browser
items: Open Chrome DevTools > Network tab (F12)
Right-click the desired request
Select Copy > Copy as cURL
Paste in Parse Mode

tips.title: Tips
items: Query parameters can be entered directly in the URL or added as separate fields
Bearer tokens set in the auth section are automatically added to headers
When entering JSON body, adding Content-Type: application/json header is recommended
Use Ctrl+Enter to quickly parse or save to history

whatIs.title: What is the cURL Command Builder?
whatIs.description: The cURL Command Builder automatically generates cURL commands for HTTP requests and converts them into code snippets for Python requests, JavaScript fetch, Axios, and other languages. It saves developers from repeatedly writing verbose command-line arguments during API testing and integration work. Complex authentication headers and multipart file upload configurations are generated in just a few clicks.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.) and enter the URL.
2. Add request headers (Authorization, Content-Type, etc.).
3. Enter the request body (JSON, form-data, raw text, etc.).
4. Copy the generated cURL command or convert it to your preferred language (Python/JS/Axios).

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Add the -v (verbose) flag to print all request and response headers—very useful for debugging.
Bearer token authentication is automatically formatted as -H 'Authorization: Bearer {token}'. Just enter the token and the complete header is generated.
File uploads use the -F 'file=@/path/to/file' format. Selecting the multipart/form-data type automatically produces the correct syntax.
Add --compressed to automatically decompress gzip responses and reduce API response payload size.

items.q: Does the generated cURL command work on Windows?
items.a: Windows 10/11 includes curl natively. However, single quotes may be treated differently in PowerShell—replace them with double quotes or use the Windows curl syntax.

items.q: Is it safe to share a cURL command that contains an API key?
items.a: Absolutely not. Never share cURL commands containing API keys, Bearer tokens, or passwords in public channels. Always replace sensitive values with placeholders before sharing.

items.q: How do I save the response to a file?
items.a: Add -o output.json to save the response to a specified file. Use -O to auto-save using the filename from the URL.

comprehensivePropertyTax.title: Comprehensive Real Estate Tax Calculator 2025
comprehensivePropertyTax.description: Automatically calculate Korea's Comprehensive Real Estate Tax (종부세) for 2025. Check tax rates by taxpayer type, credits, burden ceiling, and farmland surtax step by step.

prevYearTax.label: Previous Year Tax (Optional)
prevYearTax.helpText: Used for tax burden ceiling calculation. Skip to ignore ceiling.

result.title: Calculation Summary
result.notSubject: Not Subject to Comprehensive Real Estate Tax
result.notSubjectDetail: Fair market value {fairMarket} KRW of total assessed {total} KRW is below the deduction of {deduction} KRW, resulting in 0 tax base.
result.placeholder: Enter property assessed values on the left to calculate the tax automatically.

step3.label: Basic Deduction -> Tax Base
step3.desc: Fair market value - deduction of {deduction} KRW

step6.label: Tax Burden Ceiling
step6.appliedDesc: Capped at {rate} of previous year tax
step6.notAppliedDesc: Tax is within the ceiling limit.

rateTable.title: Tax Rate Table
rateTable.corpFlat: Corporations are subject to a flat 5.0% rate on the entire tax base.

overview.title: Overview
items: Comprehensive Real Estate Tax is levied on owners holding properties above a certain value as of June 1 each year.
Taxable: When the sum of housing assessed values exceeds the basic deduction threshold.
Deduction: Single-home 1.2B KRW, General 900M KRW, Corporation 0 KRW (since 2023).
Fair market value ratio: 60% applies for 2025.
Payment period: December 1-15 each year.

rates.title: Tax Rates
items: General (2 or fewer): 0.5% to 2.7% progressive rates in 7 brackets.
3+ properties: 1.2% to 6.0% surtax rates apply.
Corporation: Flat 5.0% on the entire tax base.
Farmland surtax: Additional 20% of the base tax is levied.

credits.title: Tax Credits (Single-home Only)
items: Long-term holding credit: 20% (5-10yr), 40% (10-15yr), 50% (15+yr).
Combined cap for elderly + long-term: Maximum 80%.
Burden ceiling: 150% of prior year tax (general), 300% (3+/corp).

tips.title: Tax-saving Tips
items: Check assessed values on the MOLIT Real Estate Price Portal.
File an objection to lower the assessed value if it seems too high.
Single-home owners get a 1.2B KRW deduction plus elderly/holding credits.
Installment payment available if tax exceeds 2.5M KRW.
Registered rental housing may qualify for assessment exclusion.

whatIs.title: What is the Comprehensive Real Estate Tax Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Comprehensive Real Estate Tax (종합부동산세, jonbuse) Calculator automatically computes the tax for homes and land using 2025 tax rates and the fair market value ratio. It correctly applies owner-type tax rates (single homeowner, multi-homeowner, corporation), senior and long-term holding credits, and the 150% annual tax burden cap for an accurate liability estimate. Use it to understand your jonbuse exposure before making real estate decisions.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the taxpayer type (single homeowner, multi-homeowner, or corporation) and number of homes.
2. Enter the officially assessed price (공시가격) for all holdings combined.
3. Select applicable credits: senior credit (age 60+) and long-term holding credit (5+ years).
4. Enter last year's tax amount to apply the tax burden cap, then view the final tax payable.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The jonbuse assessment date is June 1. To minimize tax, sell before May 31 or purchase on June 2 or later.
The single homeowner basic deduction is 1.2 billion KRW. No jonbuse is levied if your home's assessed value is below this threshold.
The senior credit (up to 30%) and long-term holding credit (up to 40%) can be combined for a maximum 70% reduction.
Joint-name ownership with a spouse gives each person a 900 million KRW deduction (total 1.8 billion), which can be more favorable than sole ownership.

items.q: When is the jonbuse payment due?
items.a: Payment is due December 1–15 each year. If the tax exceeds 5 million KRW, installment payment over 6 months is available. Tax notices are sent in November.

items.q: Does registering a rental property as a long-term rental business exclude it from jonbuse calculation?
items.a: Registered long-term rental properties may qualify for exclusion from the combined calculation if all conditions are met, including the mandatory rental period and rent increase cap compliance.

items.q: Does my jonbuse automatically increase when the assessed price rises?
items.a: Yes. Since jonbuse is based on assessed prices, rising values increase tax liability. However, the annual tax burden cap (150% of prior year tax) limits sudden large increases.

cagrCalculator.title: CAGR Calculator
cagrCalculator.description: Calculate Compound Annual Growth Rate, project future values, and compare investments

whatIsCagr.title: What is CAGR?
items: CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) is the annualized compound growth rate of an investment
Unlike simple average returns, CAGR reflects compounding effects for accurate performance measurement
It smooths out period-to-period volatility, showing the average annual growth from start to end
Enables apples-to-apples comparison of different investment products

formula.title: Formulas
items: CAGR = (End Value / Start Value) ^ (1 / Years) - 1
Future Value = Start Value x (1 + CAGR) ^ Years
Required Period = ln(Target / Start) / ln(1 + CAGR)
Monthly compounding: annual rate divided by 12 and compounded monthly

usage.title: How to Use
items: Measure actual returns on stocks, real estate, funds, and other investments
Compare performance of different investment alternatives on equal footing
Project future investment value and calculate time to reach goals
Analyze real returns on long-term financial products like pensions and savings

tips.title: Investment Tips
items: CAGR reflects past performance and does not guarantee future returns
Real CAGR adjusted for inflation = Nominal CAGR - Inflation Rate
Aim for stable CAGR through diversified investments to reduce risk
Rule of 72: Time to double your money ≈ 72 / CAGR(%)

whatIs.title: What is the CAGR Calculator?
whatIs.description: The CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) Calculator computes the annualized growth rate of an investment or business metric over a given period. Just enter the starting value, ending value, and number of years to instantly see the compounded annual rate. It is widely used in stock return analysis, corporate revenue growth tracking, and market size forecasting.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the starting value (initial amount or figure).
2. Enter the ending value (final amount or figure).
3. Enter the number of years.
4. The CAGR is calculated automatically, along with future value projections and investment comparison analysis.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: CAGR smooths out volatility. A 10-year CAGR of 10% can hide sharp drops and spikes along the way. Always review year-by-year returns as well.
Reverse calculation: to double your money in 10 years, you need a CAGR of 2^(1/10)−1 ≈ 7.2%. Use the reverse mode to find the required growth rate for any goal.
The historical average CAGR of the S&P 500 is approximately 10% (including dividends). Use it as a performance benchmark.
In due diligence, comparing revenue, operating profit, and user growth by CAGR gives an objective view of a company's growth trajectory.

items.q: What is the difference between CAGR and simple annual average return?
items.a: Simple average is the arithmetic mean of annual returns; CAGR is the geometric mean accounting for compounding. With volatile investments, CAGR is always lower than the simple average.

items.q: Can CAGR be negative (a loss)?
items.a: Yes. If the ending value is less than the starting value, the result is a negative CAGR. For example, 10 million KRW shrinking to 8 million over 5 years gives a CAGR of approximately −4.3%.

items.q: Can I convert quarterly or monthly data into an annualized CAGR?
items.a: Yes. Enter the period as a decimal: 18 months = 1.5 years, 6 months = 0.5 years. The calculator will return the CAGR for that duration.

chess.title: Chess
chess.description: Classic chess game with AI opponent

rules.rule1: White moves first. Each piece has its own movement rules.
rules.rule2: Pawn: forward 1 (2 from start), diagonal capture. Knight: L-shape.
rules.rule3: Bishop: diagonal, Rook: straight, Queen: diagonal + straight.
rules.rule4: King: 1 square any direction. Castling moves king and rook together.
rules.rule5: Attack the opponent's king (check). If they can't escape, it's checkmate!
rules.rule6: When a pawn reaches the opposite end, it promotes to queen/rook/bishop/knight.

pieces.title: Piece Types and Values
items: King: Most important piece, moves 1 square any direction
Queen: 9 points, unlimited diagonal + straight movement
Rook: 5 points, unlimited straight (horizontal/vertical) movement
Bishop: 3 points, unlimited diagonal movement
Knight: 3 points, L-shaped movement (can jump over pieces)
Pawn: 1 point, forward 1 (2 from start), diagonal capture

whatIs.title: What is Chess?
whatIs.description: Chess is a classic two-player strategy board game played on an 8×8 board where each player commands 16 pieces—King, Queen, Rooks, Bishops, Knights, and Pawns—with the objective of checkmating the opponent's King. Play against a three-level AI to sharpen your skills. All official rules are implemented, including check, checkmate, castling, en passant, and pawn promotion.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select a difficulty level (Easy, Medium, or Hard).
2. Choose your side: White or Black.
3. Click a piece to highlight legal moves, then click a destination square to move.
4. When in check, you must resolve it immediately. The game ends on checkmate or draw.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Opening: push central pawns (e4, d4) and quickly develop knights and bishops. Controlling the center early is key.
King safety: castle early to protect your King and activate a Rook toward the center.
Pawn structure: pawns cannot move backward, so think carefully before advancing them. Doubled and isolated pawns become weaknesses.
Endgame: a pawn reaching the opponent's back rank promotes to a Queen. Pawn promotion is a powerful strategic goal.

items.q: What is the en passant rule?
items.a: When an opponent's pawn advances two squares from its starting position and lands beside your pawn, you may capture it diagonally on the very next move as if it had only moved one square. This opportunity disappears if you don't take it immediately.

items.q: What are the ways a game can end in a draw?
items.a: A draw occurs in cases of stalemate (no legal moves but not in check), the 50-move rule (50 moves with no pawn move or capture), threefold repetition of position, or insufficient material (e.g., King vs. King).

items.q: How do I beat the AI?
items.a: Start on Easy mode to practice strategy. Key principles: remember piece values (Queen=9, Rook=5, Bishop/Knight=3, Pawn=1) and avoid losing material in unfavorable exchanges.

biorhythm.title: Biorhythm Calculator
biorhythm.description: Physical, emotional, and intellectual rhythm charts with compatibility analysis
biorhythm.emptyState: Enter your date of birth to see your biorhythm.

status.high: Great condition. You're full of energy!

what.title: What is Biorhythm?
items: Biorhythm theory suggests that a person's physical, emotional, and intellectual states fluctuate in fixed cycles from their date of birth.
Proposed by Dr. Wilhelm Fliess in the late 19th century, each rhythm follows a sine wave pattern.
Positive (+) phases represent active periods, negative (-) phases are low periods, and zero-crossing days are considered unstable 'critical days'.

rhythms.title: Three Rhythms
items: Physical (23-day cycle): Affects stamina, endurance, immunity, and athletic ability. Positive phases favor physical activity and exercise.
Emotional (28-day cycle): Affects mood, feelings, creativity, and relationships. Positive phases favor social and artistic activities.
Intellectual (33-day cycle): Affects thinking, concentration, judgment, and memory. Positive phases favor learning and important decisions.

critical.title: What are Critical Days?
items: Critical days are when the biorhythm curve crosses zero, transitioning from positive to negative or vice versa.
During these times, physical, emotional, or intellectual stability may decrease, potentially increasing the chance of accidents or mistakes.
Days marked with (!) in the calendar are critical days.

disclaimer.title: Scientific Evidence
items: Biorhythm theory has not been scientifically validated. Multiple studies have found no significant correlation between biorhythms and actual health or performance.
This calculator is for entertainment and reference only. Do not base important decisions on biorhythm readings.
If you have health or condition concerns, please consult a medical professional.

whatIs.title: What is the Biorhythm Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Biorhythm Calculator visualizes three cyclic rhythms based on your birth date: physical (23-day), emotional (28-day), and intellectual (33-day). According to the theory, abilities peak when a rhythm is high, while 'critical days' when a rhythm crosses zero may require extra caution. A compatibility analysis feature compares the rhythm alignment of two people.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your date of birth.
2. Set the date range you want to view (default: ±30 days from today).
3. Review the charts for all three rhythms.
4. For compatibility analysis, enter a second person's birth date to compare rhythm similarity.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: A 'triple high' day when all three rhythms peak simultaneously can be an ideal time for important exams, presentations, or athletic events.
Critical days when a rhythm crosses zero may involve reduced judgment. Consider postponing major decisions to a more stable period.
When the emotional rhythm is at its low point, mood swings may increase. Be mindful of impulsive words or actions in relationships.
Biorhythm theory lacks scientific validation. Use it for entertainment and reference only—do not base important decisions on it.

items.q: Is biorhythm theory scientifically proven?
items.a: No. Proposed in the 19th–early 20th century, biorhythm theory has not been scientifically validated. Studies show biorhythm predictions perform no better than chance. Use it for fun.

items.q: Is there a day when all three rhythms align identically to your birth date?
items.a: Yes—every 21,252 days (approximately 58.2 years). That is the LCM of the three cycles (23, 28, and 33 days).

items.q: Does a high compatibility score mean two people are truly well-matched?
items.a: The compatibility score reflects the mathematical similarity of two people's rhythm cycles. Real-life compatibility is determined by personality, values, and communication—treat the score as a fun reference only.

rentSubsidy.title: LH Monthly Rent Subsidy Calculator
rentSubsidy.description: Calculate monthly rent subsidies for youth, newlyweds, and general households.

whatIs.title: What is the Rent Subsidy Calculator?
whatIs.description: This tool checks eligibility and estimates support amounts for government rent subsidy programs (youth, newlywed, general low-income). Enter household income, family size, rent, and region for qualification assessment.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Select application type (youth/newlywed/general).
Enter monthly household income (pre-tax) and family size.
Enter current monthly rent, deposit, and residential region.
View eligibility status and estimated monthly subsidy amount.

tips.title: Tips
items: Youth rent subsidy targets ages 19-34 with income below 60% of median, providing up to 200K KRW/month.
Income thresholds vary by household size. Enter the exact number of family members.
Other housing support programs (housing benefits, jeonse loans) are also available.

items.q: What are youth rent subsidy requirements?
items.a: Ages 19-34, living independently, personal income below 60% of median income, and household income below 100%.

items.q: How much subsidy can I receive?
items.a: Youth program provides up to 200K KRW/month (2.4M KRW/year). Actual amount depends on rent and income level.

items.q: Are housing benefits and rent subsidy the same?
items.a: No. Housing benefits are part of the basic livelihood security system for households below 48% of median income. Rent subsidy is a separate program.

mbtiTest.title: MBTI Personality Test
mbtiTest.description: Discover your MBTI type with our detailed 48-question test
mbtiTest.question: Question
mbtiTest.shareText: My MBTI is {type} ({nickname})! Take the test →
mbtiTest.disclaimer: This test is for entertainment purposes only. It is not an official MBTI assessment.

what.title: What is MBTI?
items: MBTI (Myers-Briggs Type Indicator) is a personality type classification tool
It classifies personalities into 16 types based on 4 preference dimensions
Energy Direction (E/I), Perception (S/N), Judgment (T/F), Lifestyle (J/P)

howTo.title: How to Take the Test
items: 48 questions total, 12 questions per dimension
Choose the answer that feels more natural to you
Don't overthink — answer based on your usual behavior
The test takes approximately 5-10 minutes

axes.title: 4 Preference Dimensions
items: E(Extraversion) vs I(Introversion): Where you get energy from
S(Sensing) vs N(Intuition): How you perceive information
T(Thinking) vs F(Feeling): How you make decisions
J(Judging) vs P(Perceiving): How you approach life

whatIs.title: What is the MBTI Personality Test?
whatIs.description: The MBTI Personality Test uses 48 questions based on Carl Jung's psychological type theory to classify your personality into one of 16 types. The four axes—E/I (Extraversion/Introversion), S/N (Sensing/Intuition), T/F (Thinking/Feeling), and J/P (Judging/Perceiving)—reveal your strengths, weaknesses, career tendencies, and interpersonal style.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Read each of the 48 questions and choose the answer that feels more like you.
2. There are no right or wrong answers—respond based on your typical self, not your ideal.
3. After completing all questions, your result will show one of the 16 types.
4. Review each axis score and the detailed type description.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Answer based on your usual self, not how you wish to be or what others expect of you.
If your E/I score is close to 50/50, you may be ambiverted—displaying traits of both types depending on context.
MBTI is not a fixed trait. Your type can shift with age, experience, and environment. Individual differences within the same type are also significant.
When using MBTI for career guidance, treat preferred types as suggestions—every type can excel in every profession.

items.q: Why does my MBTI result change each time I take the test?
items.a: Results can vary based on your mental state, recent experiences, and how you interpret the questions. Axes near the 50% threshold are especially prone to flipping. Take the test on different days for a more consistent result.

items.q: What is the rarest MBTI type?
items.a: INFJ is considered the rarest, found in roughly 1–2% of the global population. The most common types include ISFJ, ESFJ, and ISTJ.

items.q: Is MBTI appropriate as a hiring tool?
items.a: MBTI was not designed for selection purposes. Its predictive validity for job performance is low, and professionals advise against using it in hiring decisions.

linuxCommand.description: Select options with checkboxes to auto-generate the complete command. Reference for 15 essential Linux commands.
linuxCommand.selectOptionsHint: Select options above or enter arguments.

mbtiCompatibility.title: MBTI Compatibility
mbtiCompatibility.description: Detailed compatibility analysis between two MBTI types
mbtiCompatibility.matrixDesc: Click a cell to see detailed analysis
mbtiCompatibility.disclaimer: MBTI compatibility is for entertainment only. Real relationships depend on individual effort and communication.

rating.title: Rating Levels
items: Soulmate (★★★★★): Naturally perfect match
Very Good (★★★★): Complementary and growth-oriented
Good (★★★): Works well with some effort
Average (★★): Differences exist but manageable
Caution (★): Frequent conflicts possible, requires significant effort

tips.title: Usage Tips
items: MBTI shows personality tendencies, not absolute standards
Individual differences exist even within the same type
Check both male and female perspectives for a complete view
Low compatibility doesn't mean a bad relationship — effort matters most

whatIs.title: What is the MBTI Compatibility Analysis?
whatIs.description: The MBTI Compatibility Analysis evaluates the relationship between two personality types in romantic, friendship, and workplace contexts by analyzing interaction patterns based on their types. It highlights shared strengths, potential conflict areas, and complementary qualities to help you build deeper understanding and smoother relationships. Analysis is grounded in established MBTI theory and psychological research.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your MBTI type from the 16 available options.
2. Select the other person's MBTI type.
3. Choose the relationship type (romantic, friendship, or coworker).
4. Review the compatibility score, similarities, differences, and relationship advice.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Same-type pairs (e.g., INFP + INFP) understand each other well but also share weaknesses. Different types often complement and strengthen each other.
The T/F axis is a common source of conflict. T types approach problems logically while F types lead with emotions—aligning communication styles is key.
J/P pairings may clash on planning versus spontaneity. Acknowledging each other's style and finding middle ground takes practice.
A low compatibility score does not determine the success of a relationship—real outcomes depend on effort and communication.

items.q: Which MBTI pairing is the most compatible?
items.a: In theory, pairs sharing the T/F and J/P axes while differing on E/I and S/N (e.g., INTJ + ENTJ) are considered mutually complementary. In practice, all pairings succeed with effort.

items.q: Can couples with very different MBTI types have lasting relationships?
items.a: Absolutely. Low-compatibility pairings build wonderful relationships every day. Shared values, mutual respect, and communication matter far more than MBTI scores.

items.q: Can I analyze compatibility if I don't know the other person's type?
items.a: You can observe the person's behavioral patterns and select an estimated type. For the most accurate analysis, having the other person take the test directly is best.

chmodCalculator.title: chmod Permission Calculator
chmodCalculator.description: Set Unix/Linux file permissions visually with checkboxes — chmod commands are generated automatically
chmodCalculator.octalHint: You can type a 3-digit octal number (0–7) directly

basics.title: How to Calculate Permission Bits
items: Read (r) = 4, Write (w) = 2, Execute (x) = 1
Each digit represents Owner · Group · Others in order
Example: 755 → Owner rwx(7), Group r-x(5), Others r-x(5)

common.title: Commonly Used Permissions
items: 755: Suitable for executables and web directories
644: General files, HTML/CSS/config files
600: Private keys and secret config files
400: Read-only files (write-protected)
750: Group read/execute, others blocked
777: All permissions (security risk — avoid in production)

security.title: Security Guidelines
items: 777 grants write access to all users — a serious security risk
Minimize write permissions on web server files (644 recommended)
SSH private keys (.pem, id_rsa) must always be set to 600
Do not grant execute (x) permission to non-executable files
Principle of Least Privilege: grant only the minimum permissions needed

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Use chmod -R 755 /dir to apply permissions recursively to a directory
find . -type f -exec chmod 644 {} \; changes all files at once
find . -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \; changes all directories at once
Use ls -la to check the current permissions of a file

whatIs.title: What is the chmod Calculator?
whatIs.description: The chmod Calculator lets you visually set Unix/Linux file and directory permissions using checkboxes, then automatically generates the corresponding chmod command and octal permission code. Set read, write, and execute permissions for owner, group, and others intuitively. Conversely, enter a numeric code like 777 or 644 to see what each permission means.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Check the desired read, write, and execute boxes for owner, group, and others.
2. Alternatively, type an octal code (e.g., 755) directly to decode the permission breakdown.
3. Copy the generated chmod command (e.g., chmod 755 filename).
4. Special permissions (setuid, setgid, sticky bit) are also configurable.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Common web server permissions: directories 755 (rwxr-xr-x), HTML/CSS/JS files 644 (rw-r--r--), PHP files 644, upload directories 755.
Permission 777 (full read/write/execute for everyone) is a serious security risk. Never use it on a server.
755 is the standard for executables and scripts: full access for the owner, read + execute only for group and others.
Use chmod -R 755 /path to recursively apply permissions to all files and subdirectories.

items.q: How is the number 755 calculated?
items.a: r=4, w=2, x=1. Owner: rwx = 4+2+1 = 7. Group: r-x = 4+0+1 = 5. Others: r-x = 4+0+1 = 5. Combined: 755.

items.q: What is the difference between chown and chmod?
items.a: chmod changes who can read/write/execute a file (permissions), while chown changes who the file belongs to (ownership). They control different file attributes.

items.q: Does chmod work on Windows?
items.a: chmod does not work on native Windows NTFS. It is available inside WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) and partially in Git Bash.

ranges.title: Port Ranges
ranges.wellKnownDesc: IANA standard assignments. System services only. Requires root/admin to bind.
ranges.registeredDesc: IANA-registered applications. Databases, middleware, and major services.
ranges.dynamicDesc: Assigned by OS as ephemeral/client ports. Can also be used for custom services.

security.title: Port Security Tips
items: Principle of least privilege: only open ports you need, and close unused ones immediately.
Never expose database ports (MySQL 3306, PostgreSQL 5432, MongoDB 27017, Redis 6379) directly to the internet — use VPN or SSH tunnels.
Replace unencrypted protocols like Telnet (23) and FTP (21) with SSH (22) and SFTP.
RDP (3389) is a frequent brute-force target — place it behind a VPN and consider changing the default port.
Redis (6379), Memcached (11211), and Elasticsearch (9200) have no authentication by default — always configure auth.
Restrict firewall rules to specific IP ranges and audit them regularly.
Exposing the Docker daemon API (2375) to the internet gives full server control — always use TLS (2376).

tcpUdp.title: TCP vs UDP
tcpUdp.tcpDesc: Connection-oriented, reliable delivery, ordered packets, retransmission support. Slower but stable. Used by HTTP, SSH, databases, etc.
tcpUdp.udpDesc: Connectionless, fast, no reliability guarantee. Used by DNS, DHCP, streaming, gaming, and VoIP where speed matters most.

whatIs.title: What is the Port Number Reference?
whatIs.description: The Port Number Reference is a quick-lookup tool for developers and system administrators to search TCP/UDP port numbers by service name, number, or description. It covers well-known ports (0–1023), registered ports (1024–49151), and dynamic ports (49152–65535). Essential for firewall configuration, network troubleshooting, and security audits.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Type a port number (e.g., 443) or service name (e.g., https) in the search box.
2. Review the protocol (TCP/UDP), official service name, and description in the results.
3. Use the range filter (Well-known / Registered / Dynamic) to browse categories.
4. Identify what a specific service uses, or discover what an open port belongs to.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Common ports to memorize: HTTP(80), HTTPS(443), SSH(22), FTP(21), SMTP(25), DNS(53), MySQL(3306), PostgreSQL(5432), Redis(6379), MongoDB(27017).
Use ports 1024+ for development servers. Ports below 1024 (well-known) require root/administrator privileges to bind.
Close unnecessary ports on public-facing servers. Restrict ports 23 (Telnet), 21 (FTP), and 3389 (RDP) especially.
Use netstat -tuln or ss -tuln to list currently open ports on your system.

items.q: Should I use TCP or UDP?
items.a: Use TCP for reliability-critical applications like web, email, and file transfer. Use UDP for speed-critical applications like video streaming, DNS, gaming, and VoIP.

items.q: Can the same port number be used by both TCP and UDP simultaneously?
items.a: Yes, TCP 443 and UDP 443 are treated as separate sockets. DNS (port 53) is a prime example of a service that uses both TCP and UDP.

items.q: How do I resolve a port conflict?
items.a: Use lsof -i :PORT or netstat -tuln | grep PORT to identify the process using the port, then kill it or reconfigure your application to use a different port.

httpStatus.title: HTTP Status Code Reference
httpStatus.description: Search meanings, causes, and fixes for 60+ HTTP status codes
httpStatus.searchPlaceholder: Search by code number or description (e.g. 404, not found, redirect)

calculationHistory.title: Calculation History
calculationHistory.description: View and manage all saved calculations from every calculator in one place
calculationHistory.searchPlaceholder: Search by title or tool name...
calculationHistory.clearAllDescription: This will permanently delete all {count} saved calculations. This action cannot be undone.
calculationHistory.emptyDescription: Calculations are saved automatically when you use any calculator. Pick one below to get started.

picross.title: Picross
picross.description: Logic puzzle game - fill cells using number clues to reveal a picture
guideRulesItems: Number clues on rows and columns indicate consecutive filled cells.
There must be at least one empty cell between groups of filled cells.
Fill cells to satisfy all clues and reveal the hidden picture.
0 means no cells are filled in that row/column.
guideTipsItems: Start with rows/columns that have large number clues.
When the clue sum is close to the grid size, use overlap technique to find definite cells.
Mark empty cells with X to help with reasoning.
Completed row/column clues turn green.
guideControlsItems: PC: Left-click to fill a cell, right-click to mark X.
Mobile: Tap to fill a cell, long press to mark X.
Click a filled cell again to clear it.

hero.title: May Global Income Tax Filing Guide
hero.subtitle: From who must file to tax rates and saving strategies — prepare for your May tax filing with this complete guide.
hero.deadlinePast: This year's filing period has ended. Check back in May next year.
hero.deadlineNote: Diligent filing confirmation subjects: until June 30

targets.title: Who Must File
targets.subtitle: If any of the following applies to you, you are required to file a global income tax return in May.

freelancer.title: Freelancers & Self-Employed
freelancer.desc: Sole proprietors, freelancers, and personal service providers with business income. Filing is required regardless of income level.

employee.title: Employees with Extra Income
employee.desc: Employees whose other income (side jobs, lecture fees, royalties) exceeds 3 million KRW, or those who worked at two or more places.

rental.title: Real Estate Rental Income
rental.desc: Those with rental income from housing (monthly rent), commercial properties, or land. Even housing rental income under 20M KRW may require filing.

financial.title: Financial Income Over 20M KRW
financial.desc: If combined interest and dividend income exceeds 20 million KRW, it is subject to global taxation and must be reported.

exempt.label: Exempt from filing:
exempt.desc: Employees with only wage income who completed year-end tax adjustment, those with only retirement or capital gains income, and those with financial income under 20M KRW are not required to file.

toolBundle.title: Tax Calculator Bundle
toolBundle.subtitle: Access all the calculators you need for your May tax filing in one place.

incomeTax.title: Income Tax Calculator
incomeTax.desc: Calculate global income tax based on business or employment income

yearEndTax.title: Year-End Tax Calculator
yearEndTax.desc: Estimate tax refund with itemized deductions for wage earners

freelancerTax.title: Freelancer Tax Calculator
freelancerTax.desc: Calculate net income after 3.3% withholding and final tax payable

acquisitionTax.title: Acquisition Tax Calculator
acquisitionTax.desc: Calculate acquisition tax and related costs for real estate

capitalGainsTax.title: Capital Gains Tax Calculator
capitalGainsTax.desc: Calculate capital gains tax on real estate or stock profits

taxCalculator.title: Tax Calculator
taxCalculator.desc: Comprehensive calculator for income, VAT, and capital gains taxes

taxRates.title: 2025 Global Income Tax Rate Table
taxRates.subtitle: Progressive 8-bracket rates apply to your taxable income (gross income minus all deductions).
taxRates.note: Taxable income is gross income minus all applicable deductions. Rates apply only to the portion of income within each bracket, not the full amount.
taxRates.exampleText: Taxable income of 60M KRW → 15% on first 50M + 24% on excess 10M, minus progressive deduction 1.26M → Total tax ≈ 8.64M KRW

tips.title: 6 Tax-Saving Strategies
tips.subtitle: Review these items before filing — they can legally reduce your tax bill.

t1.title: Maximize Pension Savings & IRP Deductions
t1.desc: Combine pension savings fund (up to 6M KRW/yr) and IRP (up to 3M more) for a maximum 9M KRW tax credit at 16.5% rate.

t2.title: Document All Business Expenses
t2.desc: Equipment, transportation, communications, books, and business meals can all be claimed as expenses. Keep tax invoices and card receipts.

t3.title: Compare Simple vs Standard Expense Rates
t3.desc: If your income is below the threshold, the simple expense rate (60–90% by industry) lets you reduce income without receipts. Actual expenses may be higher.

t4.title: Yellow Umbrella Deduction for SMEs
t4.desc: Small business owners can deduct Yellow Umbrella (노란우산) contributions (up to 5M KRW) from income — a powerful tax reducer.

t5.title: Keep Financial Income Under 20M KRW
t5.desc: Once combined interest and dividends exceed 20M KRW, they enter global taxation at higher rates. An ISA account helps manage this threshold.

t6.title: Check All Available Tax Credits
t6.desc: Youth employment at SMEs (90% reduction), startup SME credits, e-filing credit (20K KRW), and medical/education/donation credits are easy to miss.

timeline.title: 2025 Tax Filing Schedule
timeline.subtitle: Know the key deadlines and file on time to avoid penalties.

e1.title: Filing Season Opens
e1.desc: Electronic filing available on Hometax (hometax.go.kr) or via the mobile Son-Tax app.

e2.title: General Filing & Payment Deadline
e2.desc: Final deadline for most taxpayers. Late filing incurs a 20% non-filing penalty plus daily interest.

e3.title: Installment Payment 1st Due
e3.desc: If tax owed exceeds 10M KRW, pay 50% by this date. The remaining 50% can be paid by June 30.

e4.title: Diligent Filing Confirmation Deadline
e4.desc: High-revenue business owners must file with a CPA verification letter by June 30.

e5.title: Installment Payment 2nd Due
e5.desc: Remaining 50% installment payment deadline. No interest is charged on approved installments.

timeline.penaltyDesc: Missing the deadline incurs a 20% non-filing penalty (40% for fraudulent non-filing) plus a daily late payment surcharge of 0.022%. Always file on time.

q1.q: When is the global income tax filing period?
q1.a: Every year from May 1 to May 31. Diligent filing confirmation subjects have until June 30. File electronically via Hometax (hometax.go.kr) or the mobile Son-Tax app.

q2.q: I'm an employee — do I need to file?
q2.a: If you only have wage income and completed year-end tax adjustment, you do not need to file. However, if side income exceeds 3M KRW, you worked at multiple jobs, or your financial income exceeds 20M KRW, you must file.

q3.q: I already had 3.3% withheld — do I still need to file?
q3.a: Yes. The 3.3% withholding is a provisional payment, not the final tax. You settle the actual amount through the May global income tax filing. You may receive a refund or owe additional tax.

q4.q: When will I receive my tax refund?
q4.a: Refunds are issued within 30 days of filing. Register your bank account on Hometax for automatic deposit. Check refund status via the 'Refund Inquiry' feature on Hometax.

q5.q: Can I file without a tax accountant?
q5.a: Freelancers and business owners with simple income structures can use Hometax's pre-filled (모두채움) or simple filing feature. Complex situations (multiple income types, rental income, etc.) warrant professional advice.

whatIs.title: What is Comprehensive Income Tax?
whatIs.description: Comprehensive income tax is filed every May by individuals with income beyond employment (business, freelance, interest/dividend, pension, or other income), covering the previous year's combined income.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Check the countdown to the May 31 filing deadline at the top.
Review the income tax rate table by taxable income bracket.
Follow the step-by-step filing procedure guide.
Use linked calculators to estimate your tax amount.

tips.title: Tips
items: Freelancers (3.3% withholding) may get refunds by applying expense ratios.
Late filing incurs a 20% penalty plus daily late payment interest.
Use the NTS 'pre-filled filing' for quick, easy submission.

items.q: Who needs to file?
items.a: Anyone with business income, freelance income (3.3%), interest/dividends over 20M KRW, pension over 12M KRW, or other income over 3M KRW.

items.q: When is the filing period?
items.a: May 1 to May 31 each year. Extended to June 30 for certified diligent filers.

items.q: What are the tax rates?
items.a: Progressive rates from 6% to 45% based on taxable income brackets: 6% up to 14M, 15% up to 50M, 24% up to 88M, 35% up to 150M, and higher.

colorBlindnessSimulator.title: Color Blindness Simulator
colorBlindnessSimulator.description: Apply color vision deficiency (CVD) filters to images to experience how color-blind people see the world.
colorBlindnessSimulator.uploadPrompt: Drag & drop an image or click to upload
colorBlindnessSimulator.uploadSubPrompt: Supports JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF · Max 10MB
colorBlindnessSimulator.sliderHint: Drag the slider to compare original vs simulated view
colorBlindnessSimulator.noImage: Upload an image or select a sample

protanomaly.name: Protanomaly (Red-weak)
protanomaly.description: Reduced L-cone sensitivity — weak red perception

deuteranomaly.name: Deuteranomaly (Green-weak)
deuteranomaly.description: Reduced M-cone sensitivity — most common type

tritanomaly.name: Tritanomaly (Blue-weak)
tritanomaly.description: Reduced S-cone sensitivity — weak blue perception

achromatopsia.name: Achromatopsia (Total color blind)
achromatopsia.description: No cone function — sees the world in grayscale

infoPanel.title: Understanding Color Vision Deficiency
infoPanel.whatIsText: Color vision deficiency (CVD) occurs when one or more types of cone cells in the eye are absent or have reduced function, making it difficult to distinguish certain colors. It is often X-linked and therefore more common in males (~8%) than females (~0.5%).
designTipItems: Don't rely on color alone — use icons, patterns, and text labels to convey information
Ensure sufficient luminance contrast so color-blind users can still perceive content
For red-green CVD, prefer blue and orange as your primary contrast pair
Use CVD-friendly palettes (e.g., Okabe-Ito) for data visualizations

q1.q: What is the difference between color blindness and color weakness?
q1.a: Color blindness (dichromacy) means a specific cone type is completely absent, so the person cannot distinguish that color at all. Color weakness (anomalous trichromacy) means the cone is present but has reduced sensitivity. Color weakness is far more common than color blindness.

q2.a: The simulator uses color transformation matrices based on the Brettel/Viénot algorithm to approximate color-blind vision. While individual variation means it cannot be perfectly accurate, it is widely used for design accessibility reviews.

q3.q: Is my uploaded image sent to a server?
q3.a: No. All image processing is done entirely in your browser using the Canvas API. Your images are never uploaded to any server.

q4.a: Congenital color blindness has no known cure. Specialty lenses (e.g., EnChroma glasses) can improve color discrimination for some people, but effectiveness varies. Consult an ophthalmologist if you suspect a color vision deficiency.

whatIs.title: What is this tool?
whatIs.description: The Color Blindness Simulator shows how uploaded images appear to users with various color vision deficiencies (red-green, blue-yellow, total color blindness). Useful for web/app designers to verify accessibility.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Drag and drop or select an image to upload.
Select the color vision type to simulate (protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia, achromatopsia, etc.).
Compare the original and simulated images side by side.
Adjust your color palette and retest if issues are found.

tips.title: Tips
items: Red-green color blindness affects ~8% of males worldwide - always test for this.
Don't rely on color alone to convey information. Use icons, patterns, and text labels.
Mild deficiencies (anomaly types) can also be simulated for comprehensive coverage.

items.q: What's the difference between color blindness and color weakness?
items.a: Color blindness (-opia) means complete loss of certain cone cell function, while color weakness (-omaly) means partial reduction. Both are simulated here.

items.q: Can this be used for accessibility auditing?
items.a: Yes. WCAG 2.1 recommends not relying solely on color for information. Use this tool to verify your designs are accessible.

items.q: How accurate is the simulation?
items.a: It uses the Brettel/Viénot algorithm, a well-established method in academic research with high correlation to actual color vision deficiency experience.

screenCompare.title: Screen Size Comparison
screenCompare.description: Visually compare screen sizes of smartphones, tablets, laptops, and monitors at accurate proportions
screenCompare.scaleNote: * Displayed at proportional scale — not actual size

mahjongSolitaire.title: Mahjong Solitaire
mahjongSolitaire.description: Match pairs of identical tiles in this classic tile-matching puzzle
mahjongSolitaire.stuckMessage: No matching pairs available. Try shuffle or undo.
rulesItems: Click two matching free tiles to remove them.
Free tile: not covered by another tile, and has at least one open side (left or right).
Flower tiles (🌸🌺🌻🌷) match any other flower. Season tiles (🌸☀️🍂❄️) match any other season.
tipsItems: Remove tiles from higher layers first to open up tiles below.
Check the positions of all 4 copies of a tile before matching.
To free a blocked tile, remove tiles on one of its sides first.
Use hints or shuffle when you're stuck.

crossword.title: Crossword Puzzle
crossword.description: Fill in Korean words using across and down clues in this crossword puzzle game.
crossword.hint: Click a cell and type Korean characters. Press Tab to switch direction, arrow keys to navigate.

guide.title: How to Play
items: Click a cell to see the clue for that word
Type Korean characters — the cursor moves to the next cell automatically
Click the same cell again to toggle between across and down
Press Tab to quickly switch between across and down direction
Use the Check button to highlight incorrect letters in red
Use the Reveal Word button to show the answer for the current word
A new daily puzzle is available every day at midnight

whatIs.title: What is Crossword?
whatIs.description: Crossword is a classic puzzle game where you fill in Korean words based on across and down clues. It helps expand vocabulary and general knowledge across a wide range of topics. Multiple difficulty levels are available, with hint and answer-reveal functions to help when you get stuck.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Read the across and down clues and type your answers into the corresponding numbered squares.
2. Click a square or use the arrow keys to navigate; type to enter letters.
3. When all squares are filled, the puzzle automatically checks your answers.
4. Use the hint button for an extra clue, or reveal a single letter if you're stuck.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Use intersections strategically: letters shared between across and down words narrow down possibilities for both answers simultaneously.
Start with the shortest words (2–3 letters). Filling them first reveals crossing letters that unlock longer words.
Re-read clues multiple times. Meanings that seem obscure at first often click after you've filled in some crossing letters.
For specialized terms and proper nouns, think of the most well-known term in that field first.

items.q: How do I correct a wrong answer?
items.a: Click the square and press Delete or Backspace to erase the letter, then retype. The reset button clears the entire puzzle.

items.q: Do new puzzles become available?
items.a: Multiple puzzles are included and new ones become available after completing the current one. New daily puzzles may also be added regularly.

items.q: Can I play on mobile?
items.a: Yes. Tapping a square brings up the keyboard automatically. Touch navigation between squares is also supported.

resultMessages.perfect: A match made in heaven! Soulmates! 💕
resultMessages.great: Great compatibility! You two are perfect together 😍
resultMessages.good: Good compatibility! You'll be happy together 😊
resultMessages.average: Average compatibility. With effort, it can work out 🙂
resultMessages.belowAverage: Could be better. But love conquers all! 💪
resultMessages.low: Low compatibility. But love knows no bounds! ❤️

howItWorks.title: How It Works
items: The characters from both names are arranged alternately
Each character's consonants/vowels are converted to ㅇ and ㅎ
Stroke counts are calculated (ㅇ=1, ㅎ=3)
Adjacent numbers are summed down to single digits for the final score

tips.title: Good to Know
items: Name compatibility is meant for fun
Only Korean names are supported (2-5 characters)
Save the result card as an image or share via link
Same names always produce the same result

whatIs.title: What is the Name Compatibility Test?
whatIs.description: The Name Compatibility Test analyzes the stroke count, phonetics, and consonant/vowel composition of two Korean names to produce a fun compatibility score and personality pairing description. It combines traditional Korean naming science (성명학) with statistical patterns to evaluate romantic, friendship, and professional relationships. Results are shareable as an image card for social media.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the first person's name in Korean.
2. Enter the second person's name in Korean.
3. Select the relationship type (romantic, friend, or business).
4. View the compatibility score, pairing description, and advice—then share the result.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: This is a fun tool, not a scientific assessment. Enjoy it lightheartedly and don't read too much into the results.
Save the result image and share it on KakaoTalk or Instagram for a fun conversation starter with friends.
Results may differ depending on whether you include the family name. Try both full name and given name only for comparison.
Compare multiple name combinations to find which pairing produces the highest score—it's a fun challenge.

items.q: Is name compatibility scientifically supported?
items.a: No. Name compatibility is based on traditional Korean naming philosophy but has no scientific validation. Use it purely for entertainment.

items.q: Can I use English names?
items.a: The analysis is designed for Korean names. You can transliterate English names into Korean phonetics and enter them to get a result.

items.q: Should I be concerned if I get a low score?
items.a: Not at all. Actual relationship quality depends on personality, shared values, and communication—factors far more significant than name compatibility.
