personalColor.title: Personal Color Analysis
personalColor.description: Find your best color tone with 12 questions

springWarm.name: Spring Warm
springWarm.description: You have a bright and warm aura like spring! Clear and warm colors suit you best.
springWarm.characteristics: Warm and bright skin tone, yellowish undertone, brown or light brown eyes
springWarm.celebrities: Jessica Alba, Amanda Seyfried, Emma Stone
springWarm.fashionTips: Coral lipstick, peach blush, ivory/beige base, gold accessories suit you perfectly

summerCool.name: Summer Cool
summerCool.description: You have an elegant aura like a cool summer breeze! Pastels and cool colors suit you best.
summerCool.characteristics: Pink-toned bright skin, blue undertone, black or dark brown eyes
summerCool.celebrities: Cate Blanchett, Anne Hathaway, Lupita Nyong'o
summerCool.fashionTips: Rose pink lipstick, lavender eyeshadow, silver accessories, blue-based makeup suit you perfectly

autumnWarm.name: Autumn Warm
autumnWarm.description: You have a mature aura like warm autumn foliage! Deep and warm colors suit you best.
autumnWarm.characteristics: Golden skin tone, warm medium undertone, dark brown or amber eyes
autumnWarm.fashionTips: Brick red lipstick, terracotta blush, gold/bronze accessories, natural makeup suit you perfectly

winterCool.name: Winter Cool
winterCool.description: You have a striking aura like crisp winter! Bold and vivid colors suit you best.
winterCool.characteristics: Cool-toned white or olive skin, distinct eye color, sharp features
winterCool.fashionTips: Red or wine lipstick, bold eyeliner, silver/platinum accessories, monochrome style suit you perfectly

questions.question: What is your skin tone under sunlight?
Which looks better on you — silver or gold accessories?
What compliments do you get about your skin?
What is your natural hair color in sunlight?
White vs ivory clothes — which suits you better?
What color clothing makes your face glow?
What color are your veins? (inner wrist)
What lip color suits you best?
How does your skin react to tanning?
options: Doesn't tan much or turns red

questions.question: What overall color tone feels most comfortable?
What patterns or prints look best on you?

whatIs.title: What is Personal Color?
items: It's finding the most flattering color palette based on your natural skin, eye, and hair color
Divided into 4 types: Warm (Spring/Autumn) and Cool (Summer/Winter)
Knowing your personal color makes choosing clothes, makeup, and hair color easier
Professional analysis uses draping under specific lighting, but self-tests can give a general idea

tips.title: How to Use
items: Top and accessory colors near your face matter most
Bottom colors don't matter as much
Makeup matching your personal color maximizes the effect
This test is for fun. For accurate analysis, consult a professional

whatIs.title: What is the Personal Color Self-Assessment?
whatIs.description: The Personal Color Self-Assessment analyzes your physical characteristics—skin tone, eye color, hair color—through 12 questions to identify which of the four seasonal color palettes (Spring Warm, Summer Cool, Autumn Warm, Winter Cool) suits you best. Use the results to discover flattering clothing colors, makeup shades, and hair colors for your personal style.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Look in a mirror under natural light—no makeup is most accurate.
2. Answer all 12 questions about your skin, eyes, hair, and veins honestly.
3. Your seasonal color type (Spring Warm / Summer Cool / Autumn Warm / Winter Cool) is revealed.
4. Review the recommended and unflattering color palettes for your season.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Self-assessment has limitations. A professional colorist's draping test is most accurate. Use this tool to understand your general direction.
Spring Warm: bright warm tones (peach, coral, golden yellow). Summer Cool: soft cool tones (lavender, pink, sky blue).
Autumn Warm: deep earthy tones (mustard, brown, olive). Winter Cool: high-contrast vivid colors (black, white, royal blue, cherry red).
If your hair is dyed, base your answers on your natural, undyed hair color for the most accurate result.

items.q: How do I tell if I'm warm or cool tone?
items.a: Check the veins on your inner wrist: greenish veins suggest warm tone; bluish veins suggest cool tone. Gold jewelry looking better also indicates warm tone; silver indicates cool.

items.q: Does personal color apply to darker skin tones too?
items.a: Yes. The warm/cool undertone distinction applies to all skin tones regardless of lightness or darkness. Everyone has colors that work better or worse for their undertone.

items.q: My result shows two overlapping seasons—what should I do?
items.a: If you're on the boundary, both seasons may suit you. Focus on colors common to both seasons, or use the season with more strongly matching characteristics as your primary reference.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Look in a mirror under natural light—no makeup is most accurate.
2. Answer all 12 questions about your skin, eyes, hair, and veins honestly.
3. Your seasonal color type (Spring Warm / Summer Cool / Autumn Warm / Winter Cool) is revealed.
4. Review the recommended and unflattering color palettes for your season.

items.q: How do I tell if I'm warm or cool tone?
items.a: Check the veins on your inner wrist: greenish veins suggest warm tone; bluish veins suggest cool tone. Gold jewelry looking better also indicates warm tone; silver indicates cool.

items.q: My result shows two overlapping seasons—what should I do?
items.a: If you're on the boundary, both seasons may suit you. Focus on colors common to both seasons, or use the season with more strongly matching characteristics as your primary reference.

ovulationCalculator.title: Ovulation Calculator
ovulationCalculator.description: Enter your menstrual cycle to predict ovulation and fertile window
ovulationCalculator.disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Consult a gynecologist for accurate diagnosis.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
items: Ovulation typically occurs 14 days before the next period starts
The fertile window is 5 days before ovulation to 1 day after
Predictions may be less accurate with irregular cycles
Combining with basal body temperature and ovulation tests improves accuracy

tips.title: Important Notes
items: Average menstrual cycle is 28 days, but 21-35 days is normal
Ovulation can vary due to individual differences, stress, and health
For conception, intercourse every 2-3 days during fertile window is recommended
This calculator alone is insufficient for contraception; always use proper birth control

whatIs.title: What is the Ovulation Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Ovulation Calculator predicts your ovulation date, fertile window, and next expected period by entering your last menstrual period start date and average cycle length, displayed visually on a calendar. It is used by couples planning to conceive to time intercourse and as a reference for natural family planning. A cycle-tracking feature helps you monitor your own patterns over time.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter the start date of your last menstrual period.
2. Enter your average cycle length in days (typically 21–35 days).
3. Optionally adjust the luteal phase length (default: 14 days).
4. View the predicted ovulation date, fertile window start/end, and next expected period on the calendar.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The fertile window spans roughly 6 days: from 5 days before ovulation through ovulation day. The 1–2 days just before ovulation carry the highest conception probability.
For irregular cycles, using the average of at least 3 months of data produces more reliable predictions.
More precise ovulation tracking methods include: basal body temperature (rises 0.3–0.5°C after ovulation), LH test strips (ovulation predictor kits), and cervical mucus observation.
This tool is for reference only and should not be relied on for contraception. The typical failure rate of the natural family planning method is about 20–25%.

items.q: My cycle isn't 28 days. Can I still use this?
items.a: Yes. You enter your actual cycle length, so any cycle between 21 and 35 days is supported. 28 days is just a statistical average—normal cycles range from 21 to 35 days.

items.q: Can stress or dieting affect my ovulation date?
items.a: Yes. Extreme stress, rapid weight changes, and excessive exercise can shift ovulation timing earlier or later. Consult a gynecologist if your cycle suddenly changes significantly.

items.q: Can I use this calculator to confirm pregnancy?
items.a: No. This tool only estimates ovulation. Pregnancy must be confirmed with a home pregnancy test or medical examination. Use a test if your period is more than two weeks late.

csatGrade.title: CSAT Grade Calculator
csatGrade.description: Check your grade based on raw score for each CSAT subject
csatGrade.subject: Select Subject
csatGrade.disclaimer: Grade cutoffs are based on 2025 CSAT. Actual cutoffs vary each year.

grading.title: Grading Method
items: Relative grading: Top 4% = Grade 1, Top 11% = Grade 2, Top 23% = Grade 3...
Absolute grading (English/Korean History): Grades based on raw score ranges
English: 90+ = Grade 1, 80+ = Grade 2...
Korean History: 40+ = Grade 1, 35+ = Grade 2...

tips.title: Notes
items: Displayed cutoffs are based on previous year and may differ from actual CSAT
Standard scores and percentiles apply only to relatively graded subjects
Exploration subjects have separate converted standard scores
Official cutoffs are released by KICE on score release date

whatIs.title: What is the CSAT Grade Calculator?
whatIs.description: The CSAT Grade Calculator instantly displays your estimated grade by subject using the latest grade cutoff data after you enter your raw scores. It supports all subjects including Korean, Math, English, Social Studies, Science, Vocational Education, and second foreign languages. Full grade cutoff tables are provided to help you set target scores and plan your study strategy.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select the exam year (academic year).
2. Select a subject and enter your raw score.
3. Your corresponding grade is displayed instantly.
4. Review the full grade cutoff table to identify the score needed for your target grade.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Grade cutoffs change every year. Prior-year cutoffs are reference data only—always check the official KICE score report after the actual exam.
English is graded on an absolute scale: 90+ = Grade 1, 80+ = Grade 2, etc.—unlike the relative-scale subjects.
Exploratory subject (탐구) cutoffs vary by selected elective. The same raw score may earn different grades in different subjects.
Mock exam (모의고사) cutoffs differ from actual CSAT cutoffs due to different test-takers and difficulty levels.

items.q: Can the same raw score earn different grades in different years?
items.a: Yes. CSAT uses relative grading (except English), so the same raw score can land in different grade brackets depending on that year's score distribution. Standard scores and percentiles are what actually determine university admissions.

items.q: Which side of a grade cutoff boundary counts as which grade?
items.a: The cutoff is the minimum score for that grade. For example, if Grade 1 cuts at 91, a score of 91 is Grade 1 and 90 is Grade 2.

items.q: How do I compare scores across different exploratory electives?
items.a: Universities use standard scores and percentiles to compensate for difficulty differences between electives. A lower raw score in a harder subject may produce a higher standard score.

lottoTax.title: Lottery Prize Tax Calculator
lottoTax.description: Calculate taxes on lottery winnings and see your actual take-home amount
lottoTax.comparisonDesc: Compare taxes and take-home amounts for various prize amounts

taxStructure.title: Tax Structure
items: ≤50,000 KRW: Tax-free
>50,000 ~ ≤300M KRW: 22% (Income Tax 20% + Local Tax 2%)
>300M KRW: 33% (Income Tax 30% + Local Tax 3%)
Winnings are classified as 'other income' and withheld at source
No separate income tax filing is required (separate taxation)

claimProcess.title: How to Claim
items: ≤50,000 KRW: Collect at any lottery retailer
>50,000 ~ ≤2M KRW: Any NH Bank branch
>2M KRW: NH Bank headquarters (ID required)
1st prize: NH Bank HQ 1st floor (Jung-gu, Seoul)
Must be claimed within 1 year of the draw date

tips.title: Good to Know
items: Lotto 1st prize odds: approximately 1 in 8,145,060
Taxes are automatically withheld — you receive the net amount
Pension lottery is taxed on each monthly payment
Winnings are not included in health insurance premium calculations
Sharing with family/friends may trigger gift tax

whatIs.title: What is the Lotto Tax Calculator?
whatIs.description: The Lotto Tax Calculator computes the tax withheld on Korean lottery winnings and shows your actual take-home amount. Prizes over 200,000 KRW are subject to 22% withholding (20% income tax + 2% local income tax); amounts exceeding 300 million KRW are taxed at 33% (30% + 3%). Use it to plan your finances if you win.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select your prize tier or enter a custom prize amount.
2. View the withholding tax amount and net take-home figure.
3. Calculate combined winnings if you won multiple tiers simultaneously.
4. Annuity lottery options can also be calculated to show the after-tax monthly payout.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Lotto jackpot prizes are already net of 22% withholding when you collect from Dong Haeng Bokwon. No separate tax filing is required.
Lottery winnings are classified as other income (기타소득). If total other income is 3 million KRW or less annually, withholding is final. Above that, comprehensive income tax filing may be required.
For annuity lotteries, the monthly payment exceeding 200,000 KRW is subject to 22% withholding. Calculate the total 20-year tax burden as well.
Overseas lottery winnings may trigger Korean tax reporting obligations. Consult a tax accountant before collecting.

items.q: If I share winnings with family, is gift tax triggered?
items.a: Yes. Giving money to family members triggers gift tax. Spouses are tax-free up to 600 million KRW over 10 years; adult children up to 50 million KRW. Amounts above those thresholds require a gift tax filing.

items.q: Can I choose between lump sum and annuity?
items.a: Standard Lotto (6/45) pays only as a lump sum. Pension Lottery 720+ offers a choice: 7 million KRW/month for 20 years, or a lump sum (approximately 50–60% of total annuity value).

items.q: Is the take-home amount final, or are there additional taxes later?
items.a: The tax on the winnings themselves is fully settled at the time of collection. Subsequent investments or purchases may generate their own taxes (capital gains, acquisition tax, etc.) separately.

items.q: Can I choose between lump sum and annuity?
items.a: Standard Lotto (6/45) pays only as a lump sum. Pension Lottery 720+ offers a choice: 7 million KRW/month for 20 years, or a lump sum (approximately 50–60% of total annuity value).

items.q: Is the take-home amount final, or are there additional taxes later?
items.a: The tax on the winnings themselves is fully settled at the time of collection. Subsequent investments or purchases may generate their own taxes (capital gains, acquisition tax, etc.) separately.

q1.question: How much tax do I pay on a Lotto 1st prize?
q1.answer: Based on an average 1st prize of ~2B KRW: 22% on the first 300M (66M tax) and 33% on the remaining 1.7B (561M tax), totaling ~627M in taxes. Take-home is approximately 1.373B KRW.

q2.question: Do I need to file income tax separately?
q2.answer: No. Lottery winnings are subject to separate taxation, so the withholding tax at source completes your tax obligation. No additional filing is needed.

q3.question: Can I share my winnings with family?
q3.answer: Yes, but gift tax applies. Spouses can receive up to 600M KRW tax-free, adult children up to 50M KRW. Amounts beyond these thresholds are subject to 10-50% gift tax.

nationalPension.title: National Pension Calculator
nationalPension.description: Estimate your national pension benefits based on contribution period and average income (2025 standards)
nationalPension.minContribution: Minimum 10 years (120 months) of contribution required
nationalPension.earlyDesc: Up to 5 years early, 6% reduction per year
nationalPension.normalDesc: Standard pension at normal retirement age
nationalPension.deferredDesc: Up to 5 years deferred, 7.2% increase per year
nationalPension.cumulativeDesc: Compare cumulative benefits by age for all three options
nationalPension.aValue: A Value (Average Monthly Income of All Contributors)
nationalPension.aValueDesc: 3-year average monthly income of all NPS contributors (2024)
nationalPension.bValueDesc: Your average monthly income during contribution period
nationalPension.extraYearsDesc: 5% additional benefit per year beyond 20 years

formula.title: Calculation Formula
items: Monthly Basic = {Replacement Rate × (A + B) × (1 + 0.05n)} / 12
A: Average monthly income of all contributors (~2.86M KRW in 2024)
B: Your average monthly standard income during contribution
n: Years of contribution beyond 20 (0 if less than 20)
Replacement rate: 42% in 2025 (decreasing 0.5%p/year, fixed at 40% from 2028)

earlyVsDeferred.title: Early vs Deferred
items: Early: Up to 5 years early, 6%/year reduction (max 30%). Useful for those needing income sooner
Deferred: Up to 5 years late, 7.2%/year increase (max 36%). Better for longer life expectancy
Normal pension is the most balanced choice based on average life expectancy
Consider your health and other income sources when deciding
Early pension may be reduced if you have employment income

requirements.title: Eligibility Requirements
items: Minimum contribution: 10 years (120 months)
Pension start age: 60-65 depending on birth year
Early pension: 10+ years contribution + not working + from 5 years before start age
Survivor pension: Paid to survivors of contributors with 10+ years
Disability pension: Paid based on disability grade for illness/injury during contribution

whatIs.title: What is the National Pension Calculator?
whatIs.description: The National Pension Calculator estimates your expected monthly National Pension benefit based on years of contribution and average monthly income, using 2025 rules. Compare three scenarios—early collection (up to 5 years early, 6% reduction per year), standard collection (age 63), and deferred collection (up to 5 years delayed, 7.2% increase per year)—to find the optimal claiming strategy.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your total years of National Pension contributions.
2. Enter your average monthly income during the contribution period (based on actual premiums paid).
3. Select your intended collection timing (early, standard, or deferred).
4. View the estimated monthly benefit, annual total, and the break-even points for early vs. deferred collection.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The minimum contribution period is 10 years. Below that, you receive a lump-sum refund instead of a monthly pension. You can use voluntary continued enrollment to reach 10 years.

Deferred collection advantage: delaying by 1 year increases your benefit by 7.2%, and the maximum 5-year delay boosts it by 36%. If you expect good health and longevity, deferral can pay off significantly.

If a spouse dies and you receive survivor's pension, there are limits on collecting both your own old-age pension and the survivor's pension simultaneously. Compare both amounts to make the best choice.
The National Pension Service's 내연금 app or website provides more precise estimated benefits based on your actual contribution record.

items.q: If the pension fund runs out, will I stop receiving payments?
items.a: The fund is projected to be depleted around 2055, but payments would continue from the contributions of active workers at that time. Benefit levels or retirement age may be adjusted, but the system itself would not disappear.

items.q: Can voluntary enrollees (housewives, students, etc.) receive a pension?
items.a: Yes. Anyone aged 27–60 may enroll voluntarily. Premiums start from as low as 90,000 KRW/month, and 10+ years of contributions qualify you for a retirement pension.

items.q: Is pension income taxable?
items.a: Pension amounts corresponding to premiums paid after 2002 are subject to income tax as pension income. After the pension income deduction, the effective tax is minimal for annual pensions under approximately 9 million KRW.

items.q: Can voluntary enrollees (housewives, students, etc.) receive a pension?
items.a: Yes. Anyone aged 27–60 may enroll voluntarily. Premiums start from as low as 90,000 KRW/month, and 10+ years of contributions qualify you for a retirement pension.

items.a: Pension amounts corresponding to premiums paid after 2002 are subject to income tax as pension income. After the pension income deduction, the effective tax is minimal for annual pensions under approximately 9 million KRW.

q1.question: What is the minimum contribution period?
q1.answer: You need at least 10 years (120 months) of contributions to receive the old-age pension. If less than 10 years, you receive a lump-sum refund.

q2.question: Is early or deferred pension better?
q2.answer: If you live to average life expectancy (~83), total benefits are similar. Deferred is better if you live past 85, early is better if below average.

q3.question: Is inflation adjustment included?
q3.answer: Yes, the national pension adjusts annually based on CPI changes. This calculator shows amounts without inflation adjustment.

mealDiary.title: Meal/Calorie Diary
mealDiary.description: Track daily meals and monitor calorie & nutrient trends

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Select a date and choose meal type (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snack)
Search for food in the database or use 'Manual Entry' to add custom items
Adjust portions to auto-calculate calories and nutrients
Check the trend charts below for 7-day or 30-day intake patterns
Export to CSV to backup data or analyze in Excel

tips.title: Diet Tips
items: Recommended daily intake for adults: ~2,000 kcal (varies by activity level)
Ideal ratio: Carbs:Protein:Fat = 5:3:2
Tracking alone improves food awareness — keep recording consistently
Data is stored in your browser for 90 days

whatIs.title: What is the Meal / Calorie Diary?
whatIs.description: The Meal / Calorie Diary helps you record daily food intake and calorie counts, then visualizes your consumption trends by day, week, and month with charts. Features include a favorites list for frequently eaten foods, nutrient goal setting, and CSV export for detailed analysis. Data is stored locally in your browser—nothing is sent to external servers.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Select a date and enter the food name and calorie count.
2. Categorize entries by meal time: breakfast, lunch, dinner, or snack.
3. Add frequently eaten foods to favorites for quick entry.
4. Review the trend chart to track daily calorie intake and goal achievement rate.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Daily recommended calories: approximately 2,500 kcal for adult men and 2,000 kcal for adult women. A daily 500 kcal deficit translates to roughly 0.5 kg of weight loss per week.
For unknown food calorie counts, refer to the Ministry of Food and Drug Safety's food nutrition database (식품안전나라). For packaged foods, check the nutrition label.
Consistency is the key. Don't aim for perfection from day one—even if you miss a day or two, just pick up where you left off.
Export to CSV weekly and analyze in a spreadsheet to identify which days and foods are responsible for calorie overruns.

items.q: Can my records disappear unexpectedly?
items.a: Data is stored in browser localStorage. Clearing browser cache or using incognito mode will delete your data. Export to CSV regularly to back up important entries.

items.q: Can I track macronutrients (carbs, protein, fat) in addition to calories?
items.a: Yes. The tool supports nutrient logging. Enter carbohydrate, protein, and fat values for each food to view daily macronutrient balance in chart form.

items.q: Can I sync data across multiple devices?
items.a: The current version uses local browser storage, so cross-device sync is not supported. Export your data as CSV and import it on another device to transfer records.

salaryRank.title: My Salary Rank - Top %?
salaryRank.description: See where your salary ranks in Korea based on official data
salaryRank.enterSalary: Enter your salary to check your rank
salaryRank.dataSourceDesc: NTS Income Percentile Statistics (2023), Statistics Korea Economically Active Population Survey, MOEL Employment Survey
salaryRank.chartNote: Blue bar = Your position · Source: NTS 2023 Income Statistics
salaryRank.contributeDesc: Share your salary anonymously to help build more accurate community statistics
salaryRank.contributeError: An error occurred. Please try again later.

howToRead.title: How to Read Results
items: 'Top 10%' means you're in the top 10 out of 100 workers
Overall rank is based on all Korean wage earners (~20 million)
Age/gender/industry ranks show your position within that group
Median salary is the exact middle (50th percentile) value

dataExplain.title: About the Data
items: NTS Income Percentiles: Based on year-end tax filing gross salary distribution
Statistics Korea: Age and gender income distribution surveys
MOEL Employment Survey: Industry-specific wage data
Data is based on 2023 tax year (published 2024), updated annually

tips.title: Notes
items: Salary includes base pay + bonuses + allowances (excluding tax-free items)
Self-employed/freelance income uses separate statistics
Real purchasing power may differ from nominal salary due to inflation
Same salary feels different depending on region (Seoul vs. rural areas)

whatIs.title: What is 'My Salary Rank'?
whatIs.description: My Salary Rank shows where your annual salary stands among all Korean workers using official data from the National Tax Service (NTS) wage statistics and Statistics Korea salary survey. Filter by age group, gender, and industry to see your relative position within a specific peer group. Anonymous community-sourced real-world data is combined with official statistics for a more realistic comparison.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter your annual salary (pre-tax total compensation).
2. Optionally select your age group, gender, and industry for a more targeted comparison.
3. View your percentile rank among all workers and within your specific peer group.
4. Review the salary distribution chart to visualize your position.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: The 'salary' compared here is gross annual income including bonuses and performance pay—enter your total annual cash compensation.
The same salary can feel very different depending on region, occupation, and company size. Cost-of-living differences between the Seoul metro area and other regions also matter.
For salary negotiation: knowing your percentile rank versus the industry average for your age group gives you a concrete data point for negotiation.
Use the top 10% salary threshold as a long-term career target and work backward to set annual milestones.

items.q: What year is the data from?
items.a: The NTS wage statistics reflect the most recently published year (2023 income year). New data is typically released each October–November and the tool is updated accordingly.

items.q: Can freelancers or self-employed individuals use this for comparison?
items.a: This tool is based on employment income (salary). Business and freelance income is reported differently, making direct comparison difficult. You can use your gross annual earnings as an approximation for reference.

items.q: How much do you need to earn to be in the top 1%?
items.a: Based on the latest NTS statistics, the top 1% cutoff is approximately 200 million KRW per year (2023 income year). Top 10% is roughly 80 million KRW and top 20% is roughly 55 million KRW.

items.a: Based on the latest NTS statistics, the top 1% cutoff is approximately 200 million KRW per year (2023 income year). Top 10% is roughly 80 million KRW and top 20% is roughly 55 million KRW.

q1.question: What is the median salary in Korea?
q1.answer: As of 2023, the median gross salary of Korean workers is approximately 35M KRW. The average is about 42M KRW, but this is skewed upward by high earners, making the median more representative.

q2.question: How much do I need to earn to be in the top 1%?
q2.answer: The top 1% of wage earners in Korea earn approximately 200M KRW or more. Top 5% is about 110M KRW, and top 10% is about 85M KRW.

q3.question: How accurate is this data?
q3.answer: Based on official NTS tax filing statistics and Statistics Korea surveys. Interpolation between brackets is estimated. For exact percentiles, check the NTS TASIS portal.

whatIs.title: What is the Stock Calculator?
whatIs.description: A tool that accurately calculates brokerage fees, taxes, and realized profit/loss for stock trades. Enter buy and sell prices to see commissions, transaction tax, after-tax returns, and break-even price. Supports analysis for various investment styles.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Enter buy information: price per share, quantity, and commission rate.
2. Enter sell information: target selling price and quantity.
3. Click Calculate to see total amounts, commissions, transaction tax, and realized profit/loss.
4. The break-even price (BEP) is automatically calculated.

tips.title: Key Investment Tips
items: Fees and taxes have a bigger impact than expected. Frequent trading accumulates costs — always check your break-even.
Compare PER against industry average. PBR below 1x indicates undervaluation. ROE above 15% is favorable.
Diversify across 5–10 stocks; avoid investing more than 20% in a single stock.
Set stop-loss rules in advance: -5 to -10% from buy price to prevent large losses.
For volatile stocks, use dollar-cost averaging (DCA) to lower average buy price.
Emotional trading (FOMO, panic selling) is the most common failure. Stick to your plan.
Long-term investing (1+ year) incurs less in fees/taxes and benefits from compound growth.

items.q: Securities transaction tax vs capital gains tax?
items.a: Transaction tax is auto-levied at 0.20% of sell amount (KOSPI). Capital gains tax applies to major shareholders (₩1B+) or foreign stock sales. Most retail investors only pay transaction tax.

items.q: What are typical commission rates?
items.a: Online trades typically range from 0.015% to 0.025%. Some brokerages offer lifetime zero-commission promotions.

items.q: Fundamental vs technical analysis?
items.a: Fundamental analysis is essential for long-term value investing. Technical analysis is useful for short-term timing. Combining both is most effective.

items.q: ETFs or individual stocks for beginners?
items.a: ETFs offer diversified exposure without individual analysis and lower costs. Consider individual stocks after gaining experience.

whatIs.title: What is the Exchange Rate Calculator?
whatIs.description: A real-time currency converter supporting 12 major world currencies including USD, EUR, JPY, GBP, and CNY. Use it for travel budgets, international remittances, or trade payments.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Click 'Refresh Rates' to load the latest exchange rate data.
2. Enter the amount and select source and target currencies.
3. The converted amount is displayed immediately with reverse conversion.
4. Save results or share via URL for later reference.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Airport exchange charges 3–5% — the most expensive. Apply for online bank exchange (0.5–1.5%) before your trip.
Japanese yen has large nominal values. Pre-convert JPY prices to your currency to gauge actual costs.
Combine a small amount of cash with a no-foreign-fee credit card for cost-effective overseas spending.
Rate volatility spikes after central bank decisions and economic data releases — time conversions carefully.
Keep exchange receipts on business trips for tax reporting and expense reimbursement.

items.q: How often are rates updated?
items.a: Rates are fetched from an external API each time you click Refresh. On weekends and holidays, the previous business day's closing rate is used.

items.q: Where is the cheapest exchange?
items.a: Online bank exchange (mobile apps) at 0.5–1.5% is cheapest. Bank branches charge 1.5–3%, airports 3–5%.

items.q: What factors influence exchange rates?
items.a: Interest rates, GDP growth, inflation, trade balances, and political instability. US Fed rate decisions particularly impact most currencies.

items.q: Are HKD and CNY the same currency?
items.a: No. HKD is Hong Kong's currency, CNY is mainland China's. They are traded separately with different exchange rates.

bonusCalculator.title: Bonus Calculator
bonusCalculator.description: Calculate the after-tax take-home amount for performance bonuses and incentives

simulation.title: Bonus Ratio Simulation
simulation.description: Compare take-home amounts at different bonus ratios

taxAnalysis.title: Tax Structure Analysis
taxAnalysis.description: Adding a bonus increases total income, which may push you into a higher tax bracket

whatIs.title: What is a Performance Bonus?
whatIs.description: A performance bonus is additional compensation paid beyond base salary, determined by company profitability or individual performance. In Korean companies, bonuses come in various forms including PS (Profit Sharing), PI (Productivity Incentive), management performance bonuses, and individual incentives. PS distributes a portion of company profits to employees, primarily at large corporations like Samsung and Hyundai, with amounts ranging from 0% to several hundred percent of annual salary. PI is awarded based on department or team-level target achievement. Under Korean tax law, bonuses are classified as earned income, subject to progressive income tax (6-45%), local income tax (10% of income tax), and four types of social insurance (National Pension, Health Insurance, Long-term Care Insurance, and Employment Insurance). When a bonus is added, total annual income increases, potentially pushing the taxpayer into a higher tax bracket with a higher marginal rate, resulting in a greater perceived tax burden. This calculator applies the latest 2025 tax rates and social insurance contribution rates to automatically compute deductions and take-home amounts from your pre-tax salary and bonus inputs. The ratio simulation feature lets you compare scenarios from 50% to 500% bonus ratios at a glance, while the tax structure analysis visually shows how tax brackets and additional tax burdens change with and without the bonus.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Enter your pre-tax annual salary and bonus ratio (or amount)
Enter the number of dependents accurately — it affects income tax calculations
Use the simulation tab to compare different ratios at a glance
Check the tax analysis tab for bracket changes and marginal tax rates

tips.title: Tax-Saving Tips for Bonuses
items: Contribute your bonus to an IRP (Individual Retirement Pension) for up to 9M KRW tax credit
Combined with pension savings, up to 9M KRW annual tax credit (16.5% if salary ≤55M KRW)
Spreading bonus payments across months can lower your marginal tax rate
Maximize non-taxable benefits like meal allowances (200K KRW/month)
Don't miss additional deductions at year-end (medical, education, donations, etc.)

items.q: Are social insurance contributions applied to bonuses?
items.a: Yes, bonuses are considered earned income, so national pension, health insurance, and employment insurance apply. However, national pension has a monthly cap (5.9M KRW), limiting the additional burden for high earners.

items.q: Why does the tax rate on bonuses seem higher than regular salary?
items.a: The bonus itself isn't taxed at a higher rate. Rather, adding the bonus increases your total annual income, potentially pushing you into a higher progressive tax bracket (6-45% in Korea).

items.q: How much can I save by putting my bonus into a retirement pension?
items.a: You can contribute up to 9M KRW annually to an IRP, with a 16.5% tax credit (salary ≤55M KRW) or 13.2% (>55M KRW). A full 9M KRW contribution saves up to 1.485M KRW in taxes.

items.q: Why do withholding taxes differ from actual taxes?
items.a: Withholding uses simplified tax tables for monthly deductions. Year-end settlement reconciles with actual income and deductions. Even if a large amount is withheld from a bonus month, you may get a refund.

items.q: What's the difference between a bonus and performance pay?
items.a: A regular bonus splits the annual salary into extra payments on specific months (total unchanged). Performance pay is additional income beyond the annual salary. Tax calculation methods are the same.

items.q: Does splitting bonus payments across months reduce taxes?
items.a: Splitting bonus payments across months may lower the withholding rate per month, but since total annual income remains the same, the final tax amount at year-end settlement is identical. However, it can help with cash flow management.

items.q: Are bonuses received after resignation still taxed?
items.a: Yes, bonuses paid after resignation are still classified as earned income and subject to income tax and social insurance contributions. They are processed as earned income (not severance), with taxes withheld before payment.

whatIs.title: What is the Enneagram?
whatIs.description: The Enneagram is a personality typing system categorizing human personality into 9 distinct types, each with a unique worldview, core motivation, and fundamental fear. It's a rich psychological tool used for self-understanding, improving relationships, and guiding personal growth.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: 1. Answer all 36 questions honestly, based on actual day-to-day behavior.
2. For each question, select how strongly it applies to you.
3. Once complete, scores for all 9 types are calculated and your primary type is identified.
4. Explore your type's core motivation, fear, wings, and growth direction on the results page.

tips.title: Pro Tips
items: Your core type is stable, but stress and growth situations can shift behavioral patterns.
Considering wing (adjacent type) influence provides a more nuanced self-portrait.
Every type has healthy and unhealthy expressions — consciously work toward the healthy direction.
Understanding others' Enneagram types can dramatically reduce conflict and improve communication.
This is a self-exploration tool and does not replace professional psychological counseling.

items.q: Can your Enneagram type change?
items.a: The core type is generally fixed for life. Under stress or growth, you may exhibit traits of adjacent types or move along integration/disintegration arrows.

items.q: Similar scores across multiple types?
items.a: This often indicates strong wing influence. Read both descriptions to find which resonates more deeply.

items.q: How does it differ from MBTI?
items.a: MBTI focuses on cognitive functions and preferences. The Enneagram centers on core motivations, fears, and desires — exploring the deeper 'why' behind behavior.

items.q: Surprised by my result?
items.a: Read full descriptions of the top types. The one whose core fear and desire resonate most is usually the most accurate.

projection.title: Axis Projection
projection.dragToStart: Drag shapes or press play to start

satVisualizer.title: SAT Collision Detection Visualizer
satVisualizer.description: Separating Axis Theorem — Detect collision between two convex polygons using separating axes

whatIs.title: What is SAT?
whatIs.content: If two convex shapes are not colliding, there must exist an axis (Separating Axis) that separates them. Conversely, if projections overlap on all candidate axes, the shapes are colliding. This is the core of the Separating Axis Theorem.

math.title: Mathematical Principle
math.content: For each edge of both polygons, compute the perpendicular normal. These normals are the separating axis candidates. Project all vertices onto each axis (using dot product) to get 1D intervals, then check if the intervals overlap. If any axis has no overlap, there is no collision.

convex.title: Why Only Convex Polygons?
convex.content: Concave polygons cannot be fully tested with edge normals alone. To apply SAT to concave shapes, you must first perform Convex Decomposition.

optimization.title: Optimization Techniques
optimization.content: Early Exit: Return 'no collision' as soon as one separating axis is found. This makes most non-collision cases very efficient. The MTV (Minimum Translation Vector) is the axis direction with the smallest overlap, used for collision resolution.

stepsGuide.description: Click each axis to see its projection on the canvas.

threeView.explanation: Project two 3D boxes onto X, Y, and Z axes. If shadows overlap on ALL axes, it's a collision. If there's a GAP on even one axis, the shapes are separated. The 4 viewports show projections from each axis direction.

whatIs.title: What is SAT Collision Detection?
whatIs.description: SAT (Separating Axis Theorem) is a key algorithm used in game physics engines, robotics, and CAD to quickly and accurately detect collisions between convex shapes.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Drag the blue (A) or amber (B) polygon on the canvas to move it
Press the play button to run the step-by-step axis checking animation
Check GAP/OVERLAP for each axis in the projection panel
Adjust vertices, size, and rotation in the parameter panel to experiment
View and copy the TypeScript implementation in the Code tab

tips.title: Learning Tips
items: Start by placing shapes far apart and observe the first axis projection
Slowly move shapes closer and watch GAP turn into OVERLAP
A triangle (3) and square (4) check 7 axes total (3+4=7)
Two pentagons check 10 axes — more vertices means higher cost

items.q: Can SAT work with circles?
items.a: Yes, circles theoretically need infinite axes, but in practice you only need to add one axis toward the nearest vertex. Commonly used for polygon-circle collision.

items.q: Does SAT work in 3D?
items.a: Yes, in 3D the axis candidates are face normals plus cross products of all edge pairs. Used in OBB (Oriented Bounding Box) collision detection.

items.q: What is SAT's time complexity?
items.a: O(n × m) — where n is the vertex count of polygon A and m is polygon B's. Early Exit makes non-collision cases much faster.

aabbVisualizer.title: AABB Collision Detection Visualizer
aabbVisualizer.description: Axis-Aligned Bounding Box — Detect collision between two rectangles aligned to axes

stepsGuide.description: Check X and Y axis projections to determine collision.
stepsGuide.colliding: Collision! Both X and Y axes overlap

whatIs.title: What is AABB Collision Detection?
whatIs.description: AABB (Axis-Aligned Bounding Box) is the most basic and fastest algorithm for detecting collisions between two axis-aligned rectangles. Widely used in game physics, UI hit testing, and broad phase collision detection.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Drag the blue (A) or amber (B) box to move it
Watch the X and Y axis projection bars update in real time
Adjust box sizes with sliders to experiment with different cases
View and copy the implementation code in the Code tab

tips.title: Learning Tips
items: AABB doesn't consider rotation — boxes are always axis-aligned
If either X or Y axis is separated, there's no collision (Early Exit)
AABB is a special case of SAT: only 2 axes (X and Y) to check
Games often use AABB for quick filtering before precise detection (SAT, etc.)

items.q: What's the difference between AABB and SAT?
items.a: AABB only handles axis-aligned rectangles and checks 2 axes. SAT handles any convex polygon and checks all edge normals. AABB is much faster but inaccurate for rotated shapes.

items.q: Why use AABB in Broad Phase?
items.a: With hundreds of objects, applying SAT to every pair is slow. AABB quickly filters out obviously separated pairs, greatly reducing the number of precise checks needed.

items.q: How does 3D AABB work?
items.a: Same principle. Compare ranges on X, Y, and Z axes — all three must overlap for a collision.

bfsDfsVisualizer.title: BFS / DFS Search Visualizer
bfsDfsVisualizer.description: Compare Breadth-First Search and Depth-First Search on a grid

stepsGuide.description: Observe which nodes are explored at each step.
stepsGuide.bfsDescription: BFS uses a queue (FIFO) to explore nodes level by level from the start.
stepsGuide.dfsDescription: DFS uses a stack (LIFO) to go deep in one direction before backtracking.

whatIs.title: What are BFS and DFS?
whatIs.description: BFS (Breadth-First Search) and DFS (Depth-First Search) are the two most fundamental graph/tree traversal algorithms. BFS explores nodes closest to the start first, while DFS goes as deep as possible before backtracking.

howToUse.title: How to Use
items: Click/drag on the grid to draw or erase walls
Set the start point (green) and goal point (red)
Choose BFS, DFS, or side-by-side comparison mode
Press play to watch the search process step by step
Use the random maze button to experiment with different scenarios

tips.title: Learning Tips
items: BFS guarantees the shortest path (in unweighted graphs)
DFS uses less memory but doesn't guarantee the shortest path
Observe the different search patterns in mazes with narrow corridors
Compare visited node counts and path lengths in side-by-side mode

items.q: Is BFS always better than DFS?
items.a: No. BFS is ideal when shortest path is needed, while DFS is better for exhaustive search or topological sorting. DFS also uses less memory.

items.q: What's the time complexity?
items.a: Both are O(V + E) — V is the number of nodes, E is the number of edges. On a grid, that's O(rows × columns).

items.q: How does this relate to A*?
items.a: A* extends BFS by using a heuristic (estimated distance to goal) to guide the search direction. It finds the shortest path much more efficiently than BFS.
